R 尚且只是个学生，并且签订的是暂时性(类似于兼职)的雇佣合同，所以其实对于汇 兑损益地错误处理，并不是最终责任人。相反， M 明显知道这样处理是错误地，但因为 这样的汇兑收益可以使得利润表更好看，使得公司业绩达标，加上M 的薪酬有部分是 与 profit 直接挂钩的，这些都会促使M 选择忽视这一错误。所以为了保住自己的地位， M 不但没有指出更正，还在财报公布之后对于R 进行了纪律处分，这是明显缺乏诚信以 及专业价值观的做法(这里其实点出纪律处分存在恐吓威胁，会迫使R 为了保住工作进 一步地发生不道德行为，也是合理的)。 Ms Malgun should not have left the preparation of the year-end financial statements to Mr Raavi as he is a student accountant and has only been with the company for three months.She has significant experience and expertise in their preparation.Work pressure can influence ethical behaviour. Difficult performance goals and time pressure make unethical behaviour more likely.When employees are under pressure,this not only affects their wellbeing and motivation,but also their behaviour.Ms Malgun is an expert in IFRS standards and should have ensured that she allocated some time to assist Mr Raavi in the preparation of the year-end financial statements.It is the responsibility of both Ms Malgun and Mr Raavi to engage in fair and accurate reporting with regard to the truthfulness of the data they provide as well as its completeness. It is ethically important for accountants to present the financial information in a way which is clear and honest. 此外，由于R 尚且还是个学生，工作经验不丰富，仅在公司工作了3个月， M 理论上是 不应该把年报准备工作交给 R的，因为比较起来明显 M 更具有经验也更专业。但是业 绩指标、时间压力都使得 M 做出不道德行为。M 是 IFRS方面的专家，她应该确保有足 够的时间协助 R 一起编制财报，并且两个人应该对于财报的真实性和完整性负责，给出 公平准确的报告。对于财务人员来说，清晰、公平、诚实是非常必要的道德原则。 Competition can influence unethical behaviour.Individuals are more inclined to engage in unethical behaviour when their organisation is in competition with other organisations or they have been given targets which have to be met.When unethical behaviour leads to a gain for a company,managers choose less severe disciplinary measures for their employees.Thus,although Ms Malgun knew of the error in the financial statements,she only reprimanded Mr Raavi after the financial statements had been published and even then,she then offered him a full-time contract instead of his current temporary contract. 这里进一步在 apply to case,点出当不道德的行为能够使得公司收益的时候，大部分管 理者都不太会对于员工采取很严厉地措施，所以尽管 M 知道财报是有错误的，在给R 纪律处分之后，竟然继续提供了一份全职的合同给到R, 这其实是不合理的。 Notes: 本题答案并没有像平时授课中给大家讲授过的道德题的答题模板来进行，相反 更像是结合案例，对于M 和 R 的所作所为进行一些评判，指出不合理的地方，这也是 近期道德题的一个趋势，答案会更趋向开放式的回答，凡是结合案例言之有理，都是可 以得分的。 2.Sale of licence 题干解析： A 公司在x7.1.1授予(卖给)K 一个可以无限期在南美出售头疼产品Headon 的许可证， 同时 A 保留了在世界其他地方售卖Headon 的权利。南美市场份额达到20%。用于生产 Headon 的生产流程并不是专有的，其他几家公司也都可以为K 代工生产。K 如果直接 从 A 公司买这批药物的话，需要按照在成本之上加上50%利润的价格购买。 同 日 ，K 预付了15m 的款项，并当在南美销量超过35m 的时候，再额外支付3m 给 A 公司。A 公司也正确地将研发Headon 产生的开发成本进行了资本化处理，目前无形资 产的账面价值为30m。(A 卖许可证，同时可以帮助 K 生 产Headon 这款头痛药。 K 有 许可证后，可以向A 购买药物再卖出，也可以向其他厂家购买药物。) (b)Discuss how the granting(sale)of the licence to Kokila Co should be accounted for by Agency Co on 1January 20X7. (5marks) Requirement 解析： 讨论一下对于A 公司授予也就是卖给 K 公司许可证相关的事项，该如何做会计处理。(应 根据IFRS15 收入准则以及IAS 38 无形资产准则，结合案例进行合理分析。) 答案解析： Agency Co had correctly capitalised development costs for Headon at a carrying amount of $30 million.IAS 38 Intangible Assets states that an intangible asset,in this case a proportion of the development costs,may be derecognised on disposal or when no future economic benefits are expected from its use or disposal.【终止确认定义】 The gain or loss arising on derecognition is the difference between the net proceeds and the carrying amount of the asset.Gains are not classified as revenue.The amount of gain or loss arising from the derecognition will be affected by the determination of the transaction price with reference to IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers. 讨论了售卖无形资产(许可证)以及终止确认的问题。当无形资产一部分是由于研发费 用资本化形成的时候，在处置掉或者预期无法再通过处置或者售卖获得未来经济利益流 入的时候，进行终止确认。处置利得作为gain or loss计入利润表。但是这里答案有强 调，处置利得与主营业务收入是不同的。 In assessing whether an entity's promises to transfer goods or services to the customer are separately identifiable【distinct】,the objective of IFRS 15 is to determine whether the nature of the promise is to transfer each of those goods or services individually or,instead,to transfer a combined item. 谈到收入确认，主要是看转让的商品或者服务是否是distinct的，能否被单独买卖，需 要识别出合同当中存在几个履约义务。 Kokila Co can benefit from the licence without Agency Co's manufacturing service because there are other entities which can provide the manufacturing service.Therefore, Agency Co's promises to grant the licence and to provide the manufacturing service are separately identifiable. 结合案例，K 公司即使没有向 A 购买但也可以从其他公司获得这部分的生产服务，因此 A 公司提供许可证以及提供制造服务是可以单独区分的。(这里其实就是根据五步法里 的第二步确认合同当中包含的不同的履约义务) The consideration for the licence comprises the up-front payment of $15 million and a variable consideration of $3 million.Initially,only the up-front payment will be recognised as proceeds together with the gain on the disposal of the South American development costs.The variable consideration will be recognised in SOPL when it occurs, i.e.when South American sales exceed $35 million.The performance obligation needs to be satisfied before the payment is recognised. 许可证的销售对价包含15m 的预付款和3m 的可变对价。那么在合同签订初期，根据 15m 的售价加上许可证的账面价值，可以求得许可证的处置利得。而3m 的可变对价， 需要在销售额超过35m 的时候被确认。 Judgement is required to determine the portion of the carrying amount of the intangible asset to derecognise,relative to the amount retained.Therefore,a gain is recognised on disposal of the South American development costs of $9 million($15m-($30m×20%)). 需要判断一下终止确认的无形资产的账面价值有多少，这时通过题目给到的条件南美市 场的市场价值占比20%合理推断出资产的账面价值约为30*20%=6,所以这张许可证卖 了 1 5m, 但是账面价值为6m, 因此有一个9m 的处置利得。 3.Drug development 题干解析： A公司研发一款药物，并且与其他已经获得监管许可的产品相类似。现有已经批准产品 X 的专利权快要到期了，A 公司也已经向政府申请了新产品的监管许可，并且在申请中 对于新产品与已开发产品进行比较分析，为了进一步证明产品的生物相似性。政府已经 审核了A 公司申请并且允许A 公司进行初步的医学试验。试验进展顺利，由于药品是 用于治疗一种非常特殊情况的症状，所以只会跟一小部分人相关。使用该产品的人如果 被证明有效的话，则只需要支付一小部分名义金额就可以了。 (c) Discuss the accounting treatment of the costs incurred to date in developing the biosimilar drug.(4marks) Professional marks will be awarded in part(a)(i)for the quality of the discussion.(2 marks) Requirement 解析： 题目要求讨论研发药品的成本费用相关会计处理的问题，其实主要是围绕海盗条件来讨 论一下费用应该资本化还是费用化的问题。 答案解析： Development costs are capitalised as an intangible asset if certain criteria in IAS 38 are met. 满足确认条件的情况下可以资本化，也就是满足海盗条件的时候。 There is no definitive starting point for the capitalisation of internal development costs and,therefore,Agency Co must use its judgement,based on the facts and circumstances of each product.A strong indication that Agency Co has met allof the IAS 38 criteria arises when regulatory approval is issued for the biosimilar drug as it proves the technical feasibility of the asset.This is often the most difficult criterion to demonstrate. 结合案例，当药品获得监管许可的时候，其实可以表明资本化条件的其中之一：技术上 的可行性是可以满足的。 Another criterion to be met is that the asset should generate probable future economic benefits and demonstrate the existence of a market,or the usefulness of the asset if it is to be used internally.At present,this criterion has not been met as the product is aimed at a small group of people who willonly pay a notional amount if it is an effective product. 除此之外，海盗条件里的很有可能带来未来经济利益的流入，还需要有足够的能力去使 用或者售卖这个产品。但结合案例，这部分的条件是没有被满足的，因为本身产品只针 对小部分特殊人群，同时即使被证明有效，也只是象征性地付一笔名义上的金额，无法 证明能够很有可能带来经济利益流入。 In addition,regulatory approval has only been applied for,and there is a concern over the limited market and revenue stream.Thus,the costs are unlikely to meet the capitalisation criteria and all costs to date will be written off to profit or loss. 而且目前只是在申请监管批准环节，加上市场需求量有限，以及收入金额较为微小，因 此是不满足海盗条件的，所以不能被资本化，而是要直接作为费用计入利润表。

stem_zh: Question 3 (a)Option 1 Leased for a four-year period At 1 January 20X7,a right-of-use asset and lease liability of $50,803 would be recognised according to IFRS 16 Leases.The annual lease component of the lease payments is $14,016(12×$1,403-$235)and the service component is $2,820(12× $235).At 31 December 20X7,operating expenses willcomprise the service component of $2,820,and depreciation of $12,701($50,803/4).An interest expense of $2,274 will be recognised as a finance cost.The lease liability recognised will be $50,803 less the annual payments of $14,016 plus the interest element of $2,274,i.e.$39,061.The closing lease liability will be split between its non-current and current liability in the statement of financial position.IFRS 16 requires a company to recognise interest on lease liabilities separately from depreciation of lease assets. 第一种获得汽车的方式是租赁： 合同中约定了如下内容：S 公司每月需支付出租方$1,403元，年实际支付金额即为 $16,836元($1,403*12月),租期为四年，其中每月支付的款项包含租金$1,168 ($1,403-$235)和服务费$235(题目已知)。所以该合同由租赁合同和服务合同两部 分构成，这两者需区分开来进行账务处理的。其中每年支付的租金为$14,016($1,168*12 月),这部分需要按照 IFRS16号准则来进行账务处理；每年支付的服务费为$2,820 ($235x12月),这部分则直接作为费用计入损益表。分录如下： Dr Service cost $2,820 Cr Cash $2,820 根据IFRS16 号准则，作为承租方初始计量即20×7年1月1日时应当同时确认使用权资 产和租赁负债，金额为$50,803(题目已知)。分录如下： Dr Right of use asset $50,803 Cr Lease liability $50,803 后续计量即20×7年12月31日时企业需针对使用权资产计提折旧费用$12,701 ($50,803/4yrs), 使 用 权 资 产 在 2 0 × 7 年 1 2 月 3 1 日 的 账 面 价 值 为 $ 3 8 , 1 0 2 ($50,803-$12,701);对租赁负债进行计息$2,274(题目已知)和付租金$14,016,租 赁负债在20×7年12月31日的账面价值为$39,061。根据IFRS16 号准则的规定，租赁 负债的期末数一部分需要列示在流动负债中，一部分金额需要列示在长期负债中。分录 如 下 ： Drinterest cost $2,274 Cr Lease liability$2,274 Dr Lease liability$14,016 Cr Cash $14,016 Option 2 Purchased on 1 January 20X7 If the cars were purchased on 1 January 20X7,then depreciation would be charged of $11,380($75,274-$29,753=$45,521/4 =$11,380)and interest of $3,763($75,274× 5%)would also be charged.The cars would have to be serviced at a cost of $2,820. 第二种获得汽车的方式是全额贷款购买： 根据题意得知该汽车在20×7年1月1日的公允价值为$75,274元。贷款购买的话分录如 下 ： Dr PPE $75,274 Cr loan liability $75,274 在20x7 年12月31日，企业需要对固定资产计提折旧费用$11,380(($75,274原值- $29,753残值)/4yrs 使用年限),计提折旧之后该固定资产期末账面价值为$63,894。分 录如下 ： Dr depreciation expense $11,380 Cr PPE $11,380 企业需要对贷款计息，金额为$3,763($75,274×5%),且根据题意得知企业今年没有还款， 贷款的期末数为$79,039($75,274+$3,763)。分录如下： Dr Interest cost $3,763 Cr Loan liability $3,763 企业今年支付年服务费为$2,820($235x12 月),分录如下： Dr Service cost $2,820 Cr Cash $2,820 Option 3 Leased on a 12-month agreement Instead of applying the recognition requirements of IFRS 16,a lessee may elect to account for lease payments as an expense on a straight-line basis over the lease term for the following two types of leases: (i)leases with a lease term of 12 months or less and containing no purchase options;and ii)leases where the underlying asset has a low value. The effect of applying the IFRS 16 exemption would be that neither an asset nor a liability will be recognised and therefore it will not affect the statement of financial position.Neither a right-of-use asset nor lease liability will be recognised if this exemption is applied.Instead,an expense will be recognised in the statement of profit or loss. The cost of the short-term lease would be included in operating expenses at $22,800(12 x$1,900). 第三种获得汽车的方式是通过短期租赁： 对于租期不超过12个月的租赁，我们可以直接将当期的租金作为费用计入损益表。即 出现operating expenses $22,800(12×$1,900)。这种情况下企业无需确认使用权资产和 租赁负债。 It can be seen that the impact on EBITDA is greatest if 12-month leases are chosen.This is because the cost is shown in operating expenses.Additionally,profit before tax is lower under this option.EBITDA does not include lease interest when IFRS 16 is used and thus is naturally higher. There will be no effect on EBITDA if Stem Co leases or buys the cars and,further,the impact on profit before tax is minimal with profit being lower if Stem Co purchases the cars. 通过上述对三种方式的分析之后，可以得出答案最后的计算表格。通过查看该表格，很 显然，前面两种方式下，对EBITDA的影响是一样的，而第三种方式下， EBITDA的金额 最小。 而对于PBT的影响，第一种方式下税前利润金额>第二种方式>第三种方式。 If 12-month leases are chosen,then there will be no recognition of an asset for the cars which will result in a higher asset base for the four-year lease/purchase of cars,which will affect ratios such as asset turnover.Similarly,a liability will not be recognised in the case of the 12-month lease which will mean higher financial liabilities for the four-year lease/purchase,which willaffect financial leverage(gearing). The carrying amount of the leased cars will typically reduce more quickly than the carrying amount of lease liabilities.This is because,in each period of the lease,the leased car is depreciated on a straight-line basis,and the lease liability is reduced by the amount of lease payments made and increased by the interest which reduces over the life of the lease.Consequently,although the amounts of the lease asset and lease liability are the same at the start and end of the lease,the amount of the asset would typically be lower than that of the liability throughout the lease term.This will result in a further reduction in reported equity as compared to 12-month leases.This will be similar to the effect on reported equity which arises from financing the purchase of the cars through a loan. 对于资产负债表的影响的分析，第三种方式不会在报表上确认资产和负债。而租赁或者 购买都是会确认资产和负债的。但是影响不一样，所以对于一些财务指标比方说 gearing 的影响是不同的。 Profit before accounting for cars Service cost Operating expense EBITDA Depreciation Interest Profit before tax PPE Lease/loan liability Lease over four years Purchase with loan ($) ($) 100,000 100,000 (2.820) 97,180 (12,701) (2.274) 82,205 82,205 38,102 (50,803-12,701) 39,061 (2,820) 97,180 (11,380) (3.763) 82.037 63,894 (75,274-11,380) 79,039 (75,274+3,763) 12-month leases ($) 100,000 (22800 ) 77,200 77,200 0 (b)(i) The equity method is a measurement method and not a consolidation method,as the equity-accounted entity remains as a single line in the investor's statement of financial position and IFRS standards consolidation is based on the existence of control. Equity accounting is a measurement method for investments where there is 'significant influence'and recognises an associate's profits which have not been received and could not be successfully demanded.The equity method provides better information than that provided by cost,but it can be argued that where investments are listed,then there is no reason not to use fair value.The equity method is likely to be better than cost because cost is in isolation an uninformative basis for decision-making.However,if an investment is listed,then its fair value would be easier to establish and more intuitively appealing than the numbers derived from the equity method.If the associate is unlisted,then there might be questions about the verifiability of fair value.However,even then,there appears to be no reason why the equity method should be preferred to IFRS 13 Fair Value Measurement. 题干分析： b(i) 问要求考生回答两个问题：1)权益法是什么,2)对联营公司的投资的账务处理 方式中权益法是不是比成本法或者公允价值更相关。 答案
explanation_zh: 权益法下初始计量是按照成本核算，后续计量是只要联营公司的净资产产生变动，那么 变动的金额中归属于母公司的比例要列示进报表。权益法与合并要区分开来，权益法下 联营公司只会以一个科目列示在投资者的报表中，即 investment in associate,并不会像 子公司一样，需要将母子公司的所有的资产负债一行一行加总列入合并报表。 权益法和成本法比较起来的话：如果是选择了成本法，那么当初投资联营公司的成本会 列示进报表，后续除非联营公司跟母公司发放股息才需要进行账务处理，否则不会有任 何的后续计量。所以在成本法下，后续联营公司产生的损益并不会在报表中反映出来。 但是在权益法下联营公司净资产的变动中归属于母公司的比例会反映进报表，因此，和 成本法比较起来，权益法会更相关。 权益法和公允价值比较起来需要从两个角度分析：如果联营公司是非上市公司，股价不 能够直接得到，那么这种情况下对联营公司的投资的公允价值不能够直接可靠评估出来； 但是如果联营公司是上市公司，股价能够可靠计量，那么期末用公允价值衡量对联营公 司的投资会更相关。 (ii)The following are the characteristics of a joint venture: -Joint ventures are joint arrangements which are usually structured through a separate vehicle which confers legal separation between the joint venture and the assets and liabilities in the vehicle. -The entity must be under the joint control of the venturers,which is the contractually agreed sharing of control of an arrangement,which exists only when decisions about the relevant activities require the unanimous consent of the parties sharing control. -The venturers must be able to exercise joint control of the entity. -The purpose of the entity must be consistent with the definition of a joint venture. Emphasis Co is a joint venture.Its activities are conducted through a separate legal entity and the parties participating in the decision making exercise control through their equity investments.This control is determined by the ability to appoint board members. This means that the significant decisions require the unanimous consent of all of the parties. The company holding 20%of the equity can only appoint one board member but does have the ability to prevent the remaining companies from making significant decisions without its consent. Each party to the joint venture (or each ‘joint venturer')recognises an investment,which is accounted for using the equity method in accordance with IAS 28 Investments in Associates and Joint Ventures. According to IAS 28,where an investor's investment is less than their share of the fair value of the identifiable net assets acquired,this results in a gain to the investor and is referred to as a bargain purchase.IAS 28 states that on the acquisition of the investment in an entity,any difference(whether positive or negative)between the cost of acquisition and the investor's share of the fair values of the net identifiable assets of the entity is accounted for like goodwill in accordance with IFRS 3 Business Combinations.Thus,any excess fair value of the identifiable net assets over the cost of the equity method investment would be recognised as a bargain purchase gain in earnings on the investment date,which is consistent with the accounting for bargain purchases in business combinations. However,bargain purchases are rare.Therefore,before recognising a gain on a bargain purchase,Stem Co should reassess whether it has correctly identified allof the assets acquired and all of the liabilities assumed as part of the investment,in order to ensure that all identifiable assets or liabilities are properly recognised.In addition,Stem Co should reconsider and challenge all valuations to verify that the identifiable net assets are properly measured.Stem Co should try to understand why the other parties would contribute assets of higher value than those contributed by Stem Co.Usually,investors act in an economically rational manner.There may be strategic reasons for such actions. For example,Stem Co may have specialised knowledge of the industry.Also,the fair value of the net identifiable assets of Emphasis Co may have increased before the finalisation of the agreement. Stem Co contributed cash of $150,000 to Emphasis Co.The carrying amount of the net assets contributed by the investors was $310,000 but the fair value of the net assets contributed was $470,000.Therefore,Stem Co's share of the fair value of the identifiable assets of Emphasis Co is 40%of $470,000,i.e.$188,000.This exceeds the contribution of $150,000.Once Stem Co has reassessed whether it has correctly identified all of the assets acquired and all of the liabilities assumed as part of its investment in Emphasis Co, Stem Co will record the investment at $188,000 and will record a gain of $38,000 ($188,000-$150,000). Dr Investment in Emphasis Co$188,000 Cr Cash $150,000 Cr Profit or loss $38,000 题干分析： b(ii) 问要求考生回答两个问题：1)为什么对E 公司的投资属于对合营企业的投资，2) 根据IAS28 号准则具体如何进行账务处理。题目要求写相关的会计分录。 答案解析： 既然是属于JV, 那么首先可以回答JV 的一些特征：1)JV 是属于合营安排的一种，合 营企业一般都是单独主体。2)参与方能够对JV 实施共同控制，共同控制要求分享控制 权的参与方能够达到一致同意。核心词是 unanimous consent。 根据题意得知， E 公司属于一个单独主体 (new legal entity),所有重大的决策要求全 体董事会的一致同意 (unanimous consent),而董事会成员的任命是按照持股比例来 的，持有40%的股份可以任命三个董事，持有20%的股份可以任命一个董事。虽然有一 个投资者支持有20%的股份，且只可以任命一个董事，但是所有重大的决策要求全体董 事会的一致同意，所以该股东其实也是拥有一票否决权的。各投资方设立E 公司的目的 是通过技术的开发去对 E 公司经营的结果共担风险共享收益，其实相当于是对E 公司的 净资产享有权利。所以判断出来E 公司属于合营企业。 长期股权投资的初始投资成本小于投资时应享有被投资单位可辨认净资产公允价值份 额的，应按其差额调整初始投资成本，最为一项gain 计入损益。这种情况一般我们认 为是 bargain purchase。就类似于母子公司当中存在的goodwill 。 但是，在实务中出现 bargain purchase的情况是很罕见的，所以在确认相应的gain 时我们需要重新评估，看 是不是被投资单位可辨认净资产公允价值评估的不准确。而且一般如果出现这种情况， 说明别的投资者的初始投资成本大于投资时应享有被投资单位可辨认净资产公允价值 份额，但是不同的投资者不同的企业都是属于理性的经济人， 一般不会出现这种情况， 所以我们还需要去考虑一下是不是S 其实存在一些隐形的优势或资源，比方说 S 公司对 该科技研发行业有充分的历史经验等等。 E 公司的可辨认净资产公允价值为47万，其中归属于S 公司的比例为47万×40%=18.8 万。与S 公司实际投入的金额15万比较，存在bargain purchase 3.8万。该差额需要调 整初始投资的成本，所以分录借方为investment in associate 18.8 万(15万+3 . 8万), 同时该差额也要作为一项gain 计入损益表。 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION

stem_zh: Question 4 (a)There is significant interest in crypto assets with implications for both new and traditional investors.There is a growing need for clarity regarding the accounting and related disclosures relating to these new investments. The general disclosure principles which should be used to help investors can include that the disclosures should be entity-specific as information tailored to an entity's own circumstances is more useful than generic information which is readily available outside the financial statements. 每个企业持有加密货币或者进行ICO 的目的，方法都可能不一样，所以这部分披露要根 据企业自身特点定制化打造。 Thus,detailed information concerning the company's holding of crypto assets and initial coin offerings(ICO)should be disclosed. The company's involvement in ICOs or other issues of crypto assets should be described as simply and directly as possible without a loss of material information and without unnecessarily increasing the length of the financial statements. 披露要把握度的平衡，既要细致保证完整性，又要考虑不增加财报披露的负担。其实就 是披露的收益要大于成本。 Additionally,the information disclosed should be organised in a way which highlights important matters which includes providing disclosures in an appropriate order and emphasising the important matters within them. It is important that the terms of an ICO are disclosed so that investors can determine the rights associated with it. 披露的重要性 The information about crypto assets should be linked when relevant to other information in the financial statements or to other parts of the annual report to highlight relationships between pieces of information and improve navigation through the financial statements. 披露的相关性 Commodity broker-traders holding crypto assets as inventory at fair value less costs to sell,in addition to the general IAS 2 Inventories requirements,will need to disclose the carrying amount of such inventories carried at fair value less costs to sell.In addition, IFRS 13 Fair Value Measurement disclosure requirements for recurring fair value measurements would also apply. 相关性的例子说明，根据持有目的披露你的计量方法 The information about crypto assets should be provided in a way which optimises comparability among entities and across reporting periods without compromising the usefulness of the information. 披露信息的可比性 Holders of crypto assets classified as intangible assets under IAS 38 Intangible Assets will need to disclose,by class,a reconciliation between the opening and closing carrying amounts,whether the useful life is assessed as indefinite,and,if so,the reasons supporting the indefinite useful life assessment,and a description of individually material holding. Finally,the proper application of materiality is key to determining what information to disclose.The judgemental nature of materiality assessments could lead to entities omitting useful information concerning crypto assets from the financial statements. Similarly,difficulties in exercising judgement around materiality could contribute to 'disclosure overload'. 披露信息的重要性水平 结论：本题看似讨论很高深的加密货币，ICO。 其实实质还是讨论信息的质量特征。信 息的质量特征就会围绕我们在 FRAMEWORK 里面学到的基本的质量信息特征和增强的 信息特征去谈。所以我们可以认为，任何一类涉及到信息好坏的讨论的题目，考生都可 以往信息质量特征上结合。比较重要的就是相关性，公允陈述，可比性，重要性，收益 大于成本。另外相关性可以细分为完整性，中立客观，无差错去讨论。 6分答出标红部分即可 (b)Advise whether the various development and promotional costs related to the ICO can be accounted for as an intangible asset at 31 March 20X7. (5 marks) 解题分析：本题问的是ICO 平台是否可以作为无形资产在报表记账。那就要先看 ICO 平 台是否满足无形资产的定义，再看其是否满足初始计量的条件。如果都满足就可以记账 If the costs do not satisfy the requirements of IAS 38,they are recognised as expenses. The costs satisfy the requirements for recognition of intangible assets if,and only if,it is probable that the future economic benefits which are attributable to the asset will flow to the entity and the cost of the asset can be measured reliably.The probability of future economic benefits must be based on reasonable and supportable assumptions about conditions which will exist over the life of the asset. 关于确认条件的很可能的准则的内容 In making the decision on recognition of the costs incurred,Symbal Co should evaluate whether after the issue of the tokens,it is still capable of controlling the trading platform and whether it may reasonably expect future economic benefits from the token holders. It is important to know whether Symbal Co will be able to get future economic benefits from token holders by providing them with future services other than another issue of tokens. 关于确认条件的很可能的准则的内容，结合案例的分析 If costs incurred will not ensure further economic benefits,they should be immediately recognised as an expense in profit or loss. 及其关键，重要，所有非金融资产的初始确认，资本化和费用化必写的一句判断标准。 就是这个支出是否是为了未来的经济利益流入，如果是那么收入成本要匹配，所以先资 本化，未来对应未来的经济利益流入，费用化。 In this case,Symbal Co promises to produce gains for investors from trading the tokens on the platform and in return,the company takes a percentage of the profit as a fee. Thus,the company can reasonably expect further economic benefits after the issue of tokens. The costs may be recognised as an intangible asset and amortised over the useful life of these assets. 上面判断标准的案例应用讨论，注意题目问的是怎么计量，并不是只问初始或者后续， 所以谈一句后续计量的一般标准 However,IAS 38 states that an entity should expense promotional activity costs when incurred.Thus,these costs should be excluded from the intangible asset. If during future reporting periods new circumstances are revealed,which indicate that there may be no more future economic benefits,then the value of the intangible asset would be impaired and written down. 上面判断标准的案例应用讨论，注意题目问的是怎么计量，并不是只问初始或者后续， 所以可谈一句减值处理的问题。 总结：这是SBR 现在的一种标准题型，一定要扣题，题目是 how to account,没有指明 是初始，还是后续，还是减值，还是确认。所以从头到尾完成得写一下如何初始确认， 后续计量，减值，还有终止确认，都会有分(虽然本题考官答案没写终止确认，但考生 写了应该是有分得) (c)Discuss how the receipt of $1 million cash in the pre-sale agreement should be accounted for in the financial statements for the year ended 31 March 20X7 and how the $10 million raised in the ICO should be accounted for in the financial statements for the year ended 31 March 20X8. (6 marks) 解题分析：当看到题目里面提到企业收钱，就要想到这是销售收钱，还是融资收钱。因 为题目有提到1M'raising funds', 这就是融资的key word, 那就说明这是融资行为。融 资行为就要考虑是股权融资还是债权融资。这里关键就是先判断是否是 FL, 如果不是， 就是 Equity。 讨论的关键是

1. 定义 The critical feature of a liability is the contractual obligation to deliver cash or another financial instrument. 2.2. 合同分类 a.基本金融合同(债券) b.优先股(现金结算) c.优先股等权益工具结算类合同 d.contingent payments Year ended 31 March 20X7 The success of the ICO is not within the control of Symbal Co as the ICO can be abandoned if the minimum fundraising level of $9 million is not reached.Neither does the investor have the right to be repaid $1 million in cash prior to 30 April 20X7. However,on the basis that the occurrence of a successful ICO is beyond the control of the entity,the agreement contains a financial obligation,because it represents a contractual obligation to deliver cash or another financial asset to another entity if the ICO does not occur by 30 April 20X7. At 31 March 20X7,the $1 million is viewed as a financial liability of Symbal Co in accordance with IAS 32 Financial Instruments: Presentation at initial recognition. 这里的1M 就是属于d.contingent payments 的范畴， Year ended 31 March 20X8 At 30 April 20X7,the funds paid by the holders of tokens of $10 million have the one-off right to 10%of profits from the year ended 31 March 20X8 but they do not have the right to their redemption or residual interest in the assets.Due to this reason,the company should not record any inflows as a financial liability or equity but record them as income by the following accounting entry: Dr Bank $10 million Cr Other financial income $10 million 股权融资和债券融资都会有付股利或者付利息的后续责任，如果没有后续义务那就不是 融资行为，不是融资行为，那就是赚钱了 Also at 30 April 20X7,the liability of $1 million recorded for the pre-sale agreement will be reversed and recorded as income. Initially,at 30 April 20X7,the commitment to the holders of tokens to pay 10%of annual profits for the year ended 31 March 20X8 is considered by Symbal Co to be a contingent liability.IAS 37 Provisions,Contingent Liabilities and Contingent Assets defines a contingent liability as a possible obligation depending on whether some uncertain future event occurs.The recognition of the liability depends on whether there are annual profits.Therefore,a liability should be recognised if the company earns profits during the reporting period to 31 March 20X8.Symbal Co will recognise a financial liability to the holders of tokens and an expense to profit or loss. 根据前面讲到的判断是否是 FL The tokens are not equity instruments as they do not have a residual interest in the assets of the entity after deducting all of its liabilities and they have a contractual obligation to deliver cash. (d)解题分析：本题首先要判断给到员工的ICO 是按照IFRS2 还是 IAS19 计 量 ，IFRS2 是 基于权益工具价格的结算，所以除非ICO 是 equity, 否则就一定不是IFRS2。 所以这 里只能拿是IAS19 员工福利. 对于IAS19 要区分是什么类型的员工福利，员工福利有短期福利(一年之内支付的), 长期福利(一年以后，但退休之前支付的),养老金福利(退休之后支付的),终止福 利(被企业终止劳动合同要支付的补偿)四种。根据题意是可以判断这里是短期福利， 所以按照短期福利的会计处理方式，确认短期负债，并计入当期损益 When assessing the accounting treatment of such arrangements,an entity should consider the characteristics of the ICO tokens generated.Equity is the residual interest in the assets of an entity after deducting all of its liabilities. Unless the ICO tokens meet the definition of equity,the arrangements would not meet the definition of a share-based payment arrangement in accordance with IFRS 2 Share-based Payment.Instead,they would fall within the scope of IAS 19 Employee Benefits as a non-cash employee benefit. 区分是IFRS2 还是IAS19 IAS 19 can then be used to determine the recognition,as well as the measurement,of the employee benefit.The tokens do not meet the definition of the equity of Symbal Co as they do not grant the directors a residual interest in the net assets of the Symbal Co. Therefore,the arrangements do not meet the definition of a share-based payment arrangement in accordance with IFRS 2.Instead,it is a non-cash short-term employee benefit. Short-term employee benefits are those expected to be settled wholly before 12 months after the end of the annual reporting period during which employee services are rendered.The substance of the arrangement is an exchange of employee services for the tokens. 根据IAS19 判断是哪种福利 The arrangement includes a condition that the directors should be in employment at 31 March 20X7.Symbal Co should recognise a liability and short-term employee benefit expense at 31 March 20X7.Symbal Co would measure the amount which it expects to pay by using the fair value of the tokens to be delivered to the employees,or by using the estimated cost of the goods or services which it expects to deliver in the future.This amount would be (5×$50,000)$250,000. Thus,at 31 March 20X7: Dr Employee costs $250,000 Cr Short-term employee benefit liability $250,000 短期福利的会计处理
2. 然后结合题干信息指出来哪些指标/因素是会影响到可持续经营假设的，可以根据我们所学习的 financial indicators,operating indicators and others这几个角度分别来写。 答案
explanation_zh: 可持续经营假设的定义 可持续经营假设是编制财务报表的最基本的假设，因此财报都是假设企业至少会在未来的12个月可以 继续按照当前的状态持续经营下去。如果这个假设不成立的话，企业必须按照另外的基础也就是破表 清算的基础来编制报表。(1分) 管理层责任 保证财报是基于正确合适的基础来编制的是管理层的责任，因此管理层需要判断企业当前以及未来面 临的这样状况会不会影响到企业的可持续假设，如果有影响的话，这些重大不确定性必须要在报表里 进行及时的披露。(1分) 重大不确定的披露 在评估持续经营假设是否合适时，该标准要求应考虑所有关于未来的可用信息，即至少从报告期结束 后12个月开始。(1分) 结合题目，找出 indicators 该评估需要在财务报表发布之日之前进行，并应考虑到政府和银行为缓解疫情影响而采取的措施。 Wing Co利用了业务削减贷款计划和国家银行的短期债务计划，但其业务在报告日期前后已暂停(1 分)。此外，该公司有几份合同可能会亏损，其投资性房地产似乎因疫情而贬值，董事应考虑到疫情 大流行对实体活动的现有和预期影响(1分)。 Wing Co有着盈利的经营历史，并依赖外部融资资源， 但由于该流行病，它发现自己陷入了困境。董事们必须考虑对流动性和盈利能力的预期影响，然后才 能确信持续经营的基础是适当的。鉴于业务已暂停，这一考虑尤其重要(1分)。 因为流行病不断演变的性质和相关的不确定性，所以需要作出重大判断并且不断更新截至财务报表发 布之日的评估结果。 Requirement解析： (ii) 亏损合同的计量。(4分) 答题思路： 首先指出亏损合同的定义和相关会计计量，亏损合同会导致预计负债的产生，以及存货的减值。再结 合题目指出产生亏损合同的原因。 答案解析： 亏损合同的定义， provision 的确认以及减值测试 亏损合同，是指履行合同义务不可避免会发生的成本超过预期经济利益的合同。(1分)待执行合同 GOLDEN EDUCATION ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM 变为亏损合同，同时该亏损合同产生的义务满足预计负债的确认条件的，应当确认为预计负债，预计 负债的金额应是执行合同发生的损失和撤销合同损失的较低者(1分)。在确定预计负债的时候，需 要先确定存货相关的减值(1分)。 结合题目指出原因 ( 1 分 ) 疫情影响全球供应链， Wing 签订的合同是固定价格的，因为全球生产供应的下降，导致Wing 必 须 要首先先从第三方高价采购才能运输相关货物。这就造成了成本激增。 所以所有的合同必须重新进行审核合同中义务的条款，看哪些是可以撤销并且不用支付赔偿金的合同， 这样可以降低经营成本(1分)。 Requirement解析： (iii) 投资性房地产的公允计量。(6分) 答题思路： 首先指出 FV measurement的定义以及疫情的不确定性所导致的FV的测试。接下来对公允价值的确 定进行具体的剖析，要分析 order transaction和 market based 以及active market,因为题目中提 到了当前市场低迷，交易不活跃，Wing 公司不会在当前的市场上进行交易，所以答案要体现公允价值 的定义的这几个方面。市场交易量少的话，会影响到公允价值的股价技术的选择，所以Level 2&3 需 要写出来。 答案解析： 公允价值的定义和不确定性 公允价值计量IFRS 13公允价值计量表示，公允价值计量(FV GOLDEN EDUCATION M) 的目标是估计在当前市场条件下， ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM 在计量日市场参与者之间进行有序交易以选择资产或转移负债的价格。(1分) 由于疫情，估值将受到重大测量不确定性的影响。因此， FVM 的可能估计范围将更广。Wing Co需 要进行判断，以找到在这种情况下最具代表性的公允价值。(1分) 有序交易 公允价值的定义要求有序交易，而不是强制交易。在估算 FVM 时，必须评估有序交易的证据。如果观 察到的价格是基于确定为强制的交易，则应在其上加上少量权重。董事会表示，他们不会以目前市场 上观察到的价格出售自己的资产，这并不意味着这些交易应该被推定为是被迫的。 ( 1 分 ) Market based(活跃市场+非活跃市场) 公允价值是一种基于市场的计量，而不是特定于实体的，并且与实体在市场低迷时持有资产的意图并 不相关。房地产市场的交易量大幅下降，因此，可以说市场并不活跃。 活跃市场是指资产或负债交易的频率和数量足以持续提供定价信息的市场。非活跃市场的可观察价格 可能无法代表公允价值。然而，不应忽视此类交易，并应考虑交易的相关性，如财产的类型和位置。 ( 1 分 ) FV 的估值技术 市场交易量的显著减少也会影响使用哪种估值技术。事实证明，市场方法具有挑战性，可能需要使用 其他估价技术。这可能需要不可观测输入值。 然而，交易量的大幅减少并不意味着市场不再活跃，因为交易仍可能以足够的频率和数量进行，以提 供定价信息。此外，一些类似于Wing Co持有的房产已于12月售出，因此，这些销售数据可用于衡 量其房产的公允价值。所以还必须考虑从这些市场的有序交易中观察到的相关价格。(1分) 当最近市场的交易价格可以获得时，使用 Level 2基于不可观察的输入(如收益法)来计量公允价值 是不合适的。(1分) 因此，在市场活动显著减少的情况下，评估可观察市场数据的相关性时需要进行判断。(1分) ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM Requirement解析： (b) 讨论投资者希望从报表里看到哪些信息以及信息的性质。需要讨论的内容需要包括不确定性，期后 事项，政府担保贷款的相关计量。(10分) 答题思路： 该题属于开放性的题目，所有合理的答案都会有分值。答题时可以围绕着以下展开。投资者比较关注 企业的盈利状况和现金流状况，尤其是在疫情大流行的之下，各种相关的不确定因素比如员工无法上 班，沟通交流困难，供应困难，政府政策等都有可能导致公司经营困难，以及盈利和现金流不好的结 果，所以投资人更需要一些公司的明确的披露和未来规划来了解公司信息，或者是增强对公司的信心。 如果不进行相关披露，投资人很有可能会投资其他产业。在疫情之下，公司对于员工的关怀，社会责 任感的体现，这些投资人想要了解的信息都可以写。 答案解析： 沟通效率+不确定时间的清晰明确解释 许多投资者将关注相关信息的披露，特别是关于流行病将如何影响企业报告的利润和现金流的信息。 投资者会担心，由于这场大流行，公司所面临的问题沟通效率低下，缺乏清晰度和可理解性。投资者 希望对不寻常或不经常发生的项目有明确的解释。 重要性水平+会计政策是否变更 重要性水平的判断评估可能导致公司在财务报表中遗漏有用的信息。公司自愿提供的绩效衡量信息通 常有助于投资者的分析。会计政策对于理解财务报表中的信息始终是必要的，并且与重大项目、交易 或事项有关。投资者希望了解是否有任何政策因疫情而发生变化以及企业是否在应用会计政策时做出 了重大判断或假设。 有效流程+疫情对企业的破坏 投资者将希望确保公司制定有效的流程，以识别和披露报告期后可能会影响其决策的重大事件。投资 者将需要有关该流行病对其投资的公司造成的破坏程度的信息，以及受重大估计不确定性影响的资产 和负债的信息，以便更好地了解财务影响。 收入的不确定性+合同是否能修改 公司需要使用重大的主观判断来确定与疫情相关的不确定性对其收入核算的影响，例如，可变对价的 估计。投资者将需要关于应对疫情所做决定的信息，例如，合同的修改、亏损合同以及可能受到影响 的正在进行和未来合同的披露。 能否收到保险赔偿金 其他不确定性，包括受疫情的影响，企业可以向保险公司提出索赔的潜在能力。保险约定的条款和条 件往往很复杂。投资者会想知道能够受到补偿的金额以及应收金额的任何不确定性受否反映在财务报 表中。 政府政策以及相关不确定性披露 企业需要仔细评估从政府获得的贷款、税收减免或退税的特点，并披露其影响。并且考虑与政府措施 产生的所得税相关的不确定性，公司是否要以不同金额确认和计量当前和递延所得税资产或负债。 可持续经营的披露 投资者将需要有关其业务影响的信息。例如，有关远程工作策略和办公限制的问题。考虑到疫情的严 重性，Wing Co可能会在未经投资者同意的情况下寻求一些在正常业务范围之外的其他运营，以应对 当前的业务中断。在这种情况之下，可持续经营变得至关重要。投资者需要了解裁员和业务持续发展 的计划，包括流行病对董事会和员工的影响。 疫情之下，公共卫生目标，远程行业将成为投资者关注的焦点。生物技术和医疗保健行业也将重新引 GOLDEN EDUCATION ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM 起人们的兴趣。因此，投资者可能会将资金从Wing Co转移到这些行业。 ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM

stem_zh: Q1 Kostas Co holds a controlling interest in Easton Co.It also operates a defined benefit pension scheme and holds financial assets.The Kostas directors are unsure how various matters will impact the consolidated financial statements for the year ended 30 April 20X8. The following exhibits,available on the left-hand side of the screen provide information relevant to the question: 1.Defined benefit pension scheme 2.Controlling shareholding in Easton Co. 3.Financial asset investment held by Kostas Co. 4.Potential acquisition of shares in Weston Co. This information should be used to answer the requirements within your chosen response options. Exhibit 1 Defined benefit pension scheme: Kostas Co operates a defined benefit scheme for its employees and the cost of remeasurements for the current year in accordance with IAS 19 Employee Benefits is $14 million.This remeasurement cost has been included in administrative expenses.In order to reduce the risks to which it is exposed,Kostas Co's directors are considering reducing the number of participants in the defined benefit scheme.The company would pay compensation from plan assets to those participants who are affected. Exhibit 2 Controlling shareholding in Easton Co: On 1 May 20X6,Kostas Co acquired 70%of the equity interests of Easton Co.The purchase consideration comprised cash of $150 million and the fair value of the identifiable net assets of Easton Co was $160 million at that date.The fair value of the non-controlling interest (NCI)in Easton Co at acquisition was $54 million. The share capital and retained earnings of Easton Co were $55 million and $85 million respectively and other components of equity were $10 million at the date of acquisition.The excess of the fair value of the identifiable net assets at acquisition over carrying amount is due to plant,which is depreciated using the straight-line method and has a five-year remaining life at the date of acquisition. Kostas Co disposed of a 10%equity interest to the NCI of Easton Co on 30 April 20X8 for a cash consideration of $34 million.The carrying amount of the net assets of Easton Co at 30 April 20X8 was $210 million before any adjustments on consolidation.Goodwill has been impairment tested annually and as at 30 April 20X7 had reduced in value by 15%and at 30 April 20X8 had lost a further 5%of its original value before the sale of the equity interest to the NCI. Exhibit 3 Financial asset investment: On 1 May 20X5,Kostas Co purchased a $20 million five-year bond with semi-annual interest of 5%payable on 31 October and 30 April.The purchase price of the bond was $21.62 million. The effective annual interest rate is 8%or 4%on a semi-annual basis.The bond is classified as an amortised cost financial asset in accordance with IFRS 9 Financial Instruments.During the preparation of the consolidated financial statements at the start of June 20X8,the financial controller identified that the interest due on the bond at 30 April 20X8 had not been received.She observed that the October 20X7 interest had been paid almost four weeks after the due date and is concerned that the asset may be impaired.She is considering changing the classification of the asset to fair value through other comprehensive income(FVTOCI)in order to avoid having to test for impairment. Exhibit 4 Potential acquisition of shares: The directors of Kostas Co are considering the purchase of an equity stake in another company,Weston Co, however they are concerned that the investment is of a risky nature.They are looking to acquire a 25% holding initially and if the investment proves to be successful then they will look to acquire a further 50%of the equity shares,to give a holding of75%. a)Draft an explanatory note to the directors of Kostas Co,addressing the following issues relevant to the consolidated financial statements: i) How the defined benefit scheme should be accounted for and how the change in the defined benefit scheme should be treated in the financial statements of Kostas.(6 marks) Requirements
explanation_zh: 考查DBP 应该如何进行账务处理以及案例中公司准备对养老金计划进行缩减以及结算应该如何在财报上列 示。 答案解析： DBP 是风险由企业承担的一种养老金计划，员工退休之后能够拿到固定金额的养老金，通常是按照员 工退休那年年薪的比例来支付养老金。 对于 DBP 会出现养老金资产的公允价值和养老金义务的现值这么两个科目， 一个是资产一个是负债， 但是真正会列示在报表上的是养老金净负债或净资产，即用养老金义务的现值减掉养老金资产之后的 净额列示。而这两个数是直接由精算师计算出来的，我们所要考虑的是年初养老净负债或净资产和年 末养老金净负债或将资产之间的差额是如何变动而来的，而且这两者时间的差额要么是计入当期损益 要么是计入其他综合收益。 接下来我们将对这中间的所有交易进行解释说明：DBP 净负债或净资产的利息净额是要计入当期损益 的；当期服务成本是要计入当期损益的；DBP 净负债或净资产的重新计量是要计入其他综合收益的， 主要是来自于精算假设的变化和资产投资汇报差异。因此公司将DBP 净负债或净资产的重新计量计入 当期损益中的管理费用是不正确的。 公司在考虑减少DBP 涵盖的职工人数，这个其实属于过去服务成本中的缩减，缩减会导致养老金义务 现值减少，从而使得费用减少。结算会使得养老金义务现值和养老金资产公允价值减少，如果支付的 金额与养老金义务的现值减少金额不一致，还会产生结算利得或损益。 公司如果真的进行缩减之后，那么与养老金相关的假设已经发生变化，那么后续再计量养老金相关的 交易的时候就要用新的假设。 ii)A calculation and explanation of the adjustment in equity arising from the disposal of the 10%equity interest on 30 April 20X8.(12 marks) Requirement解析： 考查分步处置中的大股东和小股东之间的交易，即原先持股70%然后卖掉10%还持股60%的交易。 答案解析： 在 2 0x8 年 的 4 月 3 0日，母公司将自己所持有的的子公司70%的股份卖掉10%给少数股东，卖掉之 后持股比例为60%,还是能够控制子公司。 NCI 的持股比例就从原先的30%增长到了40%,NCI 的 金额会增加，其增加的金额为(处置当天子公司净资产的价值+商誉)*10%。所以我们要想方设法把 这两个金额算出来。 首先计算收购日当天的商誉=母公司收购子公司的对价150m+ 少数股东权益的公允价值54m- 收购日 子公司可辨认净资产的公允价值160m=44m。 处置当天子公司的商誉=44m*(1-20% 因为减值了百分之二十)=35.2m。 处置日当天子公司净资产在合并报表上的账面价值=210m+ 公允价值的调增(160-55-85-10)10-公 允价值调整的调整即补计提的折旧(10/5*2yrs)4m=216m。 NCI 增加的金额=(216m+35.2m)*10%=25.12m 母公司卖掉10%的股份收到现金34m,NCI 增加25.12m, 所以轧差进equity 的金额为8.88m。 iii)How the bond should be classified and th e accounting treatment of the potential impairment.(6 marks) You should ignore tax effects. Requirement解析： 关于金融资产的分类以及金融资产的减值问题的考查。 答案解析： 公司在获得该债券的时候的目的就是希望持有至到期然后定期的去收本息，所以初始入账时划分为摊 余成本法核算的金融资产是正确的。 金融资产只有当公司持有其目的发生改变之后才能进行重分类。而且公司要想用FVTOCI 核算，那必 须是公司持有的目的变成了既想持有至到期定期去收本金和利息又想说如果有机会可以出售它。 公司的财务负责人想将其重分类至FVTOCI, 因为他觉得这样子可以避免去做减值测试，其实这种想法 是错误的，即使债券按照FVTOCI 核算，该进行减值测试还是无法避免的。他和按照摊余成本法的债 券减值方法一样，区别只是在于减值损失计入OCI, 而不是损益。而且在FVTOCI 的情况下，金融资 产年底时以公允价值列示，而在摊余成本法下，金融资产是用减值之后的净额列示在报表上。 初始入账的时候公司就需要针对该债券确认12个月的预期信用损失，根据题目意思，当逾期超过30 天，说明处于减值模型的stage 2,即信用风险有显著增加，所以要确认整个生命周期的预期信用损失。 相关的金额账务处理方式是借方费用贷方损失准备，后期计息还是按照扣掉减值损失之前的金额为基 础乘上实际利率。 b)Explain how the initial 25%stake in Weston Co would be accounted for in both the financial statements of Kostas Co and also the consolidated financial statements,and discuss how an additional 50%stake would be accounted for in the consolidated financial statements.(6 marks)(30 marks) Requirement解析： 持股25%在单体报表以及合并报表上的账务处理。额外再购买50%的股份在合并报表上的账务处理。 答案解析： 购买25%的股份一般会使得投资方能够对被投资方享有重大影响，即构成对联营公司的投资，对联营 公司的投资在单体报表上可以用成本法，公允价值以及权益法核算。 对联营公司的投资在合并报表上只能用权益法。在合并资产负债表上，会形成一个investment in associate 的资产类科目，其金额由初始入账成本+分享的联营公司的净资产变动中归属于母公司的比 例-减值部分-收到的来自联营公司的股息。在合并综合收益表上会有两个科目， share of profit of associate 和 share of OCl of associate。 后续如果投资方又额外购买50%的股份，那么就总共持股75%的股份，即构成控制，成为母子公司的 关系。该笔交易的实质相当于是把原先所持的25%的股份卖掉了，然后再买了75%的股份回来。所以 要将原先所持的25%的股份的账面价值调整至公允价值，账面价值和公允价值的差额确认为利得或损 失。因为实现了控制属于需要在收购日去计算商誉，从这一天起子公司的资产负债收入费用就需要和 母公司的合并起来。剩下母公司没有持股的25%的股份在合并报表上相当于是NC 少数股东权益，列 示在合并资产负债表权益中，期末NCI 金额=收购日当天NCI 金额+成为子公司之后子公司产生的损 益中乘少数股东持股比例。

stem_zh: Q2 Exhibit 1 Ethical issues and investment property: A trainee member of the accounting department has proposed to Ms Smeaton,the head accountant and an ACCA member,a number of initiatives aimed at increasing Heathcliff Co's profitability for the current period. The trainee is the nephew of Heath cliff Co's chairman,whose annual bonus is based on reported profit.After the trainee accountant proposed the initiatives,an email was accidently circulated around the company stating that if the initiatives were implemented the trainee would receive 20%of any bonus paid to the chairman.Thee trainee has proposed that a property owned by Linton Co,a subsidiary of Heathcliff Co,should be classified as investment property.The property is rented to Lucas,another of Heathcliff Co's subsidiaries.The trainee accountant argues that by classifying the property as investment property,any gains in value willbe included in profit or loss but any fall in fair value can be deferred by including in other comprehensive income. 题干
explanation_zh: Trainee 是 H 公司董事会主席的侄子，主席的奖金是以利润为基础来计算的。在提出这些计划后， 一 封电子邮件意外地在公司周围传开，称如果这些计划得到实施，trainee将收到主席奖金的20%。 Trainee 建议将H 公司的子公司L 公司拥有的房地产归类为投资性房地产。该房产出租给了Lucas, 这是H 公司的另一个子公司。trainee认为，通过将房地产分类为投资性房地产，价值增值都将计入 损益，公允价值的下降都可以通过计入其他综合收益来递延。 Exhibit 2 Useful life of assets: The trainee has suggested that the useful life of all plant and equipment should be increased from five years to ten years.The trainee argues that this will increase the annual profit dramatically and,as the asset has already been paid for,no one is bothered about how much depreciation is charged for the assets. 题干解析： Trainee 建议所有PPE的使用寿命从5年延长至10年，这样会导致折旧下降，利润大幅上升，并且也 没人在意资产到底计提了多少折旧。 Exhibit 3 Inventory write down: The trainee has proposed classifying a large write-down in inventories that occurred in the current period as a prior period adjustment. The trainee argues that the inventories were probably impaired over 12 months ago, In the year ended 31 December 20X8,and therefore it was a mistake of the previous period not to write down the inventories. 题干解析： Trainee 建议把当期产生的大量减值的存货分类为上期的调整事项，他认为可以把这些存货当作是前 12个月减值的，放在今年是个错误，就不需要在今年的报表里计提减值。 Exhibit 4 Loan notes: The trainee suggests recognising a substantial discount on loan notes acquired in the year as income in profit or loss.The loan notes were issued by one of Heathcliff Co's suppliers.The supplier had been experiencing significant financial difficulty which explains the deep discount below nominal value on issue of the loan notes. 题干解析： Trainee 建议把今年购入的债券的实质性折扣确认在利润表里。这个债券是H 公司的其中一个供应商发 行的，这个供应商今年遭遇了重大的财务困难，所以才折价发行这些公司债。 Requirements: (a)(i)Discuss whether the property meets the definition of investment property in Linton's separate financial statements and Heathcliffs consolidatedfinancial statements and explain how changes in fair value should be reflected in the financial statements.(4 marks) Requirement解析： 讨论是否符合 IP 的定义以及公允价值变动记账的问题，因此答题思路也就结合准则：IP 以 及PPE 的 定 义及计量条件进行分析即可。 答案解析： The accounting for the investment property will not affect the reported profits of Heathcliff as a separate entity because it is owned by Linton,but it will affect the consolidated financial statements.In the separate financial statements of Linton,the subsidiary,it may be correct to classify the property as investment property and recognise any gains in fair value in profit or loss;any fall in fair value must also be recognised in profit or loss. 在 L 个别财报里，如果这个房地产确实是属于投资性房地产的话，后续计量可以采用 FV model, 关 于 FV 的变动，不管是上升还是下降，都需要计入 P/L, 不影响OCl。 However,when it comes to the consolidated statements,the property cannot be classified as investment property as it is owner-occupied by the Heathcliff group.Instead it will be classified as property,plant and equipment (IAS 16Property,Plant and Equipment). 但是在合并报表层面，H 公司买入L 公司的IP, 这属于H 集团自有的资产，所以不能当作集团的IP, 而是要分类为PPE。 The property can be revalued if all other assets in the class are revalued,but any increases in value are presented as other comprehensive income and therefore not included in profit or loss.If there is a decrease in the value of the asset then that decrease will be included in profit or loss unless the asset had previously been revalued upwards.The asset must also be depreciated,even if it has been revalued,with the expense charged to profit or loss.The suggestion by the accountant is therefore incorrect. 如果集团这一类的PPE 全部进行重估的话，这个房地产也可以进行重估，重估增值的部分需要计入 OCI, 而不是P/L 。如果是重估减值的话，需要先把OCI 记录的增值的部分转回，剩余的损失再计入 P/L。并 且PPE重估过后需要按照重估价来计提折旧，折旧金额计入 P/L。所以这个会计的建议是非常 不正确的。 (ii)Discuss whether the trainee's assertions regarding the useful life of assets are correct.(3 marks) Requirement解析： 讨论资产可使用年限的确认问题，涉及到资产折旧，会计估计以及会计估计变更相关知识点。 答案解析： The determination of the useful life of non-current assets is required in order to calculate depreciation expense.Depreciation expense meets the IAS 8 definition of an accounting estimate,being a monetary amount in financial statements subject to measurement uncertainty.IAS8 Accounting Policies,Changes in Accounting Estimate and Errors permits changes in accounting estimates as a result of new information,new developments or more experience. A change in an input to an accounting estimate,such as useful life, results in a change in an accounting estimate and should be applied prospectively.Therefore,if the useful life of Heathcliff's asset sis judged to have increased,subsequent depreciation will be recognised over the revised remaining useful life resulting in a lower annual depreciation charge and higher annual profits.However,useful life should not be increased without justification,such as technological advances that have increased the life of the asset. 使用寿命涉及到折旧的计算，折旧是会计估计，会计估计的变更需要反映资产更加相关公允的信息， 并且会计估计的变更是不需要追溯调整的，只影响当前和以后。因此如果PPE 的使用寿命全部延长， 每年计提的折旧费用就会大大降低，从而导致利润增加。但是折旧的会计估计的变更是需要专业人士 进行专业技术的判断的，并不是会计人员主观想增加就增加。 The trainee's assertion that no one is bothered about the depreciation charge just because the asset has been paid for is arguable.Depreciation expense does affect reported profits;stakeholders rely on reported profits when making decisions relating to an entity.However,it is true that many analysts will add back depreciation expense to reported profits when comparing results of entities over time and against other entities.There may therefore be some justification in the initiative,but it should not be done just to increase profits Trainee 认为没有人在意折旧的变更，因为这些资产已经买过来了。这其实是不对的。折旧的计算会影 响费用，从而影响利润，利润是利益相关者做决策的时候非常关注的一个经营指标，所以有很多人是 在意折旧的。但是为了增加不同公司的会计信息的可比性，在计算一些指标的时候确实也会先调整折 旧。这里确实存在主观的判断，但是这并不可以增加利润。 (iii)Discuss whether the inventory write down is a prior period adjustment.(3 marks) Requirement解析： 讨论存货的减值以及是否涉及前期会计差错的调整 答案解析： One of the principles of IAS 2 Inventories is that inventory is measured at the lower of cost and net realisable value(NRV).If NRV has fallen below cost,inventory must be written down and any impairment charged to profit or loss in the period of the write-down.The issue here is the timing of the impairment. 存货需要以成本和可变现净值孰低来入账。当可变现净值小于成本，存货就发生了减值，减值损失应 该计入当期P/L。 If the decrease in inventory value relates to the previous period and an error was made in not writing down the inventory in that period,the accountant is correct and the error should be treated as a prior period error.If the error arose in the year ended 31 December 20X8,the results of that year should be restated when presented as comparatives in the financial statements for the year ended 31 December 20X9.As a result,the reported profit for the previous year will be reduced and therefore the opening retained earnings of the current year will also be reduced.Profit or loss for the current year will,however,be unaffected. 如果是上期存货发生的减值，那就应该计入上期的P/L 中，不能够记在当期。如果上期没记，说明有 会计差错，差错是需要调整的，否则会影响当期报表数据。前期差错如果影响的是利润的话，会影响 RE, 当期调整差错就应该把调整的内容体现在期初的RE中。因此不会对当期的P/L 产生影响。 If the impairment occurred in the current period(e.g.due to fire damage),the write-down must be made in the current period resulting in a reduction of current year profit.The statement by the trainee accountant may be correct,but the head accountant should investigate the timing of the impairment.It cannot be treated as prior period just to please the chairman and increase his bonus. 如果是当期存货发生的减值，那就必须体现在当期的P/L 中，降低利润。所以在计提减值的时候一定 要注意检查减值的时间。不能为了自身利益操纵当期 P/L, (iv)Discuss how the loan notes should be accounted for by Heathcliff Co.(3 marks) Requirement解析： 问的是债券该如何做会计处理，结合题意，需要应用IFRS9 准则：金融资产信用减值相关知识点作答。 答案解析： The significant financial difficulty of the supplier is evidence that the investment in loan notes is credit impaired on purchase(i.e.events that will have a detrimental effect on cash flows associated with the financial asset have already occurred). IFRS 9 Financial Instruments applies and requires that the investment is measured at fair value at acquisition; however,no loss allowance is initially recognised.This it the financial asset is credit adjusted(i.e.it takes into account the initial lifetime expected credit losses in the estimated cash flows). The trainee is therefore incorrect to suggest that the asset is recognised at nominal value with the discount recognised as income in profit or loss. 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION IFRS 9 要求金融工具自购买就应该用公允价值计量。供应商因为面临重大的财务困难儿折价很多发行 了债券，这也就意味着是H 公司在购买债券的时候就已经发生了信用减值，所以初始计量就应该用经 过信用调整之后的实际利率来计量，初始不需要记信用减值损失。 Trainee 建议直接把折价计入 P/L, 是不正确的。 (b)Discuss the ethical issues arising from the scenario which Ms Smeaton needs to consider and what actions she should take as a consequence.(5 marks) Professional marks will be awarded in part(b)for the quality of the ethical discussion.(2 marks) Requirement解析： 道德题解题思路，按照职业道德要求-->案例道德冲突+相关建议来进行即可。 答案解析： Ms Smeatonisa member of ACCA,a professional body,and is therefore bound by its Code of Ethics and Conduct.As a professional accountant,Ms Smeatonis expected to meet standards of professional behaviour and carry out her duties with due care and for the benefit of the company as a whole and not individuals within the company. 职业道德要求：专业性+尽职尽责，强调为公司利益而不是个人利益 As Heathcliff prepares accounts in accordance with IFRS Standards,those standards must be followed. Accounting treatments cannot be selected based on their impact on profitability.Although IFRS Standards do allow judgement to be applied,that judgement is not driven by the desires of the chairman of the company. Some judgement may wellbe required in the situations given: ·When assessing the useful life of an asset;and ·To identify the actual point in time when the inventories were impaired. 会计政策需要体现专业判断，不能是个人主观利益的驱使而选择的。 If Ms Smeaton feels pressured into taking actions of an unethical nature because of the relationship between the trainee and Heathcliff's chairman,she should seek assistance initially from her direct superior(e.g.finance director)and if that does not solve the problem she should turn to a committee of the board of directors or the board itself. As the email has now circulated within the company,Ms Smeaton must take a step back and distance herself from the proposals because the trainee has a clear conflict of interest.The head accountant should seek to ensure that the correct accounting treatment is applied in accordance with IFRS Standards,rather than the wishes of the trainee or the chairman. 亲密关系：自我利益冲突。解决：她应该首先寻求直接上级(例如财务总监)的帮助，如果这不能解 决问题，她应该求助于董事会委员会或董事会本身。由于这封电子邮件目前已在公司内部传阅，斯梅 顿女士必须退后一步，与提议者保持距离，因为有明显的利益冲突。首席会计师应努力确保根据IFRS 标准而不是trainee 或董事长的意愿进行正确的会计处理。 Ms Smeaton should consider whether the accounting judgements and decisions are for the benefit of the company as a whole and not one or two individuals within the company.Most of the initiatives may well generate greater profits for the company,but both IFRS standardand the Code of Ethics and Conduct must be adhered to. The trainee accountant may risk losing his professional qualification if it can be proven that he has acted in an unethical manner.The chairman is also in breach of his fiduciary duties(e.g.failing to exercise independent judgement)and having compromised the accountant's position may be regarded as an abuse of power. 她应考虑会计判断和决策是否有利于公司整体，而不是公司内部的一个或两个个人。大多数举措很可 能为公司带来更大的利润，但必须遵守国际财务报告准则标准和道德与行为准则。如果可以证明 trainee 的行为不道德，那么他可能会失去职业资格。chairman 也违反了其受托职责(例如，未能行 使独立判断),并且损害了会计师的地位，可能被视为滥用权力。

stem_zh: Q4 Branwell,a public limited company,designs and manufactures footwear under its own label,making wide use of social media influencers to market its products.It has a financial year end of 31 December 20X3. The following exhibits,available on the left-hand side of the screen,provide information relevant to the question. 1.Renovation costs-provides information about a clause in the lease agreement relating to the head office. 2.Licensing arrangement-includes information on a licensing arrangement with another party. 3.Flood-provides details of a flood affecting Branwell's operations during the year. This information should be used to answer the question requirements within the response option provided. Exhibits1 Renovation costs: Branwell leased its head office during the current accounting period and the agreement terminates in six years' time.There is a clause in the lease relating to the internal condition of the property at the termination of the lease.The clause states that the internal condition of the property should be identical to that at the outset of the lease. Branwell has improved the building by adding another floor to part of the building during the current accounting period. There is also a clause which enables the landlord to recharge Branwell for costs relating to the general disrepair of the building at the end of the lease. 题干
explanation_zh: Branwell 在本会计期间租用了其总部，该协议在六年内终止。租约中有一项条款涉及到租约终止时财 产的内部状况。该条款规定，财产的内部状况应与租约开始时相同。Branwell 改进了建筑，在本会计 期间增加了一层。还有一项条款允许房东在租约结束时为Branwell的一般失修有关的费用。 分析：Branwell 有自己支出改进建筑，就涉及支出资本化和费用化的问题。同时该建筑是租来的，就 涉及到租赁负债和租赁支付的会计处理问题。 Exhibits2 Licensing arrangement: To expand its market base Branwell intends to enter into a two-year arrangement with an overseas company, Parson Co whereby Parson will manufacture and sell some of Branwell's designs under licence.The licence may be cancelled by either party after 12 months.Branwell will provide Parson with its designs and details of the manufacturing processes to make its patented 'Barefoot'soles.Parson will not be not entitled to manufacture any new designs that Branwell releases over the course of the contract,however Branwell will provide details of updates to manufacturing processes in relation to existing designs.Branwellcontinually works on improving its manufacturing processes to save costs and improve output.The agreement has not yet been finalised because Branwell has not decided on the form of consideration.It will either require Parson to pay Branwell a single up-front fee of $3million,or 15%of its net revenues from the sale of the footwear each quarter. 题干解析： 为了扩大其市场基础，Branwell打算与一家海外公司Parson 签订为期两年的协议，Parson 将在许可 下生产和销售 Branwell 的一些设计。任何一方均可在12个月后取消该许可证。Branwell 将为Parson 提供其设计和制造过程的细节，以制造其获得专利的“赤脚”鞋底。Parson 将无权制造Branwell 在 合同过程中发布的任何新设计，但Branwell 将提供与现有设计相关的制造工艺更新的细节。Branwell 不断努力改进其制造工艺，以节约成本和提高产量。由于Branwell 尚未决定考虑的形式，因此尚未最 终达成协议。该公司要么要求Parson 向 Branwell 支付300万美元的预付费用，要么是每季度鞋类销 售净收入的15%。 分析：IFRS15无形资产知识版权的收入五步法。这里涉及第三步和第五步。 Exhibits3 Flood: At the start of December 20X3,flooding caused extensive damage to one of Branwell's manufacturing sites.A clean up operation,which incurred significant cost,removed debris and excess water from the site by 20 December,however engineers identified that the foundations of an older building on the site,which was used for Branwell's packaging operations,had become unstable.Management decided on 22 December to build a new replacement property on the site to house its packaging operations,using more robust weather and flood resistant materials and design.A contractor was engaged at the end of December and there build was expected to start in February20X4.The work would take an extended period of time and mean that Branwell's manufacturing capacity would be reduced.Accordingly the company's accountants budgeted a loss for the manufacturing site for the year ended 31 December 20X4.In March20X4,the government announced that the flooding was related to climate change and was expected to become a more regular occurrence.It therefore intended to construct flood barriers along rivers and would increase company tax rates by 2%to raise funds for this purpose. 题干解析： 在 2 0X3 年12月初，洪水对Branwell的一个制造基地造成了大面积的破坏。到12月20日，清理工 作造成了大量费用，从现场清除了碎片和多余的水，但工程师们发现，现场上用于 Branwell 包装作业 的一幢旧建筑的地基已经变得不稳定。管理层于12月22日决定在现场建造一个新的替代物业，以容 纳其包装操作，该物业将使用更坚固的天气和防洪材料和设计。 一个承包商在12月底签约，预计在 20X4 年2月开始建造。这项工作将需要较长时间，这意味着 Branwell的生产能力将下降。因此，公 司的会计计提了截至20x4 年 1 2 月 3 1日的制造厂房亏损。20X4 年3月，政府宣布洪水与气候变化 有关，预计将成为更频繁的事件。因此，它打算在河流沿岸修建防洪堤，并将公司税率提高2%,以为 此筹集资金。 分析：首先有洪水破坏，自然涉及到资产减值的问题。另外因为有改造工程，还会又涉及到改进支出是 否确认和资本化和费用化的问题。另外还有税率变动，这是一个比较综合的题目。 (a)Discuss the following accounting issues relating to Branwell's annual financial statements for the year ended 31 December 20X3,in accordance with IFRS Standards. (i) How Branwell should account for the additional floor in the head office and costs to be incurred at the end of the lease.(5 marks) (ii) How Branwell should account for the licensing arrangement in accordance with IFRS 15.You should refer to both payment options(8 marks) Requirement解析： (a)根据IFRS 标准，讨论了与Branwell截至20X3 年12月31日的年度财务报表相关的以下会计问题。 (i)Branwell应如何说明总部的额外楼层，以及在租约结束时将产生的费用。(5分) (ii)Branwell 应如何按照IFRS 15的规定的解释许可证收入。您应参考两种付款选项(8分) 分析思路： 第一步：分配分值。本题各小问分值明确；可略过。 第二步：判断准则。会计处理相关题目，一定要结合案例和题目判断准确考核的准则。在本题我们可 以判断出(i)涉及的是IAS16 和 IFRS16 lease;(ii)涉及的是IFRS15 收入的无形资产版权和五步法的结合。 第三步：判断考核对应准则的哪个考点。一般来说，准则考点为定义，初始确认，初始计量，后续计 量 ，终止确认这几个点。考核要么选取其中一个来考，要么综合进行考核。本题我们可以发现(i)IAS16 的资本化和费用化和IFRS16 lease的租赁负债的确认和计量；(ii)IFRS15收入的无形资产版权和五步法 的结合。 按上述步骤分析完毕后，再开始答题，才有效率。 答案解析： (a)(i)Renovation costs Branwell should capitalise the costs of the extra floor in accordance with IAS over the sixyears of the lease. 改进楼层满足资本化的条件就先资本化再折旧。 16 and depreciate these costs The requirement to restore the property at the end of the lease term to the conditions required by the terms and conditions of the lease is often referred to as a dilapidation clause.IFRS 16 Leases recognises that an obligation for those costs may be incurred at the lease commencement date or as it progresses.Such costs are recognised as part of the cost of the right-of-use asset when the obligation for the costs arises and the obligation is measured and recognised in accordance with IAS 37 Provisions,Contingent Liabilities and Contingent Assets. 结合IAS37 和 IFRS16,现在有义务在未来去支出，也是lease payment 的一部分，应包含在PV of lease liability 中。同时资本化到ROU asset。 The expected costs of removing the additional floor should be recognised as a provision when the obligating event occurs(i.e.when the floor is added),providing that a reliable estimate of the costs can be made.The corresponding accounting entry is an increase to the carrying amount of the right-of-use asset.The right-of use asset,including the restoration costs,is depreciated over the remainder of the lease term. 现在资本化的ROU asset 未来要在租期内折旧。 As regards the disrepair of the building,the estimated costs should be spread over the six years of thee agreement. IAS 37 would indicate that Branwell has a present obligation arising from the lease agreement because the landlord can recharge the costs of any repair to Branwell.Thee obligating event is the wear and tear to the building which willarise gradually over the tenancy period and its repair can be enforced through the legal agreement. The obligation relates to wear and tear and is not related to future operating costs. The wear and tear will result in at transfer of economic resources and a reliable estimate of the yearly obligation will be made,although it will not necessarily equate to one sixth per year.This amount will increase the provision that is already recognised in respect of dilapidations and is added to the carrying amount of the right-of-use asset,which is then depreciated over the remaining lease term. 现在有现时义务，在未来修复的支出也应该先计提预计负债，并资本化到ROU asset 中，再在租期内 摊销。 (ii)Revenue Branwell is providing Parson with rights to use its intellectual property (its designs and patented processes) under licence.The promises to provide these are not distinct because they are interdependent within thee context of the contract.Therefore the contract includes a single performance obligation. IFRS 15 guidance on licensing arrangements applies if there is an up-front payment.The transaction price is fixed at $200,000 and there is a single performance obligation;the key accounting issue is therefore whether revenue is recognised at a point in time or over time. 先判断1个合同内有几个履约义务，以及收入确认的类型是 at a point in time or over time. A performance obligation relating to licensing is satisfied over time if the arrangement provides access to the seller's intellectual property as it exists throughout the licence period.It is satisfied at a point in time if the arrangement provides access to the seller's intellectual property as it existed when the licence was granted. 如果这个知识版权是right to access throughout the licence period 就 是over time 如果是right to access when the licence was granted 就是 at point。 IFRS 15 goes on to specify that revenue is recognised over time if all of the following criteria are met: 1.The seller is required or expected to undertake activities that affect the intellectual property to which the licensee has rights.In this case the licence does not provide Parson with access to any new designs,however it does entitle Parson to details of the expected updates to manufacturing processes. 2.The licensee is exposed to positive or negative effects of the seller's activities that affect the intellectual property.Here it can be argued that Parson's success in manufacturing and retailing Branwell branded 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION footwear is directly linked to Branwell's activities in marketing the brand and engaging influencers to promote the brand on social media. 3.Any activities that the seller undertakes in relation to the promised designs and processes does not result in the transfer of goods or services to the licensee.Here further activities by Branwell in relation to the intellectual property are not separate performance obligations.

stem_zh: 4. 企业向客户授予的知识产权许可，同时满足下列3项条件的，应当作为在某一时段内履行的履约义务 确认相关收入；否则，应当作为在某一时点履行的履约义务确认相关收入。 (1)合同要求或客户能够合理预期企业将从事对该项知识产权有重大影响的活动。 (2)该活动对客户将产生有利或不利影响。 (3)该活动不会导致向客户转让某项商品。 Therefore where a fixed payment is made at the start of the arrangement,a contract liability is recognised and transferred to be recognised as revenue over time on a straight line basis.The contract is cancellable after 12 months and so the transaction price of $3 million should be recognised as revenue at $250,000per month for the first year of the contract.No further revenue is recognised in the second year should the contract not be cancelled. 从本案例可以判断是over time。应该分段确认收入。同时先收钱，再服务。收钱的时候只能确认为递 延收益。 The second payment option is a sales-based royalty.IFRS 15 provides specific guidance on this form of consideration.Revenue is recognised at the later of: ·When the sale or usage occurs,and ·When the performance obligation to which all or some of the sales-based royalties relate has been satisfied. 准则规定的知识产权收入按客户情况进行确认时的确认时间点的要求。 Therefore under this option Branwell should recognise revenue as Parson makes sales. Since revenue is contingent upon the level of sales achieved by Parson,Branwell should only recognise royalty revenue when it is probable that it will be received.This will normally be when sales have occurred.Practically Parson may be required to report its sales monthly or quarterly to Branwell and receipt of this sales information will allow Branwell to recognise revenue. (b)Discuss the financial reporting implications of the flood in the year ended 31 December 20X3 with reference to relevant IFRS Standards.(6 marks) Requirement
explanation_zh: (b)参考相关IFRS 标准讨论截至20X3 年12月31日的年度洪水的财务报告影响。 分析思路： 第一步：分配分值。本题各小问分值明确；可略过。 第二步：判断准则。会计处理相关题目，一定要结合案例和题目判断准确考核的准则。在本题我们可 以判断出涉及的是IAS36 和 IAS16 为 主 ； 第三步：判断考核对应准则的哪个考点。一般来说，准则考点为定义，初始确认，初始计量，后续计 量，终止确认这几个点。考核要么选取其中一个来考，要么综合进行考核。 按上述步骤分析完毕后，再开始答题，才有效率。 答案解析： The flood occurred in December 20X3,in the month preceding the year end of 31 December 20X3.Some costs had been incurred by the reporting date,however other matters remained unresolved. Branwell has incurred significant costs in conducting a clean-up operation during the financial year.These costs should be expensed as incurred and are therefore recognised in full in the statement of profit or loss for the year ended 31 December 20X3.These costs are non-recurring in nature and the management of Branwell may therefore choose to present them as exceptional items by presenting them as a single line item in the statement of profit or loss as permitted by IAS 1.The aim of such presentation is to provide more useful information to users. 资本化和费用化的问题，另外根据准则要求，如果特别重要，需要再利润表单独列报。 The flood has caused some building foundations to become unstable,indicating that the building used for packaging operations is impaired and its carrying amount should be written down to recoverable amount. 关注IAS36 减值的讨论，先要讨论减值迹象，和减值的基本规则就是不能高于可回收金额recoverable amount. This building is unlikely to generate cash flows that are independent of those from other assets and therefore the impairment test should be conducted for the cash-generating unit that the building belongs to.This may be the property as a whole or it may be the whole manufacturing site. 关注IAS36 减值的讨论，题目如果没有明确说明，就要讨论是否用CGU 进行减值测试。 Any impairment should be recognised in the financial statements at 31 December 20X3;if testing is not complete by the reporting date,the determination of recoverable amount after the reporting period (but beforee the financial statements are authorised for issue)is an adjusting event as it provides evidence of year end conditions. 如果减值测试跨越了会计期间，要考虑期后调整事项的问题。 Any impairment loss should be recognised in profit or loss. 减值损失计入当期损益 (c)Discuss,with examples,the nature of a reclasssification adjustment an d the arguments for and against allowing reclassification of items to profit or loss.You should refer to the Conceptual Framework in your discussion.You are not required to refer to any of the exhibits.(6 marks)(25 marks) Requirement解析： (c) 通过例子讨论重新分类调整的性质，以及支持和反对允许重新分类项目为损益的论点。您应该在 讨论中参考概念框架。你不需要参考任何上述案例。 分 析：重分类会把对应的资产和负债在原准则终止确认，在新准则进行确认。重分类日的新估值会成 为新准则下的初始成本。所以原准则先要把账面价值调整到新估值，才能终止确认。这个资产或者负 债账面价值的变动，根据五要素的定义，就会是收入或者费用，从而对损益可能产生影响。这是讨论 的基本。 答案解析： Reclassification adjustments are amounts recycled to profit or loss in the current period which were recognised in OCI in the current or previous periods. 重分类调整的定义先拿出来 On reclassification,these are presented as reversed through OCI in the same period as they are presented in profit or loss,so there is no overall effect on total comprehensive income. TCI=P/L+OCI 所 以OCI 减少的同时P/L 增 加 ，TCI 保持不变。 An example of items recognised in OCI which may be reclassified to profit or loss are foreign currency gains on the translation of a foreign operation and gains or losses on cash flow hedges.These are reclassified when the foreign operation is disposed of and when the hedge is realised. 重分类的举例，本题明确说了要举例子，所以要写一个。 Those items which may not be reclassified are changes in a revaluation surplus under IAS 16 Property,Plant and Equipment,and remeasurements of a defined benefit plan under IAS 19 Employee Benefits.Gains and losses on debt investments measured at fair value through OCI are reclassified to profit or loss on the disposal of the debt investment; similar amounts in respect of equity investments are not reclassified.The interaction between profit or loss and OCI is unclear to many users of financial statements,especially the notion of reclassification and when or which OCI items should be reclassified. 不可重分类的OCI 的举例 A common misunderstanding is that the distinction is based on realised versus unrealised gains.There are several arguments for and against reclassification: ·If reclassification ceased,there would be no need to define profit or loss,or any other total or subtotal in profit or loss,and any presentation decisions could be left to specific IFRS Standards. ·Reclassification protects the integrity of profit or loss and provides users with relevant information about a transaction which occurred in the period. ·Reclassification can improve comparability where IFRS Standards permit similar items to be recognised in either profit or loss or OCI. 重分类的好处讨论 Those against reclassification argue that the recycled amounts add to the complexity of financial reporting,may lead to earnings management and the reclassification adjustments may not meet the definitions of income or expense in the period as the change in the asset or liability may have occurred in a previous period. The lack of a consistent principle for determining howitems should be presented has led to an inconsistent use of OCI in IFRS Standards.Opinions vary but there is a feeling that OCI has become a home for anything controversial.Many users are thought to ignore OCI,as the changes reported are not caused by the operating flows used for predictive purposes. 重分类的坏处讨论 The IASB's Conceptual Framework for Financial Reporting has sought to clarify what distinguishes items of income and expense which are presented in profit or loss from those presented outside profit or loss (i.e.in OCI).Because the statement of profit or loss is the primary source of information about financial performance for the period,all income and expenses are,in principle,included in that statement. 讨论当期损益的重要性 The Board may,however,decide that in exceptional circumstances income or expenses arising from a change in the current value of an asset or liability(i.e.not based on historical cost)are to be included in OCI (to provide more relevant information or a more faithful representation of financial performance for the current period). 资产负债变动计入OCI 的特例还是存在。 In principle,income and expenses included in OCI in one period are reclassified from OCI to the statement of profit or loss in a future period when doing so results in more relevant information or provides a more faithful representation of financial performance for that future period. However,if there is no clear basis for identifying that future or the amount that should be reclassified,the Board may decide that income and expenses included in OCI are not to be subsequently reclassified. 从会计信息的基本质量特征出发，讨论是否重分类。

stem_zh: Q2 Jassie Co operates in the oil and gas industry.The current financial year end of Jassie Co is 31 December 20X7.The following exhibits, available on the left-hand side of the screen,provide information relevant to the question: 1.Granting of licences-describes the process of granting licences to customers. 2.Ethical issues -describes the potential ethical issues surrounding the working practices of the finance director. 1.Granting of licences Jassie Co provides services to customers to help them evaluate whether there are potential oil reserves in a particular geographical location.Customers generally enter into the contract before the survey process commences and eventually purchase a licence to search for oil deposits. 题干
explanation_zh: 许可证授予——描述向客户授予许可证的过程 道德问题——描述围绕财务总监工作实践的潜在道德问题 Jassie Co 为客户提供服务，帮助他们评估特定地理位置是否有潜在的石油储量。客户通常在 勘探过程开始前就签订合同，并最终购买石油勘探许可证。 Service phase During the service phase,Jassie Co collects site data over the first year of the contract,then processes and analyses the data over the next six months.The area surveyed is determined by Jassie Co who is the only decision-maker and the owner of both the data and the land being surveyed.All of the data is made available to customers in the final survey report.Customers cannot process the data or obtain a licence other than by Jassie Co's approval. The value to the customer is in the granting of the licence and not in the data. As data is being collected and processed,presentations and reports are given to customers to indicate the potential of an area.Customers have ongoing access to the data during collection and processing.When the final report is made available, the customer decides whether to invest in the area. 在服务阶段，Jassie 公司在合同的第一年收集现场数据，然后在接下来的六个月内处理和分 析数据。调查区域由Jassie 公司决定，他是唯一的决策者，同时也是数据和被调查土地的所 有者。所有的数据都在最终的调查报告中提供给客户。除Jassie Co的批准外，客户不能处 理数据或获得许可证。 对客户的价值在于许可的授予，而不是数据。 随着数据的收集和处理，将向客户提供演示文稿和报告，以表明该领域的是否有石油的可能 性。客户在收集和处理期间可以持续访问数据。当最终报告可用时，客户决定是否在该领域 投资。 Granting of licence The customers can exit the contract on receipt of the report but the total price of the contract,including the price of the final licence,is still payable to Jassie Co.In practice,no customer leaves the contract at this stage.The licence is granted at this stage and once the licence has been granted,there is no further analysis or service provided to the customer.The directors of Jassie Co concluded that the company has only one performance obligation,being the granting of a licence at the end of a survey. 客户可以在收到报告后退出合同，但合同的总价(包括最终许可证的价格)仍应支付给Jassie Co.。实际上，在此阶段没有客户退出合同。在此阶段授予许可证， 一旦授予许可证，就不 会向客户提供进一步的分析或服务。Jassie 公司的董事得出结论，该公司只有一项履行义务， 即在调查结束时授予许可证 This is because the collection and processing of the data(service phase)is a component of the granting of the licence and cannot be distinguished from the licence.In addition,the service phase alone is of no benefit on its own.The final licence provided to the customer is based upon the data collected and processed during the service phase. 这是因为数据的收集和处理(服务阶段)是授予许可证的一个组成部分，不能与许可证区分开 来。此外，服务阶段本身没有任何好处。提供给客户的最终许可是基于在服务阶段收集和处 理的数据。 2.Ethical issues Jassie Co uses Big Data analytics and various algorithms in order to indicate the potential of finding oil in a particular geographical location.The chief data officer understands the science and statistics,and can explain outcomes simply and clearly to customers.The finance director,Mr Betso,who is ACCA qualified,has little knowledge of the technology involved.He feels that the technology used is too complex and there is no need for him to understand it as long as the chief data officer understands it.The chief data officer reports to the finance director. Jassie Co 使用大数据分析和各种算法，以显示在特定地理位置发现石油的潜力。首席数据官 了解科学和统计数据，能够简单而清楚地向客户解释结果。财务总监Betso 拥有ACCA 资格 证，但他对相关技术知之甚少。他觉得所使用的技术太复杂了，只要首席数据官理解了，他 就没有必要理解。首席数据官向财务总监报告。 Recently,the outcome of one set of data showed that there was little chance of oil being found on one of the sites but the finance director insisted that the data was run again to include historical data which would then indicate a more favourable outcome for a customer,Olio Co. 最近，一组数据的结果显示，在其中一个地点发现石油的可能性很小，但财务总监坚持要再 次运行数据，以包括历史数据，这将表明对客户奥利奥公司(Olio Co.)更有利的结果。 Mr Betso has a friend,Ms Stylos,who works as the accountant at Olio Co.Ms Stylos has asked Mr Betso for advice as to how to account for the licence granted to Olio Co. Mr Betso suggested that Ms Stylos use IFRS 16 Leases as it is a right-to-use licence.In addition,Mr Betso knew that Ms Stylos held shares in Olio Co and suggested to her that the share price of Olio Co was likely to fall because there was little chance of oil being found as a result of the granting of the licence. Betso 先生有一位朋友Stylos 女士，她在Olio 公司担任会计师。Stylos 女士曾向Betso 先生请 教如何计量授予Olio公司的许可证。Betso先生建议Stylos 女士使用 IFRS 16租赁，因为这是 一项使用权许可证。此外，贝索先生知道斯蒂洛斯女士持有奥利奥公司的股份，并向她建议 奥利奥公司的股价可能会下跌，因为颁发许可证后发现石油的可能性很小。 Requirement解析： (a)Discuss: ·whether revenue from the service phase and the grant of a licence to a customer are separate performance obligations or a single performance obligation;and ·whether the revenue should be recognised at a point in time in accordance with IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers.(8 marks) 请讨论： ·从服务阶段和授予客户许可证获得的收入是属于不同的履约义务还是单一的履行义务； ●是否应根据国际财务报告准则第15号《与客户合同的收入》在某个时间点确认收入 答案解析： Under IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers,a performance obligation is a promise to transfer to the customer a good or service or a bundle of goods or services which is distinct.A good or service is distinct if both of the following criteria are met: the customer can benefit from the good or service either on its own or together with other resources which are readily available to the customer;and the entity's promise to transfer the good or service to the customer is separately identifiable from other promises in the contract(in other words:the promise to transfer the good or service is distinct within the context of the contract). 根据国际财务报告准则第15号《与客户合同的收入》,履约义务是向客户转让可区分的一 件商品或服务或一系列不同的商品或服务的承诺。一种商品或服务如果同时符合下列两个条 件，即为可区分的，不同的： 顾客可以从商品或服务中单独获益，也可以与顾客可随时获得的其他资源一起获益；并且实 体向客户转让货物或服务的承诺与合同中的其他承诺是分开的(换句话说：转让货物或服务 的承诺在合同条款中是可区分的)。 Entities should assess whether the good or service is capable of being distinct and whether this good or service is distinct within the context of the contract.If a promised good or service is not distinct,it should be combined with other promised goods or services until they become distinct together(a bundle).Such a bundle is then treated as a single performance obligation. 公司应评估货物或服务是否能够是可区分的，以及这种货物或服务在合同条款中是否是可区 分的。如果一种承诺的商品或服务不可取分，它应该与其他承诺的商品或服务结合在一起， 直到它们变得可区分(形成捆绑)。这样打包的服务商品被视为一个单一的履约义务。 A good or service promised to the customer is not separately identifiable from other promises in the contract when,in substance,the customer contracted for a combined good or service.In such cases,goods or services which seem to be distinct are in fact only inputs to the combined item. 当客户实际上为一种综合的商品或服务订立合同时，向客户承诺的商品或服务不能与合同中 的其他承诺分开。在这种情况下，似乎是不同的商品或服务实际上只是对组合项的输入。 The final licence provided to the customer relies on the data collected and processed during the course of the service phase,and the data and the processing have no use or relevance without the licence.The licence and data services are highly interdependent and highly interrelated.Furthermore,there is no added value for the customer before the survey is completed as it cannot process the data by itself and obtain a licence.The licence does not form part of a separate performance obligation. 提供给客户的最终许可证依赖于在服务阶段收集和处理的数据，如果没有许可证，数据和处 理就没有用途或相关性。许可证和数据服务是高度相互依赖和高度相互关联的。此外，在调 查完成之前，客户没有任何附加价值，因为他们不能自己处理数据并获得许可证。许可证不 构成单独履行义务的一部分。 Before the survey is complete,customers have access to data through presentations and reports but at this stage,there is no granting of a licence.Once the licence has been granted,there is no further analysis or service provided to the customer. 在调查完成之前，客户可以通过演示文稿和报告获取数据，但在此阶段，没有授予许可证。 一旦许可证被授予，就不会向客户提供进一步的分析或服务。 Consequently,revenue should be recognised at a point in time when the licence is granted (or when the contract is exited)and as a single performance obligation.Any service phase component does not have any significant value.The customers of Jassie Co contract for a combined good or service which includes the survey data, processing and granting of the licence.These are not separate performance obligations,but they are inputs to a combined item. 因此，收入应在授予许可证时(或合同终止时)确认，并作为单一的履约义务。任何服务阶段 组件都没有任何重要的价值。Jassie 公司的客户签订了一项综合商品或服务的合同，其中包
