括调查数据、处理和授予许可证。这些不是单独的执行义务，但它们是一个合并项目的输入。 Requirement解析： (b)Explain the ethical issues for the finance director,Mr Betso,setting out how the issues may be resolved.(10 marks) Professional marks will be awarded in part (b)for the quality of the discussion regarding the resolution of the ethical issues.(2 marks) 讨论一下财务总监的道德问题，并指出可以怎样解决 答案解析： (b)Big Data analytics and the use of algorithms are important to businesses. Accountants may not have the necessary skills or experience to take responsibility for Big Data and algorithms.Jassie Co has appointed a chief data officer to ensure clear accountability within the company.The finance director needs someone to reliably interpret the results in order that he can effectively deal with the customer.However, Mr Betso does need some understanding of the technology in order to determine company strategy and uphold company values and culture.The view is that accountability for the use of Big Data lies with senior management which includes Mr Betso. 大数据分析和算法对于业务很重要，会计也许没有大数据或者算法的必要技能或者经验，从 而无法对此负责。Jassie 公司认命了首席数据官去保证对公司的尽职负责。 财务总监需要有人对数据结果进行分析，从而对顾客有效负责。然而，Mr Betso 也需要一些 对技术的理解，从而可以对公司战略进行决策，并提升公司的价值和文化。公司恩威对大数 据负责的责任属于高级管理层，包含了 Mr Besto。 Even when correctly manipulated and processed,Big Data may not deliver the expected outcome.In this case,Mr Betso insisted that the data was run again to include historical data which would then result in a more favourable outcome for a customer.As the revised report used historic data,it may be embedded with bias from the past due to the way in which it was collected or acquired.If this is the case, it may be illegal or unethical to use this data for customer decision making.Mr Betso has obviously put the chief data officer under pressure to rerun the data to find a more favourable result.This would appear to be self-interest behaviour on the part of Mr Betso.Olio Co should feel confident that they are dealing with a company where the fair treatment of customers is central to the corporate culture. 即使数据被正确的处理和流转，大数据也许不能提供出预期的效果。在本题中，Mr Betso 坚 持要再运行一次这个数据，并包含了历史数据，从而达到对顾客有利的结果，但是如果包含 了以前的数据，那么过去数据的收集方法可能会对结果一些偏差，这样的做法对于顾客做决 策可能是或者是不道德的。Mr Betso的要求很明显对首席数据官施加了压力，体现出了self interest 的问题。Olio 公司的企业核心文化是对客户公平对待，他们可以对这个文化很自信。 Mr Betso should ask himself whether he has maintained the necessary skills and experience to carry out his role as finance director.Another example of his possible lack of professional competence is recommending to his friend that she use IFRS 16 Leases to account for the right-to-use licence to explore for oil.IFRS 16 specifically excludes licences of this nature from its scope.The main issue is that Mr Betso has given advice without first checking the nature of that advice.It demonstrates a lack of due care and poor professional behaviour which would discredit himself and possibly Jassie Co.Mr Betso has demonstrated a lack of expertise and knowledge in several areas,the result of which could impact on himself and the company.He has acted in a way which conflicts with ACCA's professional values. 另外一个点是Mr Betso 要考虑一下他是不是有足够的技能和经验来作为公司的财务总监， 是不是有专业竞争力。从他推荐他的朋友用 IFRS 16租赁准则来处理开采石油的执照可以看 出他可能缺乏专业性。 In addition,Mr Betso knows that Ms Stylos holds shares in Olio Co and has suggested to his friend that the share price of Olio Co was likely to fall because there was little chance of oil being found as a result of the granting of the licence. 另外，Mr Betso 知道他的朋友持有Olio 的股票，并和他的朋友透露股价可能下跌，因为 这里不太可能找到石油。 There are several ethical issues here.Mr Betso has manipulated data to produce a report for Olio Co which shows a more favourable outcome for locating oil than is necessarily the case.He has then disclosed confidential information about the report to his friend,Ms Stylos,which will affect the share price.Because this confidential information is not accessible to other shareholders and investors,Mr Betso is giving his friend an unfair advantage over the rest of the market which is ethically immoral since they affect others unfairly.However,the main issue is that the report was manipulated in the first place and a false report given to Olio Co. 总结：Mr Betso有几种道德问题存在。操纵数据，披露保密信息。最主要的问题是给 Olio Co 错误的报告。 Mr Betso should try to identify the parties affected by his actions and lack of expertise.He should consider who should be involved in the resolution of the problems which he has created.He may consider contacting ACCA for advice and guidance.He may approach colleagues with whom he can discuss his position and should consider involving the board of directors in its resolution. 提出解决方法/应对措施： Mr Betso 应该试着去找出因为他的不专业而受到影响的相关者。 他应该考虑对此提出一些解决措施.比如联系ACCA 官方寻求建议或者指引。他也可以找可 以就他的处境进行讨论的同事，并考虑寻求董事会的帮助。 Mr Betso should discuss his lack of knowledge with the directors of Jassie Co and the potential consequences to the business.If the directors of Jassie Co do not agree to a particular course of action,it may be appropriate to consider his position within the company. Mr Betso应该对 Jassie 公司的高层讨论他缺乏专业知识的事情，以及可能对公司带来的潜在 影响。如果Jassie 的管理层不同意他的举措。那么他在公司的职位可能需要重新考虑。 During the resolution process,he should document the substance of the discussions held,who was involved,and the reasons for the decisions which were made. 在解决问题期间，他应该记录下讨论的实质和相关决策的原因。

stem_zh: 3. 对 Ganic 公司的投资，与两个合伙人的投资关系， 一起共同成立了一家制造类 实 体。 1.Operating segments Eloa Co's equity instruments are listed.In the year ended 31 December 20X7,it operated four divisions:Tennisgear,Badmintonwear,Squashracket and Casualwear. Casual wear operates overseas and produces sports clothing.It sells 90%of its production to the other three divisions of Eloa Co.Badmintonwear and Squashracket sell the same clothing with different brand names. Eloa公司的股票已上市。截至20X7 年12月31日，它有4个运营部门：网球装 备、羽毛球装备、壁球装备和休闲服。 休闲服装部门在海外经营并生产运动服装。其产量的90%出售给了Eloa 公司的 其他三个部门，羽毛球服和壁球装备拍将相同的服饰贴牌不同的商标进行销售。 Eloa Co has stated that making the specific disclosures required by IFRS 8 Operating Segments might affect its competitive position and be misleading.Therefore,the company has currently elected not to disclose any segment information. Eloa 公司已经声明，根据 IFRS8 分部报告准则所要求的具体披露信息的要求，对 分部信息的披露可能会影响其竞争地位，并产生误导性。因此，目前公司已选择 不披露任何分部信息。 The company's chief executive officer(CEO)is the chief operating decision maker. Each division has a manager who reports directly to the CEO and their compensation is partly based upon the division's results. 该公司的首席执 行 官(CEO) 是首席运营部门决策者。每一个部门有一个经理， 他直接向首席执行官汇报，他们的薪酬部分依赖于该部门的经营结果。 Every quarter of the year,the CEO reviews the statement of profit or loss and the key performance measures such as earnings before interest,tax,depreciation and amortisation(EBITDA)for each division. 每一个季度，CEO 都会审查每个部门的损益表和关键业绩指标，如 EBITDA。 The division managers meet the CEO each quarter to review the division's performance and compare the actual results to the budgeted figures.The final budgets for each division are based upon performance and are approved by the CEO. 部门经理每季度与首席执行官会面，审查部门的业绩，并将实际结果与预算数字 进行比较。每个部门的最终预算都是基于前期业绩编制，并由首席执行官批准。 2.Divisional performance The table sets out the divisional performance for the year ended 31 December 20X7. Division Revenue Revenue Profit Assets External Internal Smillion Smillion Smillion Smillion Tennisgear 16 nil 2 15 Badmintonwear 1.6 nil 0.3 3 Squashracket 1.8 nil 0.5 4 Casualwear 1.2 10.8 3.6 20 Total 20.6 10.8 6.4 42 3.Investment in Ganic Co On 1 January 20X7,Eloa Co has undertaken a venture with two other venturers and called the entity Ganic Co.Ganic Co will manufacture tennis and badminton rackets and willsupply rackets to Tennisgear and Badmintonwear. 20X7 年 1 月 1 日 ，Eloa 公司与另外两家企业合资，成立了Ganic Co。Ganic Co将 生产网球和羽毛球球拍，并将作为供应商，将球拍销售到网球装备和羽毛球装备 分部。 Each venturer owns equity with one-third of the voting interests which also carries rights to the net assets.All key decisions which affect the venture are made by a unanimous vote of the shareholders,as outlined in an agreement between the venturers. 每个合资人都拥有三分之一 的投票权，同时也拥有分享 Ganic Co 净资产的权利。 如合资企业之间的协议中所述，所有影响合资企业的关键决定都是由股东一致投 票 ，无条件共同同意作出的。 However,Eloa Co has the option to purchase an additional 5%of Ganic Co's equity shares from each of the other two venturers for a fixed price at any time. 然而，Eloa 公司有权在任何时候以一个固定价格从其他两家合资企业手中再购买 Ganic 公司5%的股权。 Ganic Co's financial results are scrutinised quarterly by the CEO of Eloa Co and the other venturers.Eloa Co's CEO reviews the results to help make decisions about the allocation of future resources and to determine whether to continue with the venture. 每季度，GanicCo的财务业绩将由Eloa Co 的首席执行官和其他投资企业进行审查。 EloaCo 的首席执行官审查业绩结果，以帮助他们决定如何分配未来的资金和资源， 并决定是否继续投资。 Requirement
explanation_zh: (a)(i)Using exhibit 1 only,discuss,in accordance with IFRS 8 Operating Segments, whether the four divisions owned by Eloa Co should be classified as operating segments. (a)(i) 仅使用附件1,根据 IFRS 8分部报告，是否 Eloa Co 所拥有的四个部门是否 应被归类为经营部门。 (常规的对于 IFRS8 分部报告的经营分部判断的考点) (a)(ii)Using exhibits 1 and 2,determine,in accordance with IFRS 8,whether: ·the four divisions owned by Eloa Co should be identified as separate reportable segments;and ·whether it is necessary or possible for any of the divisions to be combined into a single reportable segment for Eloa Co.(9marks) (a)(ii) 使 用 案 例 1 和 2 , 根 据 IFRS 8分部报告，确定： -Eloa Co拥有的四个部门是否应被确定为单独的可报告经营部门；和 -是否有必要或可能将任何一个部门合并成一个单一的可报告部门。 (常规的对于IFRS8分部报告的报告分部判断的考点) (b)Discuss how the investment in Ganic Co should be accounted for in the consolidated financial statements of Eloa group and discuss whether Ganic Co can be classified as an operating segment.(7 marks) (b)讨论对Ganic Co 的投资如何计入 Eloa 集团的合并财务报表，并讨论Ganic Co 是否可以被归类为经营分部。 (常规的对于 IFRS11共同控制协议的判断和 IFRS8分部报告的经营分部判断的考 点) (c)Segmental reporting provides information about an entity's operations which enables users of financial reports to assess and make informed decisions on the true position and performance of an entity with diversified segments. Explain why segmental information is important to investors. (c) 对于一个企业分散化的各种分部，分部报告提供了关于其真实业绩表现和财 务状况的有关信息。该财务报告的用户可根据这些信息来评估并作出决策。 解释为什么分部信息对投资者很重要。 Note:You do not need to refer to any exhibit when answering part(c).(7 marks) 注意：在回答第(c)部分时，不需要参考任何附件 Professional marks will be awarded in part(c)for clarity and quality of the explanation of the importance of segmental information to investors.(2 marks) 答案解析： (a)(i) Operating segments Eloa Co is a listed entity and therefore is required to comply with IFRS 8 Operating Segments. 上市公司要严格按照国际财务报告准则编制财务报告 The company's view that the disclosure of segment information would be competitively harmful or misleading is difficult to understand as the IASB writes accounting standards on the basis that they provide transparent,useful information to investors. 该公司认为，部门信息的披露对竞争会有害或产生误导，这一点很难理解。因为 IASB制定会计准则的基础是向投资者提供透明、有用的信息。 Eloa Co should identify the information which would be useful to investors and how that information can be appropriately reported. Eloa 公司应确定对投资者有用的信息，以及如何适当地报告这些信息。 IFRS 8 states that an operating segment is a component of an entity: -which engages in business activities from which it may earn revenues and incur expenses; -whose operating results are regularly reviewed by the entity's chief operating decision maker;and -for which discrete financial information is available. 经营分部的定义，属于要背下来的准则内容 An operating segment includes components of an entity which sells primarily or exclusively to other operating segments. 经营分部包括一个实体主要或专门向其他经营分部销售的单元。 Information about the components engaged in each stage of production is particularly important for understanding vertically integrated entities in certain businesses,for example,Eloa Co.Therefore,the Casualwear division can be an operating segment even though it sells 90%of its production to the other divisions. 参与各个生产阶段的部门信息，对于理解某些业务中的垂直整合尤为重要，(因 为这会在集团内部构成一条产业供应链。垂直整合(Vertical Integration)是 一种 提高或降低公司对于其投入和产出分配控制水平的方法，也即公司对其生产投入、 产品或服务的分配的控制程度。)例如，Eloa Co。因此，休闲服装分部可以成为 一个经营分部(只要满足经营分部的条件),即使它将其90%的产品卖给了其他 部门。 Eloa Co appears to have four operating segments:Tennisgear,Badmintonwear, Squashracket,and Casualwear. 综合分析，4个分部都属于经营分部，原因如下. This is because each segment: -engages in business activities which recognise revenues and incur expenses as a profit or loss account is prepared quarterly; -从事的业务活动，在每季度都能确认收入和产生费用；(满足定义1) -has the CEO/chief operating decision maker reviewing their operating results quarterly to assess performance and allocate resources via the budgets;and -首席执行官/首席运营决策者是否每季度审查他们的运营业绩，以评估业绩并通 过预算分配资源。(满足定义2) -has discrete financial information available quarterly for each brand including additional performance measures such as EBITDA. -是否每季度为每个品牌提供独立的财务信息，包括额外的财务指标，如 EBITDA。 (满足定义3) 以上三个条件在附件1都能找到段落与之对应。 Each of the brand managers is directly accountable to and meets quarterly with the CODM to discuss performance and compare the actual results to the budgeted figures. 每个品牌经理都要直接向CODM 负责，并每季度与 CODM 会面，讨论业绩，并 将实际结果与预算数字进行比较。 Since Casual wear is an overseas entity,it is likely that separate disclosure is necessary so that users can better assess its performance and its significance to the group. 由于休闲服装是一个海外实体，很可能有必要单独披露，以便用户能够更好地评 估其表现及其对集团的重要性。 点评：常规的IFRS8 分部报告经营分部判断的题目，抓住准则规定的定义，结合 案例进行解析即可。 (ii)Separate reportable segments IFRS 8 requires an entity to report financial and descriptive information about its reportable segments.Reportable segments are operating segments or aggregations of operating segments which meet any of the specified criteria: -its reported revenue,from both external customers and intersegment sales or transfers,is 10%or more of the combined revenue,internal and external,of all operating segments,or -the absolute measure of its reported profit or loss is 10%or more of the greater,in absolute amount,of (i)the combined reported profit of all operating segments which did not report a loss and (ii)the combined reported loss of all operating segments which reported a loss,or- its assets are 10%or more of the combined assets of all operating segments. 报告分部的定义，属于要背下来的准则内容 注 意a(i)的定义是经营分部，这里的定义是报告分部。两者不一样，都要掌握。 Two or more operating segments may be aggregated into a single operating segment if aggregation is consistent with the core principles of the standard,the segments have similar economic characteristics and are similar in various prescribed respects. 如果将分部合并报告，能够符合准则相关的核心原则，可以将两个或两个以上的 经营分部合并为一个运营分部，条件是这些部门具有相似的经济特征，并且在不 同规定的方面也相似。 If the total external revenue reported by operating segments constitutes less than 75%of the entity's revenue,additional operating segments must be identified as reportable segments(even if they do not meet the quantitative thresholds set out above)until at least 75%of the entity's revenue is included in reportable segments. 如果经营分部报告的外部收入总额不占实体收入的75%,则必须将其他经营部门确定为 可报告部门(即使它们不符合上述规定的定量标准),直到实体至少75%的总收入包含 在可报告部门中。(这是准则内对于可报告经营分部的要求，也是必背准则内容。) Division Tennisgear Badmintonwear Squashracket Casualwear Total 10%= Revenue External $million 16 1 ·6 1 ·8 Internal $million nil nil nil 1-2 10 ·8 20 ·6 10 ·8 (20 ·6+10 ·8)x 10%=3 ·14 Profit $million 2 0 ·3 0 ·5 3 ·6 6 ·4 0 ·64 Assets $million 15 3 4 20 42 4 ·2 Meet 10% threshold individually √ X X √ Meet 10%in aggregate √ (in aggregate 3 ·4m revenue, 0 ·8m profits and 7m assets) 计算各个分部的收入，成本，资产占比，确定是否满足可报告分部的标准。 Tennisgear and Casualwear are separately reportable since they meet all three size criteria-although only need to meet one of the thresholds to be reportable. Badmintonwear and Squashracket do not meet any of the quantitative criteria and, on the face of it,are not separately reportable.The external revenue of Tennisgear is 77·7%(16/20·6)of the total external revenue so the '75%threshold'is achieved by Tennisgear alone. 根据计算结果，判断4个经营分部是否是可报告的经营分部。 T 以 及 C 三个判定 标准全部满足，可以直接确认为单独的报告分部。 B 以 及S 并不满足三个判定条 件的任意一个，对于外部收入75%的法则，也只有T 一个分部满足。 However,Badmintonwear and Squashracket are likely to share similar economic characteristics because they sell the same clothing with different brand names.If aggregated,they would exceed the 10%threshold for revenue,profit and net assets and so could be reported as a combined segment. 但是，B 和 S 可能具有相似的经济特征，因为它们销售贴有不同品牌但类型相同 的服装。如果合并，它们将超过收入、利润和净资产的10%的门槛，因此可以作 为一个合并的部门报告。 In addition,IFRS 8 gives entities discretion to report information regarding segments which do not meet the size criteria.Entities can report on such segments if,in the opinion of management,information about the segment would be useful to users of the financial statements. 此外，如果分部不满足准则规定的单独报告标准，IFRS 8 允许实体自行决定是否 披露这些不满足标准的经营分部的信息。如果管理层认为有关该分部的信息对财 务报表的使用者有用，那么实体就可以报告这些分部。 (b)Investment in Ganic Co IFRS 11 Joint Arrangements states that a joint arrangement is an arrangement where two or more parties share joint control.Joint control exists when decisions about the relevant activities require the unanimous consent of the parties sharing control. 共同控制的定义，最关键的是1,要能联合控制；2,有潜在共同控制权的任何 一方都有一票否决权，因此必须有潜在共同控制权的所有方都无条件表示同意 (unanimous consent) 才能做决策。 Even though the call option would give an additional 10%voting interest to Eloa Co, Ganic Co's governance still requires the unanimous vote of all investors to make key decisions,as outlined in an agreement between the venturers.Therefore,the investment would be under joint control regardless of the substance of the call option,which would change only the economic ownership percentage rather than the governance to control. 尽管看涨期权将给 Eloa Co额外10%的投票权，但 GanicCo 的治理仍需要所有投 资者的一致投票(unanimous vote)才能做出关键决定，正如共同控制协议所述。 因此，无论看涨期权的实质内容如何，投资都将处于共同控制之下，看涨期权的 行权与否，只会改变共同控制企业产生的经济利益的所有权，在不同投资方之间 所占有的百分比，而不是改变公司治理相关的控制权。 A joint venture is a joint arrangement whereby the parties who have joint control of the arrangement have rights to the net assets of the arrangement. JV的定义 This normally involves the establishment of a separate entity. 共同控制企业一定要有分离的企业实体，这是与共同控制项目最大的区别。 Therefore,Eloa Co should recognise its interest in Ganic Co as a joint venture.In accordance with IAS 28 Investments in Associates and Joint Ventures,it should account for this investment in the consolidated financial statements using the equity method. 结论：是JV, 因此需要按照权益法在合并报表进行账务处理。 Operating segment In this instance,the joint venture,Ganic Co,could represent an operating segment as it engages in business activities(racket production)from which it recognises revenues and incurs expenses and the CODM regularly reviews the joint venture's operating results to allocate resources and assess performance.Also,discrete financial information is available for the venture.A joint venture can be considered an operating segment even though the investor only has joint control over the performance of the investee. 在这种情况下，共同控制企业Ganic Co 可以代表一个经营分部，因为它从事业务 活动(球拍生产),并从中确认收入和产生费用。同时 CODM 定期审查共同控 制企业的经营结果，以分配资源和评估业绩。此外，该共同控制企业还可以获得 独立的财务信息。 一个共同控制企业可以被视为一个经营分部，即使投资者只能共同控制被投资方 的业绩。 The operating results should be regularly reviewed by the CODM to contribute to decisions about resources to be allocated to the segment and assess its performance. CODM 应定期审查经营结果，以帮助决定应分配给该部门的资源，并评估其业绩。 Management may regularly review the operating results and performance of an equity method investee for purposes of evaluating whether to retain the relationship. 管理层可定期审查被投资公司的经营业绩，以评估是否维持该投资关系。 However,management does not need to be responsible for making decisions about resources to be allocated or which affect its operations and performance.Therefore, control over the joint venture is not a criterion for it to be considered an operating segment. 但是，管理层不需要对要分配的资源或影响其业务和业绩的资源作出决策。因此， 对共同控制企业的控制并不是使其被视为一个经营分部的标准。 (c)Segmental information and investors According to the Conceptual Framework,the objective of financial reporting is to provide information which is useful to existing and potential investors when making decisions about to providing resources to the reporting entity.When making decisions about buying,selling or holding equity investments,investors require information about the amount,timing and uncertainty of future cash inflows to the reporting entity. 根据概念框架，财务报告的目标是在决定向报告实体提供资源时，提供对现有和 潜在投资者有用的信息。在决定购买、出售或持有股权投资时，投资者需要关于 报告实体未来现金流入的金额、时间和不确定性的信息。 Investors are interested in as much detail as possible on the discrete business sections of an entity so that they can see the return on those segments relative to their investment. 投资者对公司独立业务部门的尽可能多的细节感兴趣，因为这样他们就可以看到 这些部门相对于投资的回报。(比如：如果你要买腾讯的股票，那你应该对腾讯 不同的业务部门的细节感兴趣) Entities which operate in many countries,for example,will be exposed to varying risks and growth rates.It may be that some areas of operation are in decline,and this will impact investors'assessments of future net cash flows. 例如，在许多国家开展业务的公司将会面临不同的风险和增长率。这可能是由于 某些业务领域正在衰退，这将影响投资者对未来净现金流的评估。 Segmental reporting therefore gives more insight into the organisation's potential long-term performance.Segmental information is derived from the financial statements,but there is a degree of subjectivity in how an entity may apply IFRS 8. 因此，分部报告可以让我们更深入地了解该企业的长期潜力。分部信息来源于财 务报表，但公司如何应用IFRS 8,其实存在一定程度的主观性。 The information can differ from that reported in the financial statements.Therefore, from an investor's viewpoint,the nature and amount of the reconciliation to the financial statements are useful pieces of information. 这些信息可能与财务报表中报告的信息有所不同。因此，从投资者的角度来看， 将分部报告的信息与财务报表的信息进行对账和性质确认是有用的。 The usefulness of the reconciliation will depend on the extent and magnitude of changes to the financial statement amounts.The reconciliations can,however,be complex and difficult to understand. 对账调节的有用性将取决于财务报表数额变化的程度和幅度。然而，这种核对过 程通常很复杂，难以理解。 Segmental information is more useful if based on information used by management when making decisions.This allows investors to see the business through the eyes of management. 如果基于管理层在做决策时使用的信息，分部信息会更有用。这让投资者可以通 过管理层的眼光来看待业务。 The information disclosed should enable investors to evaluate the nature and financial effects of the business activities in which it engages and the economic environments in which it operates.The use of the management approach should have a positive effect on the quality of the segment information,from the perspective of usefulness and relevance. 所披露的信息应使投资者能够评估其所从事的商业活动的性质和财务影响，以及 其经营的经济环境。从实用性和相关性的角度来看，使用管理方法应该对部分信 息的质量产生积极的影响。(管理越有效，是否使用很优化的管理流程，数据收 集方法；分部报告的信息才会更加精确和可靠) Companies with clear and comprehensive segmental disclosure notes have an opportunity to differentiate themselves from their competitors. 拥有清晰和全面的分部信息披露记录的公司有机会与竞争对手相别，更加的与众不同。 模 考 卷 1Q1---适用于2023年9月SBR 考 试 ： Chuckle Co has equity interests in several companies including Grin Co.The directors of Chuckle Co have prepared an incorrect spreadsheet of the consolidated statement of financial position at 1 April 20X6(exhibit 4).All goodwill other than that relating to Grin Co has been impaired.The financial year end is 31 March each year. C 公司持有好几家公司的股份，其中包括G 公 司 。C 公司的高管编了合并资产负债表但是编 错了。处于G 收购的商誉之外的其他子公司的商誉全部都减值了。年底是3月31号。 1.Initial acquisition of Grin Co On 1 April 20X4,Chuckle Co acquired 30%of the equity shares of Grin Co.The consideration consisted of $100 million cash.The carrying amount of the net assets of Grin Co on 1 April 20X4 was $286 million which was the same as the fair value.Since then,Grin Co has been stated at cost in the consolidated financial statements of Chuckle Co. 在 2 0x4 年 4 月 1 号 ，C 公司购买了G 公司30%的股份，购买对价是$100 million 的现金。在 购买日当天G 公司净资产账面价值和公允价值均是$286 million。从 那 以 后，C 公司把对 G 公司的投资在合并报表上是按照成本法做账的。这边公司的做法其实是错误的。因为30% 的持股比例， 一般认为能够实现重大影响，故而对 G 公司的投资属于对联营公司的投资。 对联营公司的投资在合并报表上应该是按照权益法做账，而不是成本法。 The remaining 70%of the equity of Grin Co at 1 April 20X4 was owned by a few other investors, none of which owned more than 10%of the equity of Grin Co.Analysis shows that all shareholders have voted independently in the past.There has been no clear past voting pattern suggesting that Chuckle Co is unable to directly influence the economic decisions of the other investors.Chuckle Co and Grin Co share some key management personnel. 在 2 0x4 年 4 月 1 号 ，G 公司剩余的70%的股份被少数几个投资者所持有，没有一个股东持 有的股份超过10%。分析显示在过去所有的股东都是独立投票。过去没有明确的投票模式表 明 ，C 公司不能直接影响其他投资者的经济决策。 C 公 司 和G 公司共用一 些高管。 The carrying amount of the net assets of Grin Co on 31 March 20X6 was $348 million.The increase in the net assets of Grin Co since acquisition was solely due to profits.Grin Co has paid no dividends since 1 April 20X4. 20x6 年 3 月 3 1 号 ，G 公司净资产的账面价值是$348 million,且净资产的账面价值的增加全 部是由于G 公司的利润，从20×4年4月1号开始G 公司没有给股东们支付任何股息。从这 里可以看出在购买日当天 G 公司净资产账面价值和公允价值均是$286 million,今 年 年 底 G 公司净资产的账面价值是$348 million, 所以从 C 公司购买了G 公司的股份之后，G 公司的 净资产增加了$62million。C公司持股比例为30%,那么截止到今年年底对G 公司的投资在 合并报表上的账面价值应该是等于初始投资成本$100 million+成为联营公司之后净资产的变 动额*投资方持股比例，即+$62million*30%=$118.6million。 2.Subsequent acquisition of Grin Co Chuckle Co acquired a further 18%of Grin Co's equity on 1 April 20X6.The remaining 52%of the equity of Grin Co at 1 April 20X6 is owned by a few other investors,none of which own more than 10%of the equity of Grin Co.On 1 Chuckle Co would increase its ownership to 60%should it exercise its rights.It is believed that there would be additional cost savings should the additional shares be acquired as decisions at board level could be made more efficiently. 20x6 年 4 月 1 号 ，C 公司又购买了G 公司18%的股份，持股比例总共达到48%。 G 公司剩下 52%的股份也是掌握在少数投资者手中，没有一个股东所持股份超过10%。20x6 年 4 月 1 号 ， C 公司也购买了G 公司的一些股份期权，其行权期限持续到20x7 年3月31号。20x6 年 4 月1号，这些股份期权的行权价格稍微高于G 公司的股票价格。 G 公司这些年一直都是盈利 的，而且预期未来股票价格呈上涨趋势。如果 C 公司行权的话，那么C 公司对G 公司的持 股比例将会增加到60%。我们认为如果行权得到额外股份之后还可以进一步节约成本，因为 在董事会层面上做决策会更有效率。 3.Fair value of net assets of Grin Co The directors of Chuckle Co have prepared a spreadsheet of the consolidated statement of financial position(SOFP)as at 1 April 20X6(Exhibit 4).The investments in Grin Co have been stated at cost($100 million and $66 million)and the net assets of Grin Co have been included at their carrying amounts.The equity of Grin Co has also been included as a balancing figure. Goodwill has not been calculated. C 公司的高管在20x6 年4月1号编制了合并资产负债表，对G 公司的投资在合并资产负债 表上的金额为$100 million and $66 million 的原始成本，其中$100 million 是原先购买30%的股 份时的购买对价，$66 million 是后续额外购买18%的股份时的购买对价。这里其实做法也是 错误的，因为从30%的持股比例变成48%的持股比例外加股份期权之后，其本质是属于对G 公司的投资从对联营公司的投资变成了对子公司的投资，该笔交易在合并报表上的账务处理 要按照交易的经济实质来做账，即相当于是把原先的联营公司处置掉了，然后买入了一个子 公司。需要计算商誉，计算处置联营公司的利得或损失，需要将原本持股比例30%的账面价 值调整至公允价值等等。 G 公司的净资产也是包含在合并资产负债表当中，这一做法也是错 误的，因为子公司的净资产是会和母公司的收购对价之间相互抵消的，最终以商誉的形式呈 现在合并资产负债表当中。公司没有计算商誉，那么我们需要在解题的过程中去计算商誉。 The consideration for the further 18%of the equity shares of Grin Co on 1 April 20X6 is included in the SOFP at the cash amount of $66 million.The fair value of the original 30%equity interest was $127 million at 1April 20X6. 20×6年4月1号，C 公司又购买了 G 公司18%的股份，购买对价是$66 million, 这一天，原 本所持有的G 公司的30%的股份的公允价值是$127 million。从联营公司变成子公司，需要 将原本所持有的股份的金额从账面价值调整至公允价值。 The carrying amounts of the net assets of Grin Co on 1 April 20X6 were as follows: 20x6年4月1号，G公司的净资产的账面价值明细如下： $`millions Non-current asset 355 Current assets 214 Deferred tax liability (16) Current liabilities (205) Total 348 Included within the non-current assets is some land.The carrying amount of this land at 1 April 20X6 is $50 million but its fair value is assessed to be $60 million at this date.Current assets include finished goods with a cost of $84 million.The fair value of these goods is $131 million. 20x6 年 4 月 1 号 ，G 公司的长期资产账面价值为$355 million, 这些长期资产当中包括一项 土地，其账面价值是$50 million, 其公允价值在当天是$60 million, 这说明在计算商誉的时候 需要针对于土地进行公允价值的调增。流动资产的账面价值为$214 million, 其中包括一些产 成品，其原始成本为$84 million, 公允价值为$131million, 这说明在计算商誉的时候也需要 针对于存货进行公允价值调增。 On 1 April 20X6,the directors of Chuckle Co also identified that Grin Co had an internally generated database of customers who were likely to be interested in purchasing their products. Although there were no contractual or legal rights associated with this database,a professional expert has estimated that competitors of Grin Co would be prepared to pay $5 million for this database.Grin Co has not recognised the database as an asset within the individual financial statements. 在20×6年4月1号，C 公司的高管识别出G 公司有一项内部生产的客户数据，这些客户可 能对G 公司的产品感兴趣。尽管这些G 公司对这些数据不同在法律上的权利或者合同的权 利，但是一个外部专家估计G 公司的竞争对手愿意花$5 million 来购买该客户数据。 G 公司 没有将这项客户数据作为无形资产确认在自己的报表当中。从这里可以得知该客户数据即无 形资产的账面价值为0,但是其公允价值为$5 million,这说明在计算商誉的时候也需要针对 于该无形资产进行公允价值调增。 The current rate of tax is 20%.This rate should be applied to any fair value adjustments deemed necessary. 当前适用的税率为20%,这些税率适用于任何的公允价值调整。 Chuckle Co measures the non-controlling i proportionate share of the net assets at the date of acquisition. C 公司用的是proportionate method 去对 G 公司的少数股东权益进行核算。 4.Draft consolidated SOFP Assets Non-current Assets Property,Plant and equipment Investment in Associate Further investment in associate Goodwill Other non current assets Current Assets Total Assets Equity Sm 2,021 100 66 186 2,373 542 2.915 Equity attributable to equity holders of the company Share capital Other components of equity Retained earnings Total Equity Non Controlling Interest Grin's equity Liabilities Non Current Liabilities Deferred income tax liabilities Current Liabilities Total Liabilities Total equity and Liabilities 150 370 1,157 1,677 348 314 662 576 1,238 2,915 Requirements: (a)(i)Using exhibit 1 only,discuss why Grin Co should be classified as an associate,and not a subsidiary,on 1 April 20X4.(4 marks) 考查：根据案例1讨论在20×4年4月1号，G 公司的投资为什么属于对联营公司的投资而 不是对子公司的投资?这里就是要结合子公司和联营公司的定义去进行分析。 (a)(ii)Using exhibit 1 only,explain how Grin Co should be accounted for as an associate using the equity method in the consolidated statement of financial position of Chuckle Co at 31 March 20X6.(3 marks) 考查：根据案例1解释在20x6年 3 月 3 1 号 ， 对G 公司的投资即对联营公司的投资应该如 何按照权益法在合并资产负债表上去进行账务处理。考查的是对权益法的理解和运用。 (b)Using exhibit 2,explain briefly why the classification of Chuckle Co's investment in Grin Co should change on 1 April 20X6 to that of a subsidiary.(3 marks) 考查：根据案例2,简单解释在20×6年4月1号，为什么对G 公司的投资应该从对联营公 司的投资变成对子公司的投资?这里考查的是分步收购。 (c)(i)Using exhibit 2,explain briefly how the additional purchase of the 18%equity in Grin Co should be accounted for in the consolidated financial statements of Chuckle Co.(2 marks) 考查：根据案例2,简单解释额外购买的G 公司18%的股份应该如何在合并报表上进行账务 处理? (c)(ii)Using exhibit 3,explain how the fair value of the non-current and current assets of Grin Co at 1 April 20X6(including any deferred tax adjustments)should be calculated.(6 marks) 考查：根据案例3,解释在20x6 年 4 月 1 号 ，G 公司的长期资产和流动资产的公允价值应 该如何计算，包括其对于递延所得税的影响。 (c)(iii)Using the pre-populated spreadsheet response option with exhibit 3 and your previous answers,adjust the spreadsheet prepared by the directors of Chuckle Co inorder to prepare a corrected consolidated statement of financial position at 1 April 20X6.The spreadsheet should take into account the following: ● accounting for the associate using the equity method; ● the change in classification from associate to subsidiary; ● the fair value adjustments on acquisition;and ● goodwill using the proportionate share of net assets method to calculate the non-controlling interest.(12 marks) 考查：根据题目已知的错误的合并资产负债表和前面已知的案例，编制出20x6 年 4 月 1 号 正确的合并资产负债表。 (30 marks) Suggested answer: (a)(i)Significant influence is the ability to participate in the financial and operating policy decisions of the investee but is not control or joint control over these policies. 这 一段在写 IAS28 号准则中规定的重大影响的定义，建议背诵。 IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements states that an investor controls an investee only if the investor has all of the following: ● power over the investee; ● exposure to or rights to variable returns from its involvement with the investee;and ● the ability to use its power over the investee to affect the amount of the investor's returns. 这一段是在写IFRS10 号准则当中规定的控制的定义，建议背诵。 为什么答案一开始就要写这两个定义呢，因为题目问的是对G 公司的投资是属于联营公司 答世来联营公司和子公司的定义。 Control is presumed to exist where the investor has a majority of the voting rights of the investee. This would usually give the investor the ability to direct the relevant activities,i.e.the activities which significantly affect the investee's returns.An ownership of 50%or less of the voting rights does not necessarily preclude an investor from obtaining control. 一般情况下，如果投资方持有被投资方超过50%的持股比例，那么就认为投资方能够对被投 资方实施控制。在控制的情况下，投资方一般能够主导被投资方的相关的活动，什么是相关 的活动呢?是指那些对被投资方的回报产生重大影响的活动。但是如果持股比例没有超过 50%,也并不必然代表投资方对被投资方不能实施控制。这里也是关于控制的相关理论，建 议 背 诵。 Chuckle Co only owned 30%of the equity.Where an investor has a significant minority,close consideration should be given as to whether the voting rights alone or whether a combination of factors is deemed sufficient to obtain power.Chuckle Co and Grin Co do share some key management personnel which can sometimes be evidence of control.However,there has been no clear past voting pattern suggesting that Chuckle Co is unable to directly influence the economic decisions of the other investors.None of the other investors owns more than 10%and they have voted independently which also suggests Chuckle Co cannot influence the other investors to the extent it would give them control.With only 30%of the equity and no additional potential rights,it would appear that Chuckle Co was only able to exercise significant influence rather than control.It can be concluded that it was correct to classify Grin Co as an associate. C 公司持有G 公司30%的股份。虽然持股比例没有超过50%,但是其他的股东所持有的股份 都不超过10%,所以相比较而言， C 公司算是持股比例比较多了，算是重要的小股东。这种 情况下，我们需要考虑是不是存在其他的因素一起导致C 公司能够实现控制。C 公司和G 公 司现在共享一些高管人员，这有的时候也可能会成为实现控制的证据。譬如有的高管在母公 司担任执行董事，在子公司担任非执行董事。然而，题目中已知过去没有明确的投票模式表 明 ，C 公司不能直接影响其他投资者的经济决策，其他的股东的持股比例都不超过10%,而 且他们都是独立投票，这些迹象都表明C 公司不能实现控制。因为只持有30%的持股比例， 而且没有其他的潜在投票权，这表明C 公司只能够对 G 公司实现重大影响而不是控制，因 此 G 公司属于C 公司的联营公司。 (a)(ii)Grin Co is an associate and should have been equity accounted for in the consolidated financial statements of Chuckle Co in prior years.Therefore,as Grin Co has not been accounted for as an associate,a prior period adjustment should occur.The general principle in IAS 8 Accounting Policies,Changes in Accounting Estimates and Errors is that an entity must correct all material prior period errors retrospectively in the first set of financial statements authorised for issue after their discovery by restating the comparative amounts for the prior period(s)presented in which the error occurred.This will mean that the financial statements for the year ended 31 March 20X6 will be adjusted. G 公司是属于联营公司，在合并报表当中应该按照权益法进行账务处理。题目中是按照成本 法入账的，所以账务处理做错了，那么应该进行错误的更正，这属于IAS8号准则的范畴， 前期差错的更正按照追溯调整法进行账务处理。 The initial investment is measured at cost and the carrying amount should be increased to recognise the investors'share of the profits and other comprehensive income after the date of acquisition.In the consolidated statement of financial position,one line should be included within non-current assets as investment in associate. 权益法下对联营公司的投资初始入账应该按照成本核算，成为联营公司之后所产生的利润或 者其他综合收益中归属于投资者的比例需要反映在合并报表当中。在合并资产负债表当中应 该是在长期资产当中列示一项科目，即 investment in associate。 At 31 March 20X6,the investment in associate should be valued at $118.6 million being the initial cost of $100 million already recorded plus 30%of the increase in net assets since the acquisition date($100m+(30%x($348m -$286m))The increase of $18.6 million should also be included within consolidated retained earnings. 在20×6年3月31日，对联营公司的投资在合并资产负债表当中的账面价值是$118.6 million, 其中包括$100 million 的原始成本加上成为联营公司之后净资产的变动额$348m-$286m 的 基础上乘上 C 公司的持股比例30%,即$18.6 million。分录为：Dr investment in associate $18.6 million Cr Group R/E $18.6 million。 (b)The acquisition of the extra 18%of the equity on 1 April 20X6 would now unquestionably make Chuckle Co a significant minority investor.No other investor owns more than 10%of the equity so Chuckle Co owns a much higher proportional share (48%).Where the other shareholdings are owned by a large number of unconnected,dispersed holders,it would be clear that power has been obtained. 在20×6年4月1号，C 公司额外购买了G 公司18%的股份，在这一天总共的持股比例是48%。 那么剩下还有52%的持股比例是在别人手中，根据准则理论，如果剩下52%的持股比例都是 在大量的股东手中，且这些股东之间毫无联系，且极其分散，那么这种情况下，C 公司就获 得了控制权。 However,the other shares are owned by just a few other investors which is unlikely to be considered a large,dispersed group of unconnected shareholders. 然而，根据题目可以值得，剩下的52%的股份不是在大量的分散的无关联的股东手中。剩下 的股份只是持有在少数几个股东手中，所以不满足上面所说的那个理论。 Potential voting rights should be considered in the assessment of control.For these to be included in the assessment,the rights should be substantive.That would usually mean that they are currently exercisable and have an exercise price which is below the market price of the shares so that they are"in the money".In that sense it is worthwhile for the investor to acquire the extra shares.In the case of Chuckle Co,they own share options that are currently exercisable but not in the money.This is because the exercise price is above the share price of Grin Co.However, they are only just out of the money.In addition,the share price of Grin Co is expected to increase and cost savings are expected from a further acquisition of shares.It seems therefore that the share options would be deemed to be substantive.Since exercising these options would enable Chuckle Co to obtain a 60%shareholding,it can be concluded that Chuckle Co is able to exercise power over Grin Co from 1 April 20X6.Grin Co should be reclassified from an associate to a subsidiary at this date. 在判断是不是控制的时候要考青虑思潜在的实实质性投票权，,什么叫做实质性的呢，第一要求这个 权利是当前就可以行使的，第二要求行权的价格低于股票市场价格。在本案例中， C 公司持 有 G 公司的一些股份期权，这些期权现在是可以行权的，但是行权价格稍稍高于股价的市 价。虽然一般情况下，行权价格高于股票价格，那么一般不会行权。但是在本案例中，行权 价格只是稍稍高于股价的市价，而且行权之后还有其他的好处产生。比如G 公司的股价预 期还会增长，而且还可以节约成本。这些表明该潜在投票权也还是具有实质的。因为一旦行 权之后C 公司对 G 公司的持股比例会上涨到60%。这样子就实现了控制，那么对G 公司的 投资就从联营公司变成了子公司。 (c)(i)The additional purchase of the 18%equity would constitute a piecemeal or step acquisition. Goodwillwill be calculated as the amount by which the fair value of the consideration exceeds the fair value of the identifiable net assets on acquisition.Chuckle Co must therefore remeasure its previously held equity interest in Grin Co at its acquisition fair value and recognise the resulting gain or loss in the consolidated statement of profit or loss.Goodwill will be calculated by including both the fair value of the previously held equity interest and the fair value of the additional consideration. 额外购买18%的股份属于分步收购。因为从联营公司变成了子公司。实现控制的当天需要去 计算商誉，而且需要将原先所持有的G 公司的股份的价值从账面价值调整至控股日当天的 公允价值，这两者之间的差额属于利得或损失，要确认进合并利润表当中。 (c)(ii)It is necessary for the calculation of goodwill that Chuckle Co measures the identifiable assets acquired and the liabilities assumed at their acquisition date fair values.IFRS 13 Fair value Measurement should be considered in the assessment of the fair values.It has been identified that the fair value of the land is $10 million above carrying amount.The valuation should be representative of the amount which market participants would be willing to sell the asset or transfer the liability in an orderly transaction under current market conditions.The increase in value of $10 million will create an additional taxable temporary difference.In effect,the carrying amount of the land is increased by $10 million with no alteration to the tax base.An additional deferred tax liability arises at the acquisition date of $2 million.Since the deferred tax is an identifiable liability at the acquisition,it should be recognised on acquisition with a corresponding increase in net assets of $8 million ($10m-$2m). 在计算子公司的商誉时，需要减掉的是收购日当天子公司净资产的公允价值。这意味着子公 司单体报表上的资产负债都需要从其账面价值调整至公允价值，只要两者不一样，那么就会 出现公允价值的调增调减。根据题目可以得知，收购日当天，子公司的土地的公允价值高于 账面价值10million,所以需要进行公允价值的调增。公允价值调增之后账面价值变大，但是 税务局不认可，即计税基础还是调增之前的账面价值，所以账面价值大于计税基础，差额即 为应纳税暂时性差异，乘上税率，即为递延所得税负债10 million*20%=2,million, 对收购日 当天子公司的净资产的影响净额是10-2=8million。 Finished goods should be valued at their estimated sales price less the sum of the costs of disposal and a reasonable profit allowance for the selling effort of the acquiring entity.The fair value of the finished goods is $131 million and so a fair value adjustment of $47 million ($131m- $84m)is required.This creates a further taxable temporary difference in the consolidated financial statements of Chuckle Co with a corresponding deferred tax liability at 20%of $9.4 million. 产成品的账面价值是84m, 公允价值是131m, 那么也是需要进行公允价值的调增47m, 计 税基础还是不变，也就同样产生了应纳税暂时性差异，乘上税率20%。即47m*20%=9.4m 的递延所得税负债。对收购日当天子公司的净资产的影响净额是47-9.4=37.6m。 It is correct that the database as an internally generated intangible asset is not recognised in the individual financial statements of Grin Co.On acquisition,Chuckle Co should recognise the database as a separate intangible asset from goodwill in the consolidated financial statements providing that the database satisfies the criteria for recognition as an intangible asset and a reliable estimate of the fair value can be determined.Although there are no contractual or legal rights associated with the database,the database still appears to be identifiable as it could be sold separately to Grin Co's competitors.The professional expert's valuation of $5 million would appear to provide a reliable estimate of fair value.The database should therefore be recognised in the consolidated financial statements at $5 million with a further increase to the deferred tax liability at 20%equal to $1 million. 数据库作为无形资产，其账面价值为0,公允价值为5m, 也需要进行公允价值的调增，计 税基础也是公允价值调增之前的金额，即账面价值大于计税基础，会产生应纳税暂时性差异 5m, 乘上税率20%,即产生递延所得税负债1m, 对收购日当天子公司的净资产的影响净额 是5-1=4million。 Assets Sm W1 W2 W3 W4 W5 W6 Non-current Assets Property,Plant and equipment 2,021 10 2031 Investment in Associate 100 18.6 -118.6+127 -127 0 Further investment in associate 66 -66 0 Goodwill 2.2 2.2 Other non current assets 186 5 191 2,373 2224.2 Current Assets 542 47 589 Total Assets 2,915 2813.2 Equity Equity attributable to equity holders of the company Share capital 150 150 Other components of equity 370 370 Retained earnings 1,157 18.6 8.4 1184 Total Equity 1,677 1704 Non Controlling Interest 206.8 206.8 Grin's equity 348 8 37.6 4 -397.6 0 Liabilities Non Current Liabilities Deferred income tax liabilities 314 2 9.4 1 326.4 662 533.2 Current Liabilities 576 576 Total Liabilities 1,238 1109.2 Total equity and Liabilities 2,915 2813.2 Working 1: Dr Investment in Associate (348-286)x30%=18.6 Cr Group R/E 18.6 从20x4.4.1至20x6.3.31,C公司所持有的G公司30%的股份在合并报表上应该按照权益法 进行账务处理，而C公司只是按照成本法入账，估需要调整。 Working 2: Dr Investment in Subsidiary 127 Dr Group R/E(Gain on disposal) 8.4 Cr Investment in Associate 118.6 20x6.4.1号C公司额外购买G公司18%的持股比例，获得控制权，对G公司的投资从联营公 司变成子公司，需要将原先所持有的30%的股份的账面价值调整到公允价值，两者之间的差 额计入损益，进而影响留存收益。 Working 3: Dr Land 10 Cr DTL 10×20%=2 Cr Grin's equity 10-2=8 G公司变成子公司之后，G公司的所以资产负债在合并报表上均需要调整至公允价值。土地 公允价值的调增会使得固定资产价值增加，同时产生应纳税暂时性差异进而导致递延所得税 负债，差额使得子公司净资产增加。 Working 4: Dr Inventory 131-84=47 Cr DTL 47×20%=9.4 Cr Grin's equity 47-9.4=37.6 产成品公允价值的调增会使得存货价值增加，同时产生应纳税暂时性差异进而导致递延所得 税负债，差额使得子公司净资产增加。 Working 5: Dr The database 5 Cr DTL 5×20%=1 Cr Grin's equity 5-1=4 数据库公允价值的调增会使得无形资产价值增加，同时产生应纳税暂时性差异进而导致递延 所得税负债，差额使得子公司净资产增加。 Working 6: Dr Grin's equity 348+8+37.6+4=397.6 Dr Goodwill 2.2 Cr NCI 397.6×52%=206.8 Cr investment in S 127 Cr Further investment in associate 66 G公司变成子公司之后，需要计算商誉，同时确认少数股东权益。

stem_zh: Q2 题干分析： The accountant of Abby Co is concerned about the disclosure of trading that has occurred between Abby Co and a company that a director of Abby Co is associated with.This director is also attempting to influence how the accountant should account for two issues associated with the consolidated. 1.Related party transactions 关联方交易 The accountant has discovered that the finance director of Abby has purchased goods from a company,Arwight,which the director jointly owns with his wife and the accountant believes that this purchase should be disclosed.However,the director refuses to disclose the transaction as in his opinion it is an'arm's length'transaction.He feels that if the transaction is disclosed,it will be harmful to business and feels that the information asymmetry caused by such non-disclosure is irrelevant as most entities undertake related party transactions without disclosing them.Similarly, the director felt that competitive harm would occur if disclosure of operating segment profit or loss was made.As a result,the entity only disclosed a measure of total assets and total liabilities for each reportable segment. When preparing the financial statements for the recent year end,the accountant noticed that Arwight has not paid an invoice for several million dollars and it is significantly overdue for payment.It appears that the entity has liquidity problems and it is unlikely that Arwight will pay. The accountant believes that a loss allowance for trade receivables is required.The finance director has refused to make such an allowance and has told the accountant that the issue must not be discussed with anyone within the trade because of possible repercussions for the credit worthiness of Arwight. 会计师发现， Abby 的财务总监从 Arwight公司购买了商品，Arwight 公司是该董事与其妻子 共同拥有的公司，会计师认为该购买行为应予以披露。然而，董事拒绝披露该交易，因为他 认为这是一笔“公平交易”。他认为，如果交易被披露，将对业务有害，并认为这种不披露导 致的信息不对称是无关紧要的，因为大多数企业在进行关联方交易时都没有披露。同样的， 该董事认为，如果披露经营分部的利润或亏损，将造成竞争损害。因此，该实体仅披露了每 个可报告分部的总资产和总负债。 在编制最近一个年末的财务报表时，会计师注意到Arwight 没有支付数百万美元的发票，而 且已经严重逾期付款。该企业似乎存在流动性问题， Arwight 不太可能支付。会计师认为， 应收账款需要计提及减值准备。而财务总监拒绝这样做，并告诉会计师，由于可能会对 Arwight 的信用价值产生影响，因此不得与业内任何人讨论该问题。 分 析 ：关联方关系的披露，披露内容，经营分部的判定，披露的内容，以及隐瞒关联方关系 的原因，怀疑，道德威胁 2.Subsidiary fair value adjustments 子公司公允价值的调整 Additionally,when completing the consolidated financial statements,the director has suggested that there should be no positive fair value adjustments for a recently acquired subsidiary and has stated that the accountant's current position is dependent upon following these instructions.The fair value of the subsidiary is $50 million above the carrying amount in the financial records.The reason given for not fair valuing the subsidiary's net assets is that goodwill is an arbitrary calculation which is meaningless in the context of the performance evaluation of an entity 在完成合并财务报表时，董事建议对最近收购的子公司不进行正向公允价值调整，并声明会 计师的当前状况取决于以下指示。子公司的公允价值比财务记录中的账面价值高出50m。 对子公司净资产进行不公平估值的原因是， Goodwill 是一种任意计算，在企业绩效评估中毫 无意义。 分析：商誉的计算，资产金额低估， PPE估值技术，这样做的动机：对于报表以及财务指标 的影响 3.Goodwill impairment 商誉的减值 Finally,when preparing the annual impairment tests of goodwillarising on other subsidiaries,the director has suggested that the accountant is flexible in the assumptions used in calculating future expected cash flows,so that no impairment of goodwill arises and that the accountant should use a discount rate which reflects risks for which future cash flows have been adjusted.He has indicated that he will support a salary increase for the accountant if he follows his suggestions. 在准备 Goodwill 对其他子公司的年度减值测试时，董事建议会计在计算未来预期现金流量 时采用灵活的假设，以便Goodwill 不会发生减值，并且会计应使用反映未来现金流已调整 风险的折现率。他表示，如果听从他的建议，他将支持增加会计的工资。 分 析 ：商誉的减值，折现率的选择，对报表的影响，动机，道德威胁 Requirement
explanation_zh: Discuss the ethical and accounting implications of the above situations from the perspective of the reporting accountant.(18 marks) Professional marks will be awarded in question 2 for the application of ethical principles.(2 marks) (20 marks) 题目要求：从会计师的角度讨论上述情况对职业道德和会计记账的影响。(18分) 应用道德原则的专业分数。(2分)(20分) 解题思路： 首先问题的要求是从会计影响和职业道德的影响两个方面回答问题，所以三段话都要回答这 两个方面。同时在读题时就可以先找出不符合会计准则要求的做法，进行合理的分值分配。 关于会计做账问题，首先回答会计准则要求，再指出题目中不恰当的处理，接着站在会计师 的角度给出修改的建议，从题干分析来看， 一共有五个问题需要讨论，分别为：关联方交易 的披露，经营分部的报告披露，应收账款的减值，子公司公允价值的调整以及商誉的减值测 试。 关于职业道德的问题，首先指出题目职业道问题，再给出合理的建议去解决道德冲突。 答案解析： The objective of IAS 24 Related Party Disclosures is to ensure that an entity's financial statements contain the disclosures necessary to draw attention to the possibility that its financial position and profit or loss may have been affected by the existence of related parties and by transactions and outstanding balances with such parties.If there have been transactions between related parties,there should be disclosure of the nature of the related party relationship as well as information about the transactions and outstanding balances necessary for an understanding of the potential effect of the relationship on the financial statements. 【 准 则IAS 24 关联方交易披露的原因以及披露内容】 IAS 24要求财务报表包含必要的披露，以说明其财务状况和损益可能受到关联方的存在以及 与关联方的勒索和未偿余额的影响。如果关联方之间存在交易，则应披露关联方关系的性质 以及了解该关系对财务报表的潜在影响所需的交易和未清余额信息。 The director is a member of the key management personnel of the reporting entity and the entity from whom the goods were purchased is jointly controlled by that director.Therefore a related p arty relationship exists and should be disclosed. 结合题意，董事是企业的关键管理人员之一，采购的企业由该董事共同控制。因此，存在关 联方关系，应予以披露。 IFRS 8 Operating Segments requires an entity to report financial and descriptive information about its reportable segments.Reportable segments are operating segments or aggregations of operating segments which meet specified criteria.IFRS 8 does not contain a 'competitive harm' exemption and requires entities to disclose the financial information which is provided by the chief operating decision maker(CODM).The management accounts reviewed by the CODM may contain commercially sensitive information,and IFRS 8 might require that information to be disclosed externally.Under FRS 8,firms should provide financial segment disclosures which enable investors to assess the different sources of risk and income as management does.This sensitive information would also be available for competitors.The potential competitive harm may encourage firms to withhold segment information.However,this is contrary to IFRS 8 which requires information about the profit or loss for each reportable segment,including certain specified revenues and expenses such as revenue from external customers and from transactions with other segments,interest revenue and expense,depreciation and amortisation,income tax expense or income and material non-cash items. 【准则IAS 8 经营分部的定义以及准则要求的信息的披露】 IFRS 8经营分部要求报告分部的财务和描述性信息。可报告分部是指符合规定标准的经营分 部或经营分部的集合。IFRS8不 包含“竞争损害”豁免，并要求各披露由首席运营决策者(CODM) 提供的财务信息。 CODM审查的管理账户可能包含商业敏感信息，IFRS8 可能要求对外披露 该信息(确实以管理法编制的分布报告有利于报表使用使用者以以管理层的角度去看待的经 营情况，但也会披露企业的商业秘密，如企业的内部交易定价原则)。 公司应提供分部的财务信息，使投资者能够像管理层那样评估不同的风险和收入来源。竞争 对手也可以获得这些敏感信息。潜在的竞争危害可能会鼓励公司隐瞒分部信息。然而，这违 反了IFRS 8的相关规定，IFRS8 要求提供每个可报告分部的损益信息，包括特定的收入和费 用，如来自外部客户和与其他分部交易的收入、利息收入和费用、折旧和摊销、所得税费用 或收入和重大非现金项目。 Areas such as impairments of financial assets often involve the application of professional judgement.The director may have received additional information,which has allowed him to form a different opinion to that of the accountant.The matter should be discussed with the director to ascertain why no provision is required and to ask whether there is additional information available.However,suspicion is raised by the fact that the accountant has been told not to discuss the matter.Whilst there may be valid reasons for this,it appears again that the related party relationship is affecting the judgement of the director. 【不进行信息披露的原因】 金融资产减值等领域通常涉及专业判断，董事可能收到了额外的信息，这使得他形成了与会 计师不同的意见。这些事件应与会计师进行讨论，以确定原因并询问是否有其他可用信息。 然而，会计师被告知不要讨论此事，这很值得怀疑，即使可能有合理的理由，但关联方关系 是影响了董事的判断。 Positive fair value adjustments increase the assets of the acquired company and as such reduce the goodwill recognised on consolidation.However,the majority of positive fair value adjustments usually relate to items of property,plant and equipment.As a result,extra depreciation based on the net fair value adjustment reduces the post-acquisition profits of the subsidiary.This has a negative impact on important financial performance measures such as EPS. 公允价值向上调整增加了被收购公司的资产，因此减少了合并时确认的商誉。然而，大多数 公允价值调整(公允价值大于账面价值)通常与PPE 相关。因此，基于净公允价值调整的额 外折旧会减少子公司的收购后利润，这对 EPS 等重要财务绩效指标有负面影响。 Therefore,by reducing fair value adjustments it will improve the apparent performance of new acquisitions and the consolidated financial statements.Accountants should act ethically and ignore undue pressure to undertake creative accounting in preparing such adjustments.Guidance such as IFRS 3 Business Combinations and IFRS 13 Fair Value Measurement should be used in preparing adjustments and professional valuers should be engaged where necessary. 因此，通过减少公允价值调整，将改善新收购和合并财务报表的表面绩效。会计人员在准备 此类调整时，应采取合乎道德的行为，忽视这种创造性会计的压力。编制报表要依据准则要 求，必要时应聘请专业估价师。 In measuring value in use,the discount rate used should be the pre-tax rate which refiects current market assessments of the time value of money and the risks specific to the asset.The discount rate should not reflect risks for which future cash flows have been adjusted and should equal the rate of return which investors would require if they were to choose an investment which would generate cash flows equivalent to those expected from the asset.By reducing the impairment,it would have a positive impact on the financial statements. 【折现率的选择】【不计提减值的动机：对报表和财务指标的影响】 在衡量使用价值时，使用的贴现率应为税前利率，反映当前市场对货币时间价值和资产特定 风险的评估。折现率不应反映未来现金流已调整的风险，而应等于投资者在选择产生与资产 预期现金流相等的现金流的投资时所需的回报率。通过减少减值，将对财务报表产生积极影 响。 The offer of a salary increase is inappropriate and no action should be taken until the situation is clarified.Inappropriate financial reporting raises issues and risks for those involved and others associated with the company.Whilst financial reporting involves judgement,it would appear that this situation is related to judgement. 加薪的是不适当的，在情况澄清之前，不应采取任何行动。不当的财务报告会给相关人员和 与公司相关的其他人员带来问题和风险。虽然财务报告涉及判断，但这种情况似乎与判断有 关 。 The director is trying to coerce the accountant into acting unethically.IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements requires all standards to be applied if fair presentation is to be obtained. Directors cannot choose which standards they do or do not apply. 董事会有几起潜在的违反会计准则和不道德行为。主任试图强迫会计做出不道德的行为。 IAS1 如果要获得公允列报，就必须采用所有标准。董事不能选择他们适用或不适用的标准 It is important that accountants identify issues of unethical practice and act appropriately in accordance with ACCA's Codes of Ethics.The accountant should discuss the matters with the director.The technical issues should be explained and the risks of non-compliance explained to the director.If the director refuses to comply with accounting standards,then it would be appropriate to discuss the matter with others affected such as other directors and seek professional advice from ACCA. 会计师应识别不道德行为的问题，并根据ACCA 的道德规范采取适当行动。会计应与主管讨 论此事。应向董事解释技术问题和不合规风险。如果董事拒绝遵守会计准则，则宜与其他受 影响的董事(如其他董事)讨论该事项，并向ACCA 寻求专业建议。 Legal advice should be considered if necessary.An accountant who comes under pressure from senior colleagues to make inappropriate valuations and disclosures should discuss the matter with the person suggesting this.The discussion should try to confirm the facts and the reporting guidance which needs to be followed. 如有必要，应考虑法律建议。如果一名会计师受到高级同事的压力，要求其做出不适当的估 值和披露，那么他应该与提出这一建议的人讨论这一问题。讨论应尽量确认需要遵循的事实 和报告指南。 Financial reporting does involve judgement but the cases above seem to be more than just differences in opinion.The accountant should keep a record of conversations and actions and discuss the matters with others affected by the decision,such as directors.Additionally, resignation should be considered if the matters cannot be satisfactorily resolved. 财务报告确实涉及主观判断，但上述案例似乎不仅仅是意见分歧。会师应记录对话和行动， 与受决策影响的其他人(如董事)讨论相关事宜。此外，如果无法令人满意地解决问题，应 考虑辞职。

stem_zh: Q1 Hill Co is the parent company of a group.All entities in the group have a financial year end of 30 September 20X6. H 公司是集团的母公司，会计年度的年底是20x6 年的9月30号。 1.Disposal of Doyle Co On 1 October 20X4,HillCo purchased 60%of the ordinary shares of Doyle Co.At this date, the fair value of Doyle Co's identifiable net assets was $510 million. 20x4 年 1 0 月 1 号 ，H 公司购买了D 公司60%的股份，在收购日当天子公司净资产的公 允价值是$510m。 The non-controlling interest at acquisition was measured at its fair value of $215 million. Goodwill arising on the acquisition of Doyle Co was $50 million and had not been impaired prior to the disposal date. 收购日当天少数股东权益的公允价值是215m, 商誉是50m, 一直到处置日商誉都没有 出现减值损失。 On 1 April 20X6,Hill Co disposed of a 20%holding in the shares of Doyle Co for cash consideration of $140 million,losing control.At this date,the net assets of Doyle Co, excluding goodwill,were carried in the consolidated financial statements at $590 million. 20x6 年 4 月 1 号 ，H 公司卖掉了20%的G 公司的股份，之前持有60%的股份，卖掉20%, 还剩40%的持股比例。20%的股份出售价格为140m, 在当天也失去了控制。在这一天， D 公司净资产的在合并报表上的账面价值是590m。 From 1 April 20X6,Hill Co has the ability to appoint two of the six members of Doyle Co's board of directors.The fair value of Hill Co's 40%shareholding was $300 million at that date. Doyle Co reported a profit for the year ended 30 September 20X6 of $123 million. 从20×6年4月1号起， H 公司只有能力任命D 公司董事会6个成员当中的2个。当天H 公司所持的D 公司的40%的股份的公允价值是300m 。D 公司今年的税后利润是123m。 2.Further information The group's financial controller has produced draft extracts of the consolidated statement of cash flows and has not yet considered the impact of the disposal of Doyle Co(exhibit 1). There are also some errors.All other consolidated financial statements have been correctly prepared.The draft extracts have been included in the spreadsheet response option. 集团的财务负责人有编制出如下的合并现金流量表，但是在编表的过程中没有考虑卖掉 所持D 公司的股份对现流表的影响。而且如下的合并现流表当中有一些错误，需要考生 们去更正。 The carrying amount of the net assets of Doyle Co on 1 April 20X6 were as follows: 20x6 年 4 月 1 号 ，D 公司的净资产在合并报表当中的账面价值如下： $m Property,plant and equipment 311.9 Inventories 200.5 Trade and other receivables 181.3 Cash and cash equivalents 20.9 Trade and other payables (124.6) Total 590 The statement of cash flows figures for inventories,trade and other receivables,trade and other payables and property,plant and equipment have been calculated by considering only the movement in the balances between the consolidated statement of financial position from 30 September 20X5 to 30 September 20X6. 合并现流表当中的应收账款，应付账款，存货，固定资产的数据都是直接用期末数和期 初数之间相减得到的，即没有考虑处置子公司对其的影响。 Hill Co exchanged property,plant and equipment with one of its suppliers on 1 November 20X5.It is estimated that the fair value of both assets exchanged was $50 million although the carrying amount of the asset transferred by Hill Co was $45 million at the date of transfer. There were no other disposals of non-current assets by the group during the year other than the disposal of Doyle Co.The group also acquired property,plant and equipment during the year for cash.The depreciation charge of the group for the year ended 30 September 20X6 was $775.1 million. H 公司在20×5年11月1号，通过以物易物的方式获得了一个固定资产，不管是换入资 产还是换出资产其 FV都 是 5 0m, 换出资产的账面记账是45m 。集团今年有用现金采购 固定资产，今年固定资产的折旧金额为775. 1m。 3.Deferred tax asset Hill Co has made a loss in the year ended 30 September 20X6 in its separate financial statements,as well as in the previous two financial years.In the consolidated statement of financial position,it proposes to recognise a deferred tax asset in respect of the carry-forward of unused tax losses.These losses cannot be surrendered to other group companies. H 公司在今年出现了亏损，且之前的两个会计年度也是亏损。在合并资产负债表当中， 公司针对亏损确认了递延所得税资产，而且H 公司出现的亏损不能给到集团当中其他的 公司去抵税。 On 30 September 20X6,Hill Co breached a covenant attached to a bank loan which is due for repayment in 20X9.The loan agreement terms state that a breach in loan covenants entitles the bank to demand immediate repayment of the loan.Hill Co and its subsidiaries do not have sufficient liquid assets to repay the loan in full. 在20×6年9月30号， H 公司违背了贷款合同的契约，该贷款本身应该在20x9 年 去 偿 还，但是贷款合同当中写着公司违约了那么银行就有权利要求企业立即还钱，而 H 公 司 及其子公司没有足够多的资产去全额偿还贷款。 Hill Co has produced a business plan which forecasts significant improvement in its financial situation over the next three years as a result of the launch of new products which are currently being developed. H公司的商业计划书当中说公司未来三年的财务状况会有大大的改善，因为公司将要发 布一款新产品，虽然这款产品还处于研发过程当中。 4.Convertible bond issue On 1 October 20X6,HillCo issued a convertible bond at par value of $20 million.The bond is redeemable for cash on 30 September 20X8 at par.Bondholders can instead opt for conversion in the form of a fixed number of shares in HillCo.Interest on the bond is payable at a rate of 4%a year in arrears.The interest rate on similar debt without a conversion option is 10%. 在20x6年 1 0 月 1 号 ，H 公司发行了面值为20m 的可转债，债券的到期日为20x8年 9 月30号，到期持有人可以选择按照面值拿钱回去，也可以选择将其转换成为固定数量 的股票。可转债每年年底支付利息，票面利率为4%,实际利率为10%。 Discount factors: Year Discount rate Discount rate 4% 10% 1 0.952 0.909 2 0.907 0.826 5.Draft consolidated SOCF The draft extracts have been replicated in the spreadsheet response option. Draft extracts from the consolidated statement of cash flows for the Hill group for the year ended 30 September 20X6: Cash flows from operating activities: $m Profit before taxation 1,581.7 Adjustments for: Increase in inventories -842.4 Increase in trade and other receivables -379.2 Increase in trade and other payables 224.2 Cash generated from operations 584.3 Interest paid -126.8 Income taxes paid -424.6 Net cash from operating activities 32.9 Cash flows from investing activities Purchase of property,plant and equipment -733.8 Net cash used in investing activities -733.8 Extracts from the consolidated statement of financial position of the Hill group: 30 September 20X6 30 September 20X5 $m $m Property,plant and equipment 4,953.0 4,219.2 Requirements
explanation_zh: (a)Using exhibit 1,explain,with suitable calculations,how the investment in Doyle Co should be dealt with in the consolidated financial statements for the year ended 30 September 20X6.(7 marks) 对 D 公司的投资应该如何在20x6 年的合并报表当中进行账务处理。 (b)Using the pre-populated spreadsheet response option and exhibits 1 and 2,update the draft extracts of the consolidated statement of cash flows to adjust for the disposal of Doyle Co shares and to amend for any errors/omissions made by the directors.(13 marks) 调整合并现金流量表。 (c)Using exhibit 3,evaluate the proposed treatment of Hill Co's deferred tax asset.(4 marks) 评估 H 公司针对亏损确认DTA 的账务处理。 (d)Using exhibit 4,discuss,with suitable calculations,how the convertible bond should be dealt with in the consolidated financial statements for the year ended 30 September 20X7.(6 marks) 可转债应该如何在20 x7 年的合并报表当中进行账务处理。 答案解析： (a)Doyle Co The sale of shares results in Hill Co losing control over Doyle Co.The goodwill,net assets and non-controlling interest(NCI)of Doyle Co must be derecognised from the consolidated statement of financial position.The difference between the proceeds from the disposal (including the fair value of the shares retained)and these amounts will give rise to a $47 million profit on disposal.This is calculated as follows: H 公司卖掉20%的持股比例，那么就失去了对D公司的控制，那么D 公司的商誉，少数 股东股权，净资产就不再需要合并到财务报表当中了，即需要从合并报表当中终止确认。 并且需要计算处置掉子公司产生的利得或损失。计算如下： $m Sales proceeds 140 FV of remaining 40%interest 300 440 减去：原先所持有的60%的股份在处置日当天在合并报表上的账面价值=处置日当天的 Net asset+处置日当天的goodwill-处置日当天的NCI Net asset at disposal date 590 Goodwill at disposal date 50 Less:NCl at disposal date Less:247=215+(590-510)40% 393 Gain on disposal 47 After the disposal of shares,Hill Co owns 40%of Doyle Co's shares and has the ability to appoint two of the six members of Doyle Co's board of directors.IAS 28 Investments in Associates and Joint Ventures states that an associate is an entity over which an investor has significant influence.Significant influence is presumed when the investor has a shareholding of between 20 and 50%.Representation on the board of directors provides further evidence that significant influence exists. 卖掉股份之后，H公司只持有D公司40%的持股比例，且有权利任命董事会当中的2名 成员，根据IAS28号准则，如果投资方能够对被投资方产生重大影响，那么被投资方即 为投资方的联营公司，所谓重大影响一般是持股比例在20%到50%之间。能够在董事会 当中派代表即表明重大影响存在。 Therefore,the remaining 40%shareholding in Doyle Co should be accounted for as an associate.It will be initially recognised at its fair value of $300 million and accounted for using the equity method.This means that the group recognises its share of the associate's profit for the year,which equates to $24·6 million ($123m x6/12×40%).As at the reporting date,the associate will be carried at $324·6 million ($300m +$24·6m)in the consolidated statement of financial position. 剩下40%的持股比例应该是看成对联营公司的投资，其初始入账金额为当中股票的公允 价值即300m, 后续按照权益法在合并报表当中进行账务处理。这意味着成为H 公司的 联营公司之后D 还是产生的利润H 公司能够分享其中的40%的比例。即24.6m, 这会使 得对联营公司的投资价值增长到324.6m。 (b)The consolidated statement of cash flows: Cash flows from operating activities: Profit before taxation Adjustments for: Depreciation Profit on disposal of Doyle Co Profit on disposal of PPE Share of profit of associate Increase in inventories Increase in trade and other receivables Increase in trade and other payables Cash generated from operations Interest paid Income taxes paid Net cash from operating activity调减 卖掉所持有的G公司20%的股份所收到的钱，计入合并现流表投资活动中。今年处置掉 了子公司Doyle Co,D公司处置日的cash余额为20.9,卖掉子公司也会使得这部分现金 流出集团，所以合并现流表当中处置子公司收到的现金净额为140-20.9=119.1;卖到子 公司所产生的的处置收益会使得PBT增加，但是不会影响经营活动现金流，所以要从 PBT当中调减。 Working2: Inventory B/F a Movement of inventory 1042.9 Operating activity调减 Disposal of Doyle Co (200.5) Inventory C/F a+842.4 存货总共增加1042.9,所以在经营活动中要调减1042.9,由于合并现流表中已经调减了 842.4,所以只需要再调减200.5就好了。 T/R
