Requirement fa)00 Ad Desciptian Requirement fa)间 Requirement a)间 Rquirement (b) Rquirement (C) 高顿ACCA, 为学员赢得世界尊重 Accounting for cryptocurrencies What is cryptocurrency? Cryptocurrency is an intangible digital token that is recorded using a distributed ledger infrastructure,often referred to as a blockchain. These tokens provide various rights of use. For example,cryptocurrency is designed as a medium of exchange. Other digital tokens provide rights to the use other assets or services,or can represent ownership interests. 加密货币是一种无形的数字代币，这些代币提供各种使用权。例如，加密货币被设计为一种交换 媒介。其他数字代币提供使用其他资产或服务的权利，或者可以代表所有权利益。 These tokens are owned by an entity that owns the key that lets it create a new entry in the ledger.Access to the ledger allows the re-assignment of the ownership of the token.These tokens are not stored on an entity's IT system as the entity only stores the keys to the Blockchain(as opposed to the token itself).They represent specific amounts of digital resources which the entity has the right to control,and whose control can be reassigned to third parties. 这些代币由拥有密钥的公司拥有，该密钥允许该公司在分类帐中创建新条目。通过访问分类账， 可以重新分配代币的所有权。这些代币不存储在IT 系统上，因为公司只存储区块链的密钥(与 代币本身相反)。它们代表公司有权控制的特定数量的数字资源，其控制权可以重新分配给第三 方。 What accounting standards might be used to account for cryptocurrency? At first,it might appear that cryptocurrency should be accounted for as cash because it is a form of digital money.However,cryptocurrencies cannot be considered equivalent to cash (currency)as defined in IAS 7 and IAS 32 because they cannot readily be exchanged for any good or service.Although an increasing number of entities are accepting digital currencies as payment,digital currencies are not yet widely accepted as a medium of exchange and do not represent legal tender.Entities may choose to accept digital currencies as a form of payment, but there is no requirement to do so. 最开始，加密货币应该作为现金，因为它是数字货币的一种形式。然而，加密货币并不能等同于 IAS 7和 IAS 32 中定义的现金(货币),因为它们不能轻易地交换任何商品或服务。虽然越来 越多的公司接受数字货币作为支付，但数字货币尚未被广泛接受为一种交换媒介，也不能代表法 定货币。公司可以选择接受数字货币作为一种支付形式，但并没有要求这样做。 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION IAS 7 defines cash equivalents as 'short-term,highly liquid investments hat are readily convertible to known amounts of cash and which are subject to an insignificant risk of changes in value'.Thus,cryptocurrencies cannot be classified as cash equivalents because they are subject to significant price volatility.Therefore,it does not appear that digital currencies represent cash or cash equivalents that can be accounted for in accordance with IAS 7. IAS 7将现金等价物定义为“短期、高流动性的投资，可随时变现且价值变动风险很小”。因此， 加密货币不能被归类为现金等价物，因为它们受到价格大幅波动的影响。因此，数字货币似乎并 不代表可以按照IAS 7入账的现金或现金等价物。 Intuitively,it might appear that cryptocurrency should be accounted for as a financial asset at fair value through profit or loss(FVTPL)in accordance with IFRS 9.However,it does not seem to meet the definition of a financial instrument either because it does not represent cash,an equity interest in an entity,or a contract establishing a right or obligation to deliver or receive cash or another financial instrument.Cryptocurrency is not a debt security,nor an equity security (although a digital asset could be in the form of an equity security)because it does not represent an ownership interest in an entity.Therefore,it appears cryptocurrency should not be accounted for as a financial asset. 根据 IFRS 9, 加密货币似乎应作为FVTPL 计量的金融资产。但是，它似乎不符合金融工具的定 义，因为它不代表现金、公司股权或确定交付或收到现金或其他金融工具的权利或义务的合同。 加密货币既不是债务证券，也不是股票证券(尽管数字资产可以采取股票证券的形式),因为它 不代表公司的所有权利益。因此，加密货币似乎不应被视为金融资产。 However,digital currencies do appear to meet the definition of an intangible asset in accordance with IAS 38,Intangible Assets.This standard defines an intangible asset as an identifiable non-monetary asset without physical substance.IAS 38 states that an asset is identifiable if it is separable or arises from contractual or other legal rights. An asset is separable if it is capable of being separated or divided from the entity and sold,transferred, licensed,rented or exchanged,either individually or together with a related contract, identifiable asset or liability. This also corresponds with IAS 21,The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates,which states that an essential feature of a non-monetary asset is the absence of a right to receive (or an obligation to deliver)a fixed or determinable number of units of currency.Thus,it appears that cryptocurrency meets the definition of an intangible asset in IAS 38 as it is capable of being separated from the holder and sold or transferred individually and,in accordance with IAS 21,it does not give the holder a right to receive a fixed or determinable number of units of currency. 然而，根据IAS 38, 数字货币似乎符合无形资产的定义。该准则将无形资产定义为没有实物形 态的可识别的非货币性资产。IAS 38规定，如果一项资产是可分离的或产生于合同权利或其他 法律权利的，则该资产是可识别的(可被单独辨认的)。所谓资产是可分离的，是说如果它能够从 公司中分离或分割出来，并单独或与相关合同、可识别资产或负债一起出售、转让、许可、出租 或交换。这也符合 IAS 21的要求，准则中指出，非货币性资产的一个基本特征是没有收取(或 交付)固定或可确定数量的货币单位的权利。因此，加密货币似乎符合IAS 38中无形资产的定 义，因为它能够与持有人分离并单独出售或转让，并且根据IAS 21,它没有赋予持有人接收固 定或可确定数量的货币单位的权利。 Cryptocurrency holdings can be traded on an exchange and therefore,there is an expectation that the entity will receive an inflow of economic benefits.However,cryptocurrency is subject to major variations in value and therefore it is non-monetary in nature. Cryptocurrencies are a form of digital money and do not have physical substance.Therefore, the most appropriate classification is as an intangible asset. 持有的加密货币可以在交易所交易，因此，预计公司将获得经济利益流入。然而，加密货币的价 值会发生重大变化，因此它在本质上是非货币性的。加密货币是数字货币的一种形式，不具有物 理实质。因此，最合适的分类是作为无形资产。 I AS 38 allows intangible assets to be measured at cost or revaluation. Using the cost model , intangible assets are measured at cost on initial recognition and are subsequently measured at cost less accumulated amortisation and impairment losses.Using the revaluation model, intangible assets can be carried at a revalued amount if there is an active market for them; however,this may not be the case for all cryptocurrencies.The same measurement model should be used for allassets in a particular asset class.If there are assets for which there is not an active market in a class of assets measured using the revaluation model,then these assets should be measured using the cost model. IAS 38允许无形资产以成本或重估模型计量。使用成本模式，无形资产在初始确认时按成本计 量，随后按成本减去累计摊销和减值损失计量。使用重估模型，如果存在活跃市场，无形资产可 以按重估金额入账；然而，并非所有加密货币都是如此。对特定资产类别中的所有资产都应使用 相同的计量模型。如果在使用重估模型计量的一类资产中存在没有活跃市场的资产，则应使用成 本模型计量。 IAS 38 states that a revaluation increase should be recognised in other comprehensive income and accumulated in equity. However,a revaluation increase should be recognised in profit or loss to the extent that it reverses a revaluation decrease of the same asset that was previously recognised in profit or loss.A revaluation loss should be recognised in profit or loss. However, the decrease shall be recognised in other comprehensive income to the extent of any credit balance in the revaluation surplus in respect of that asset.It is unusual for intangible assets to have active markets.However,cryptocurrencies are often traded on an exchange and therefore it may be possible to apply the revaluation model. IAS 38规定，重估增值的部分，应在其他综合收益中确认并结转至equity。但如果之前重估减 值后又重估增值了的话，则增值的部分需要计入PL 剩余的再计入 OCl 。重估减值应确认在损益 中。但如果之前有过重估增值今年又减值的话，则减值的部分先计入 OCI 剩余的部分再计入损 益。不过无形资产有活跃市场是可能性是比较低的。但是加密货币通常在交易所交易(也就是存 在活跃市场),因此可以应用重估模型。 Where the revaluation model can be applied,IFRS 13,Fair Value Measurement,should be used to determine the fair value of the cryptocurrency.IFRS 13 defines an active market,and judgement should be applied to determine whether an active market exists for particular cryptocurrencies.As there s daily trading of Bitcoin,it is easy to demonstrate that such a market exists.A quoted market price in an active market provides the most reliable evidence of fair value and is used without adjustment to measure fair value whenever available.In addition, the entity should determine the principal or most advantageous market for the cryptocurrencies. 如果可以应用重估模型的话，IFRS 13应该用于确定加密货币的公允价值。IFRS 13定义了活跃 市场，应据此进行判断以确定特定加密货币是否存在活跃市场。由于比特币每天都有交易，很容 易证明这样一个市场的存在。活跃市场中的市场报价为公允价值提供了最可靠的证据，并在不进 行调整的情况下用于计量公允价值。此外，公司应确定加密货币的主要或最有利市场。 An entity will also need to assess whether the cryptocurrency's useful life is finite or indefinite. An indefinite useful life is where there is no foreseeable limit to the period over which the asset is expected to generate net cash inflows for the entity.It appears that cryptocurrencies should be considered as having an indefinite life for the purposes of IAS 38.An intangible asset with an indefinite useful life is not amortised but must be tested annually for impairment. 公司还需要评估加密货币的使用寿命是可确定的还是不可确定的。不可确定的使用寿命指的是资 产预计产生净现金流入的期限没有可预见的限制。就 IAS 38而言，加密货币似乎应被视为具有 不可确定的使用寿命。使用寿命不确定的无形资产不进行摊销，但必须每年进行减值测试。 In certain circumstances,and depending on an entity's business model,it might be appropriate to account for cryptocurrencies in accordance with IAS 2,Inventories,because IAS 2 applies to inventories of intangible assets.IAS 2 defines inventories as assets: ·held for sale in the ordinary course of business ·in the process of production for such sale,or ·in the form of materials or supplies to be consumed in the production process or in the rendering of services. 在某些情况下，根据业务模型，按照IAS 2对于加密货币进行会计处理也许是适合的。因为IAS 2 适用于无形资产的存货。IAS 2将存货定义为资产： -日常业务过程中的待售资产 -在生产过程中用于销售，或 -在生产过程或提供服务过程中消耗的材料或供应品的形式。 For example,an entity may hold cryptocurrencies for sale in the ordinary course of business and,if that is the case,then cryptocurrency could be treated as inventory. Normally,this would mean the recognition of inventories at the lower of cost and net realisable value.However,if the entity acts as a broker-trader of cryptocurrencies,then IAS 2 states that their inventories should be valued at fair value less costs to sell.This type of inventory is principally acquired with the purpose of selling in the near future and generating a profit from fluctuations in price or broker-traders'margin.Thus,this measurement method could only be applied in very narrow circumstances where the business model is to sell cryptocurrency in the near future with the purpose of generating a profit from fluctuations in price. 举个例子，公司可能在正常业务过程中持有用于销售的加密货币，如果是这种情况，那么加密货 币可以被视为库存。通常，这就意味着需要按照成本和可变现净值中的较低者确认存货价值。然 而，如果该公司作为加密货币的交易商，则IAS 2规定其库存应以公允价值减去销售成本进行估 值。这种类型的存货获得的主要目的是在不久的将来出售，并从价格波动或经纪人的保证金中产 生利润。因此，这种计量方法只能适用于非常有限的情况，即商业模式是在不久的将来出售加密 货币，目的是从价格波动中获得利润。 As there is so much judgement and uncertainty involved in the recognition and measurement of crypotocurrencies,a certain amount of disclosure is required to inform users in their economic decision-making.IAS 1,Presentation of Financial Statements,requires an entity to disclose judgements that its management has made regarding its accounting for holdings of assets,in this case cryptocurrencies,if those are part of the judgements that had the most significant effect on the amounts recognised in the financial statements.Also IAS 10,Events after the Reporting Period requires an entity to disclose any material non-adjusting events. This would include whether changes in the fair value of cryptocurrency after the reporting period are of such significance that non-disclosure could influence the economic decisions that users of financial statements make on the basis of the financial statements. 由于加密货币的识别和计量涉及太多的判断和不确定性，因此需要进行一定程度的披露，以告知 报表使用者便于更好的做决策。IAS1 要求公司披露其管理层对其持有资产(在本例中为加密货 币)的会计处理所做的判断，前提是这些判断对财务报表中确认的金额具有最重大的影响。 IAS 10要求公司披露所有重大非调整事件。这将包括报告期后加密货币公允价值的变化是否重大， 以至于不披露可能会影响报表使用者做出决策。 So,accounting for cryptocurrencies is not as simple as it might first appear.As no IFRS standard currently exists,reference must be made to existing accounting standards(and perhaps even the Conceptual Framework of Financial Reporting).SBR candidates should be prepared to adopt this approach in an exam situation because it allows them to substantiate their conclusion which is an approach that will be expected by employers in practice. 因此，加密货币的会计计量并不像最初看起来那么简单。由于目前没有对应的准则规范，必须参 考现有的会计准则(甚至可能是财务报告的概念框架)。 SBR 考生需要好准备在考试中采用这 种方法，因为这使他们能够证实自己的结论，这是雇主在实践中所期望的方法。 IFRS 9,Financial Instruments -part 1 Recognition of financial assets and liabilities In accordance with IFRS 9,Financial Instruments,a company recognises a financial asset or a financial liability when the company becomes party to the contractual provisions of the instrument.For example,if a company receives a firm order for goods from a customer,it should delay recognition of the trade receivable until at least one of the parties has performed under the agreement. This would normally be when the goods are shipped or delivered. In contrast, however,a forward contract or option is recognised on the commitment date if it falls within the scope of IFRS 9. 根据IFRS9 金融工具的规定，当公司成为金融工具合同条款的一方时(签约， 成为契约的一方),就要确认金融资产或金融负债。比方说，如果一家公司从客 户那里收到一份确定的货物订单，它应该推迟确认应收账款，直 到至少一方已经 履行了协议(否则就是待执行合同，不用记账)。通常是在货物装运或交付运走的 时候。然而，与之相反，如果远期合同或期权属于IFRS0 的范畴，则在承诺日 当天予以确认。 Except for trade receivables,a company measures a financial asset or financial liability on initial recognition at its fair value. The treatment of transaction costs directly attributable to the acquisition or issue depends on the instrument's measurement category. 除了应收账款，金融资产/负债在初始确认的时候，应按照公允价值进行计量。 而收购或发行所产生的直接归属交易成本，根据金融工具的计量类别进行相应处 理 。 Measurement of financial assets A financial asset is measured at fair value through profit or loss(FVTPL) unless it is measured at amortised cost or at fair value through other comprehensive income(FVTOCI).Classification depends on both the company's business model for managing the financial assets and the contractual cash flow characteristics of the financial asset. 金融资产一般是以公允价值增减变动计入利润表计量 (FVTPL), 除非根据调 减需要以摊销成本法或以公允机制增减变动计入其他综合性收益 ( FVTOCI) 计 量。具体的计量划分取决于公司管理金融资产的商业模式和屠户金融资产的契约 现金流量特征。

Question 1 In accordance with IFRS 9,what is meant by a company's 'business model? 什么是 business model商业模式? Answer According to IFRS 9,a company's business model refers to how an entity manages its financial assets in order to generate cash flows. It determines whether cash flows will result from collecting contractual cash flows,selling financial assets or both.An entity's business model is a matter of fact. 根据IFRS 9, 商业模式指的是公司如何管理其金融资产，以产生现金流。它决定 了现金流是否来自获取契约现金流，出售金融资产或两者兼有。 A financial asset is measured at amortised cost if: (i)the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is to hold financial assets in order to collect contractual cash flows; and (ii)the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding. 金融资产按照摊余成本法计量的条件是： (i)该金融资产按照商业模式持有，且其目的是持有该金融资产，并且收集契约现 金流；以及 (ii)金融资产的合同条款在特定日期产生的现金流量仅是未偿还本金的本金以及 利息。 A financial asset is measured at FVTOCl if: (i)the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial assets; and (ii)the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding. 金融资产按照FVTOCI 计量的条件是： (i) 金融资产采用商业模式，且其目标通过收取契约现金流和出售金融资产来实 现；以及 (ii) 金融资产的合同条款在特定日期产生的现金流量仅是未偿还本金的本金以及 利息。 A company can make an irrevocable election at initial recognition for investments in equity instruments to be measured at FVTOCl if they are not held for trading. 如果权益工具不是为了交易而持有的，公司可以在初始确认时对以FVTOCI 计 量的权益工具投资进行不可撤销的选择。 IFRS 9 contains an option to designate,at initial recognition,a financial asset as measured at FVTPL if it would eliminate or significantly reduce an 'accounting mismatch’.This can arise when measuring assets or liabilities,or recognising the gains and losses on them,on different bases. IFRS9 准则当中包含了一种选择，即在初始确认时，如果能够消除或显著减少 “会计不匹配(会计错配)”,则金融资产可以按照FVTPL 进行计量。当以不同 计量基础计量资产或负债，或者是确认其损益时，可能会出现这种情况。

Question 2 On 1 June 20X7,Design Co loaned $9 million to a subsidiary.There was no loan agreement or repayment date or interest charged.However,there was an expectation that the amount would be repaid on demand.This type of event has previously occurred,and the loan repaid.At 31 December 20X7,the subsidiary could not repay the loan but it was probable that repayment would occur by February 20X8. Does the inter-company loan meet the conditions in IFRS 9 to be measured at amortised cost? 20X7 年 6 月 1 日 ，D 公司向子公司贷款900 万美元。并没有贷款协议、还款日 期或收取利息。不过，预计这笔款项将按要求偿还。此类事件以前曾发生过，贷 款已偿还。截至20X7 年12月31日，该子公司无法偿还贷款，但很可能在20X8 年 2 月前偿还。 问：公司内部贷款是否符合IFRS9 中按照摊余成本法计量的要求? Answer To classify a financial asset at amortised cost, IFRS 9 states that its contractual terms must give rise to cash flows on specified dates that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding. These cash flows must be consistent with normal lending arrangements. 【引用准则】金融资产按照摊余成本法进行计量条件为持有到期，在特定的日期 收取本金以及利息。这些现金流需要符合正常的贷款安排。 As the inter-company loan can be called at any time,there is an argument that a financial institution would charge low interest for such a loan as it could be called in at any time. Thus,it could be argued that there is nothing different to a normal lending arrangement with the current terms.Therefore,the intercompany loan meets the contractual cash flow characteristics test in IFRS 9. 【引申讨论】由于公司间贷款可以在任何时候进行，因此有一种观点认为，金融 机构会对随时调用的此类贷款收取较低的利息。因此按照目前的条款，可以认为 与正常的贷款安排没有什么不同。因此，公司间贷款也是符合 IFRS9 中的契约 现金流特征测试的。 To be measured at amortised cost,the instrument must be held within a business model that has the objective to hold financial assets to collect contractual cash flows.The loan is not held to sell;therefore,the inter-company loan meets both the IFRS 9 tests to be measured at amortised cost. 【结合案例，给出结论】想要以摊余成本法进行计量，金融工具必须在持有金融 资产以收集合同现金流为目标的商业模式下持有。案例信息显示，此贷款不是为 了出售而持有的；因此，公司间贷款符合 IFRS 9的测试，可以按照摊余成本法 进行计量。 Measurement of financial liabilities Measurement of financial liabilities is easier than financial assets. Almost all financial liabilities are measured at amortised cost ,meaning that a finance cost is reported in profit or loss based on the effective rate of interest. Financial liabilities held for trading, including derivative liabilities,are measured at FVTPL. 金融负债的计量比金融资产要容易一些。几乎所有金融负债都是以摊余成本法计 量的，这就意味着财务费用根据有效实际利率计算并计入损益。交易性金融负债， 包括衍生负债，则按照FVTPL 计量。 As with financial assets,a company can designate,at initial recognition,a financial liability to be measured at FVTPL if it would eliminate or significantly reduce an‘ accounting mismatch'. 跟金融资产一样，如果能够消除或显著减少“会计错配”,公司也可以在初始确 认的时候，指定金融负债按照FVTPL进行计量。 Where a company has a financial liability that is measured at FVTPL, the fair value of the liability can depend on the credit worthiness of the company. As a result,where the credit worthiness of a company deteriorates the fair value of the liability will typically reduce,resulting in a fair value gain.And where the credit worthiness of a company improves the fair value of the liability will typically increase,resulting in a fair value loss. IFRS 9 requires the fair value movements in a financial liability designated to be measured at FVTPL that are attributable to changes in the credit risk of that liability should be presented in other comprehensive income, as opposed to being presented in profit or loss.This does not apply where this treatment would create or would enlarge an accounting mismatch. 如果一家公司的金融负债以FVTPL计量，则该负债的公允价值可能取决于该公 司的信用状况。因此，当一家公司的信用状况恶化时，负债的公允价值通常会降 低，从而产生公允价值收益(Dr Financial liability Cr PL)。当一家公司的信用度 提高时，负债的公允价值通常会增加，从而导致公允价值损失(Dr PL Cr Financial liability)。IFRS 9要求，指定以FVTPL 计量的金融负债的公允价值变动，如果 是由于信用风险导致的，则应在OCI其他综合性收益中列报，而不是在损益中 列报。如果此类会计处理会造成或扩大会计错配的情况，则不适用。

Question 3 Insert Co has applied the fair value option in IFRS 9 in order that the recognition of gains and losses on its investment properties and the related financial liability would be consistent. The investment property was measured at fair value in accordance with IAS 40 Investment Property using a discounted cash flow technique. Insert公司采用了IFRS 9 中的公允价值选择权，以便使其投资性房地产和相关金 融负债的损益确认保持一致。根据IAS 40的规定，使用折现现金流技术来计量投 资性房地产的公允价值。 Can Insert Co apply the fair value option rule in IFRS 9 to the measurement of its financial liability? 问 ：Insert 公司是否可以将 IFRS 9中的公允价值选择权应用于其金融负债的计 量? Answer IFRS 9 allows companies to designate a financial liability as measured at FVTPL if it would eliminate or significantly reduce a measurement or recognition inconsistency (an 'accounting mismatch')which would otherwise arise from measuring assets or liabilities or recognising the gains and losses on them on different bases.This is an accounting policy choice. 【引用准则】IFRS 9 允许公司将金融负债指定为以FVTPL 计量，前提是它可以 消除或显著减少因计量资产或负债或在不同基础上确认其损益而产生的计量或确 认不一致(“会计错配”)。这属于会计政策的选择。 If the fair value option was not used,then the financial liability would be measured at amortised cost and the investment property at fair value.However, there is significant correlation between the measurement of the fair value of the investment property and the related financial liability,in that both elements will use current interest rates.Therefore,the fair value option can be used to avoid inconsistency. 【结合案例】如果不使用公允价值选择权，则金融负债将按摊余成本计量，投资 性房地产将按公允价值计量。然而，投资性房地产公允价值的计量与相关金融负 债之间存在显著相关性，因为这两个要素都将使用当前利率。因此，可以使用公 允价值选择权来避免不一致。 Derecognition Derecognition is the removal of all or part of an asset or liability from the statement of financial position. 终止确认指的是从资产负债表中移除掉全部或者是部分的金融资产或者是负债。 Derecognition of financial assets A company derecognises a financial asset when the contractual rights to the cash flows from the financial asset have expired, or it transfers the financial asset such that it qualifies for derecognition.A company transfers a financial asset where it transfers the contractual rights to receive the cash flows of the financial asset,or where it retains the contractual rights to receive the cash flows of the financial asset but assumes a contractual obligation to pay the cash flows to a third party.The company also evaluates the extent to which it retains the risks and rewards of ownership of the financial asset. 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION 当金融资产现金流的合同权利到期时，或者公司转让该金融资产时，金融资产需 要被终止确认。公司转让金融资产的情况是，公司转让了其接收该金融资产现金 流的合同权利，或者公司保留了接收金融资产现金流量的合同权利但承担了向第 三方支付现金流的义务。该公司还评估其保留金融资产所有权的风险和回报的程 度。 On derecognition of a financial asset,the difference between the carrying amount and the consideration received is recognised in profit or loss. If a company neither transfers nor retains substantially all the risks and rewards of ownership of a transferred asset,and retains control of the transferred asset, the company continues to recognise the transferred asset to the extent of its continuing involvement. 金融资产终止确认时，账面金额与收到的对价之间的差额计入损益。如果公司既 没有转让也没有保留被转让资产所有权的绝大部分风险和报酬，并且保留了对被 转让资产的控制权，则该公司继续在其继续参与的范围内确认被转让资产。 Derecognition of financial liabilities A company should derecognise a financial liability from its statement of financial position when the obligation is discharged,cancelled or has expired. 当债务被解除、取消或到期时，公司应从其资产负债表中终止确认金融负债。 An exchange between an existing borrower and lender of debt instruments with substantially different terms is accounted for as extinguishing the original financial liability and recognition of a new financial liability.Similarly,a substantial modification of the terms of the existing financial liability is accounted for by extinguishing the original financial liability and recognising a new financial liability.The terms are substantially different if the present value of the cash flows under the new terms(including any fees paid net of any fees received)when discounted using the original effective interest rate is at least 10 per cent different from the present value of the remaining cash flows of the original financial liability. The difference between the carrying amount of a financial liability extinguished or transferred to another party and the consideration paid is recognised in profit or loss. 当现有借款人和贷款人之间将条款差异很大的债务工具进行交换的时候，应按照 取消(终止确认)原金融负债并确认新的金融负债进行会计处理。同样地，对现有 金融负债条款的重大修改应按照取消原有金融负债并确认新的金融负债的方式 进行核算。如果新条款下的现金流折现值(包括任何已付费用扣除任何已收费用 的净值)在使用原始实际利率折现后，与原金融负债的剩余现金流折现值相差至 少10%,则认为该条款存在重大差异。已消除地或转让给另一方的金融负债的 账面金额与支付对价之间的差额在损益中确认。 Where the terms are not substantially different the original liability is not derecognised.The liability is restated to the present value of the revised cash flows. Any increase or decrease in carrying amount is presented in the statement of profit or loss. 当条款没有重大差异的时候，则原负债不终止确认，而是按照更新调整后的现金 流折现值进行计量。账面价值的增加或减少都计入损益表。

Question 4 On 31 December 20X7,View Co modified the terms of its long-term loans (financial liabilities)such that the effective interest rate changed by 3%.As a result,the present value of the remaining outstanding loans was reduced by 25%. 20X7 年 1 2 月 3 1 日 ，View Co 修改了其长期贷款(金融负债)的条款，使实际利 率变化了3%。因此，剩余未偿还贷款的现值减少了25%。 What is the correct accounting treatment of the above? 该如何进行正确的会计处理? Answer The interest rate is substantially different from the original borrowing agreements and the discounted present value of the cash flows under the new terms is more than 10%different from the original financial loans.This means there has been a substantial modification of the terms of the existing financial liabilities. 利率与原借款协议有重大差异，新条款下的现金流折现值与原金融贷款的差异也 超过了10%。这意味着现有金融负债的条款已经发生了重大变更。 The original financial liabilities should be derecognised and new financial liabilities recognised instead.The difference between the carrying amount of the original liabilities that were extinguished and the fair value of the new liabilities should be shown in profit or loss.The new borrowings will be initially measured at fair value on the statement of financial position and subsequently measured at amortised cost using the new effective interest (unless the fair value option is applied). 应终止确认原始金融负债，并确认新的金融负债。被取消的原始负债的账面金额 与新负债的公允价值之间的差额应在损益中显示。新借款初始应以公允价值在资 产负债表中计量，之后应使用新的实际利率，按照摊余成本法进行计量(除非采 用公允价值计量选择权)。 Reclassification 重分类 When,and only when, a company changes its business model for managing financial assets,it should reclassify all affected financial assets. Reclassification should be applied prospectively from the reclassification date. The company should not restate any previously recognised gains,losses or interest already recognised. 当且仅当一家公司改变其管理金融资产的业务模式时，才应该对所有受影响的金 融资产进行重新分类。重分类应自重分类日后按照未来适用法进行会计处理。公 司对于以前年度确认的收益、损失或利息不再做任何修改了。 Financial assets designated at FVTPL and investments in equity measured at FVOCl are not subject to the reclassification requirements of IFRS 9. 指定为 FVTPL 的金融资产和以 FVTCOI 计量的股权投资不进行重分类。 Financial liabilities are never reclassified. 金融负债从不重新分类。 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION Impairment Except for investments in equity instruments,financial assets classified as amortised cost or FVTOCI must be tested for impairment at the end of each reporting period. 除投资权益类工具外，分类为以摊余成本法或按FVTOCI 计量的金融资产，必 须在每个报告期末进行减值测试。 IFRS 9 has a general approach for impairment that distinguishes between ‘12-month expected credit losses'and 'lifetime expected credit losses'. Expected credit losses are the present value of all cash shortfalls(the difference between the cash flows that are due to an entity and the cash flows that the entity expects to receive)over the expected life of the financial instrument.Expected credit losses should be measured in a way that reflects an unbiased and probability-weighted amount that is determined by evaluating a range of possible outcomes,the time value of money and reasonable and supportable information. IFRS 9对于减值采用了通用的方法，并区分“12个月预期信用损失”和“存续期预 期信用损失”。预期信用损失是指在金融工具的预期寿命内，所有现金短缺的折 现值(应付给公司的现金流与公司预计收到的现金流量之间的差额即为现金短 缺)。预期信用损失的计量方法应反映出一个无偏见和概率加权的金额，该金额 是通过评估一系列可能的结果、货币的时间价值以及合理和可支持的信息来确定 的 。 The decision as to whether the loss allowance is based upon 12-month expected credit losses or lifetime expected credit losses depends on whether there has been a significant increase in credit risk since initial recognition.An increase in crzedit risk is reflected by a change in the risk of a default occurring over the expected life of the financial asset.When making the assessment the company must consider reasonable and supportable information,that is available without undue cost or effort that is indicative of significant increases in credit risk since initial recognition.If the financial instrument has low credit risk at the reporting date,then the company can assume that the credit risk has not increased significantly since initial recognition.Low credit risk is based on a company's internal credit risk ratings,or other acceptable methods. 计提损失准备(Loss allowance,属于资产的备抵项，列示在SOFP) 是基于12个 月的预期信用损失还是存续期的预期信用损失，取决于自初始确认以来信贷风险 是否显著增加。信用风险的增加反映在金融资产预期寿命内发生违约的风险变化。 在进行评估的时候，公司必须充分考虑合理和可支持的信息，这些信息无需过多 的成本或努力便可获得，并能够表明自初始确认以来信用风险显著增加。如果金 融工具在报告日当天具有较低的信用风险，则公司可以假设自初始确认以来信用 风险并没有显著增加。低信用风险基于公司的内部信用风险评级或其他可接受的 方法。 If a financial asset's credit risk has not increased significantly since initial recognition,a company calculates 12-month expected credit losses. This means that expected credit losses are calculated based on default events that are possible within 12 months from the reporting date.Where the credit risk has increased significantly since initial recognition,a company calculates lifetime expected credit losses. This means that expected credit losses are calculated based on all possible default events over the expected life of the financial instrument.A company assesses at each reporting date whether the credit risk associated with a financial asset has increased significantly since initial recognition.For trade receivables,contract assets and lease receivables, a simplified approach may be applied.This approach allows a company to always calculate expected credit losses as equal to the lifetime expected credit losses. 如果金融资产的信用风险自初始确认以来没有显著增加，则计算 12 个月的预期 信用损失。这意味着预期信用损失是基于自报告日起 12 个月内可能发生违约的 事件进行计算的。如果自初始确认以来信用风险显著增加，公司将计算存续期预 期信用损失。这意味着预期信用损失是根据金融工具预期寿命内所有可能的违约 事件计算的。公司在每个报告日都需要评估与金融资产相关的信用风险自初始确 认以来是否显著增加。对于应收账款、合同资产和租赁应收款，可使用简化的方 法。这种方法允许公司可以一直将预期信用损失按照存续期的预期信用损失金额 来计算。 If an actual default event occurs and the financial asset becomes credit-impaired,lifetime expected credit losses are calculated as the difference between the asset's gross carrying amount and the present value of the expected future cash flows. 如果违约事件实际发生了，金融资产已经发生了信用减值，则需要确认存续期的 预期信用损失金额，也就是资产总账面金额与预期未来现金流现值之间的差额。 For financial assets measured at amortised cost,a loss allowance is recognised for the expected credit losses.This reduces the net carrying amount of the financial asset.Movements in the allowance year-on-year are charged (or credited)to profit or loss. 对于以摊余成本法计量的金融资产，需要为预期信用损失计提损失准备(Dr Loss Cr Loss allowance)。这将减少金融资产的账面净值(因为loss allowance 属于资 产备抵项)。损失准备的逐年变动计入损益。 For financial assets measured at FVOCl,no adjustment is made to the net carrying amount of the financial asset in respect of expected credit losses.This is because the financial asset must be measured at fair value.Instead,an increase (or decrease)in expected credit losses is charged (or credited)to profit or loss,with a corresponding adjustment to other comprehensive income. 对于按FVTOCI 计量的金融资产而言，则不会就预期信用损失对金融资产的账 面净值进行调整。这是因为金融资产必须以公允价值计量。所以，预期信用损失 的增加(或减少)计入(或贷记)损益，但同时需要对 OCI 其他综合性收益也进行相 应调整。 The rules are different for purchased or originated credit-impaired financial assets.These are financial assets that are already credit impaired at initial recognition.At each reporting date,a company recognises the amount of the change in lifetime expected credit losses as an impairment gain or loss in profit or loss. 对于购买或发起的信用减值金融资产，规则有所不同。这些金融资产是在初始确 认的时候就已经发生信用减值了。那么在每个报告日，公司将存续期预期信用损 失的增减变化金额作为损益确认进PL 即可。

Question 5 On 1 January 20X7,Sparrow Co purchased quoted bonds issued by Pippit Co for $10 million and measured them at amortised cost.The coupon rate of interest on the bonds was 5%,which was the same as the effective rate.Credit risk associated with the bonds on 1 January 20X7 was deemed to be low. No loss allowance was recognised on this date. 20x7 年 1 月 1 日 ，Sparrow 公司以$10m 的价格购买了Pippit公司发行的债券， 并按摊销成本法对其进行计量。债券的票面利率为5%,与实际利率相同。20 X7 年1月1日与债券相关的信用风险被认为是低的。该日未确认任何损失准备。 At 31 December 20X7,Sparrow Co received $0.5 million interest from the bonds.However,newspapers have reported that Pippit Co is in financial difficulties.The price of Pippit Co's bonds declined significantly despite an overall increase in the bonds market. 截至20x7 年 1 2 月 3 1 日 ，Sparrow 公司从债券中收到了$0.5 million 的利息。 然而，报纸报道称Pippit 公司陷入了财务困境。尽管债券市场整体上涨， Pippit Co. 的债券价格却大幅下跌。 At 31 December 20X7,12-month expected credit losses were calculated to be $1.6 million and lifetime expected credit losses were calculated to be $4.5 million. 3 1 日，12个月的预期损失为$1.6ATION截至20X7 年 1 2 月 计算为$4.5 million。 3 1 日，12个月的预期损失为$1.6 ATION million,存续期预期损失 ONLINE DELVE Y-PLATINUM Discuss how the above information should be accounted for by Sparrow Co in the year ended 31 December 20X7. 该如何进行会计处理? Answer At the reporting date,the gross carrying amount of the financial asset is $10 million. 报告日当天，金融资产的账面总值为$10m. At the reporting date,a loss allowance must be recognised because the financial asset is measured at amortised cost. 报告日当天，需要确认计提损失准备，因为金融资产是按照摊余成本法计量的。 The loss allowance should be equal to lifetime-expected credit losses if credit risk has increased significantly.This would seem to be the case because of Pippit Co's widely reported financial difficulties.Moreover,the decline in the price of Pippit Co's bonds suggests that the bonds are becoming riskier and that investors are less inclined to purchase them. 如果信用风险显著增加，则损失准备应等于存续期预期信用损失。被广泛报道的 财务困难似乎印证了信用风险的显著增加。此外，Pippit Co.债券价格的下跌也 表明，这些债券的风险变得越来越大，投资者也越来越不愿意购买。 As such a loss allowance should be recognised for $4.5 million,with a corresponding charge to profit or loss.This will reduce the net carrying amount of the financial asset presented on the statement of financial position to $5.5 million. 因此，需要确认$4.5m 的损失准备入账：Dr Loss 4.5m 因此资产负债表列示的账面净值=10m-4.5m=5.5m。 Cr Loss allowance 4.5m。 FRS 9,Financial Instruments-part 2 Derivatives 金融衍生工具 According to IFRS 9,Financial Instruments,a derivative is a contract that: ·will be settled at a future date ·requires no(or a low) initial investment,and ·changes value in response to movements in an underlying item(such as commodity prices or interest rates). 根据准则规定，衍生工具是一份合同，并且： -将在未来某个日期结算 -不需要(或很少)初始投资，以及 -根据基础项 目(如商品价格或利率)的变动而改变价值。 Derivatives can be used by companies to manage risk. For example,a company that manufactures bread may reduce its exposure to changes in the price of wheat by entering into futures contracts to buy wheat for a fixed price in six months'time.These contracts will be settled net in cash: if wheat prices rise,the company will make a gain on the contracts;if wheat prices fall,the company will make a loss. 公司可以利用衍生品进行风险管理。比如，一家生产面包的公司可能会通过签订期货合 约，约定在六个月后以一个固定价格购买小麦，从而减少小麦价格变化带来的风险。这 些合同将以现金净额结算：如果小麦价格上涨，公司将从合同中获利；如果小麦价格下 跌，公司将亏损。 All derivatives are measured at fair value. Changes in fair value are recognised in profit or loss (unless the entity has elected to apply hedge accounting by designating the derivative as a hedging instrument in an eligible hedging relationship.) 所有衍生工具均以公允价值计量。公允价值的变动直接在损益中确认(除非公司已选择 并通过指定套期工具达成有效套期关系) Note that a contract that otherwise meets the definition of a derivative would not be accounted for as a financial instrument if the company entered into it for the purpose of receiving a non-financial item (such as wheat)in accordance with its expected usage requirements. 需要注意的是，如果公司是为了按照预期的使用要求接收一个非金融项目(如小麦)而 签订的，虽然符合衍生工具定义但并不被视为金融工具。

Question 1 Insert Co entered into a forward contract to buy fuel,which was to be settled on 31 Decemher 20X7 The fuel was to he nhvsicallv delivered to the head office and to he used by Insert Co to heat its premises.The fuel was not for resale.The contract could not be settled through net cash settlement.A penalty was payable if Insert Co did not take delivery of the fuel.Insert Co had never failed to take delivery in the past. Should the contract be accounted for in accordance with IFRS 9? Insert Co签订了一项购买燃料的远期合同，该合同将于20X7 年12月31日结算。这 些燃料将实际交付给总部，并由Insert Co用来为其办公场所加热供暖。这些燃料并不 是用来转售的。该合同也不能通过净现金结算来结算。如果Insert Co没有提取燃料， 问：该合同是否应按照FIRS 9进行核算? Answer IFRS 9 should be applied to those contracts to buy or sell a non-financial item that can be settled net in cash.The exception are those contracts that are for purpose of the entity's expected purchase,sale or usage requirements.Therefore,in this case, IFRS 9 does not apply to the fuel contract as it willbe physically received and used in the company's business. IFRS 9应适用于购买或出售可以按现金净值结算的非金融项目的合同。如果是为了公 司预期采购、销售或使用要求而签订的合同则属于例外情况。因此，在这种情况下，IFRS 9不适用于燃料合同，因为它将在公司的业务中实际收到并使用燃料。 As Insert Co has never failed to take delivery in the past and a penalty is payable if Insert Co did not take delivery of the petroleum,it is unlikely that there willbe net settlement of the contract.Therefore,the purchase of the fuel should be accounted for in accordance with IAS 2,Inventories. 由于Insert Co过去从未失败交货，并且如果Insert Co没有成功交付石油，还需要支付 罚款，因此总体看来合同都不太可能按照净额结算。因此，燃料的购买应根据IAS 2, 存货进行核算。也就是一个预期的采购合同。 Hedge accounting 套期会计 Background As noted above,derivatives are used by companies to manage risk.In the financial statements,they are remeasured to fair value at each reporting date,with the gain or loss recognised in profit or loss. However,the item that was generating the risk may not be recognised in the financial statements (such as a future purchase of wheat),or it may be accounted for in a different way (such as inventories of wheat,which are measured at the lower of cost and net realisable value).This gives rise to an accounting mismatch. This causes volatility in profit or loss that can make the company look like a riskier investment to its shareholders,even though the company has taken proactive steps to manage its risk.This outcome is at odds with the purpose of hedging and can be reduced or eliminated if the company chooses to apply hedge accounting. 如上所述，公司利用衍生品来管理风险。在财务报表中，它们在每个报告日按公允价值 重新计量，其损益确认进损益。然而，产生风险的项目可能不会在财务报表中确认(例 如未来购买小麦),或者是按照不同的方式计量(比如小麦存货，按成本和可变现净值 中较低者计量)。这样会造成会计记账不匹配，从而导致利润或亏损的波动，这可能会 使公司在股东眼里是一个风险更大的投资，尽管公司已经采取了积极措施来管理风险。 这种结果与套期保值的目的不一致，如果公司选择采用套期保值会计，则可以减少或消 除这种后果。 Requirements The hedge accounting requirements in IFRS 9 are optional. f certain eligibility and qualification criteria are met,hedge accounting allows an entity to reflect risk management activities in the financial statements by matching gains or losses on financial hedging instruments(derivatives)with losses or gains on the risk exposures they hedge. IFRS 9中的套期会计要求是可选的。如果符合某些资格和标准，套期会计才允许企业 通过将金融套期工具(衍生工具)的收益或损失与对冲风险敞口的损失或收益相匹配，并 在财务报表中反映出风险管理活动。 Hedge accounting can be applied to the following examinable types of hedging relationships: 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION ·fair value hedge:a hedge of the exposure to changes in fair value of a recognised asset or liability or an unrecognised firm commitment,that is attributable to a particular risk and could affect profit or loss ·cash flow hedge:a hedge of the exposure to variability in cash flows that is attributable to a particular risk associated with a recognised asset or liability or a highly probable forecast transaction,and could affect profit or loss. 套保会计可应用于以下两类： -公允价值套期：对已确认的资产或负债或未确认的确定承诺的公允价值变动风险的套 期，可归因于特定风险并可能影响损益 -现金流套期：对现金流变化风险的套期，这种风险归因于与已确认资产或负债或极有 可能发生的预测交易相关的特定风险，并可能影响损益。 A hedging relationship qualifies for hedge accounting only if all of the following criteria are met: ·the hedging relationship consists only of eligible hedging instruments and eligible hedged items ·at the inception of the hedging relationship there is formal designation and documentation of the hedging relationship and the entity's risk management objective and strategy for undertaking the hedge ·the hedging relationship meets allthe hedge effectiveness requirements. 只有满足以下所有条件，才适用套期会计： -套期关系仅包括合格的套期工具和被套期项目 -在套保关系开始的时候，套保关系、企业的风险管理目标和承担套保风险的战略，均 有正式的书面文件记录 -套期关系符合所有套期保值有效性的要求。 In order to qualify for hedge accounting,the hedge relationship must meet the following effectiveness criteria at the beginning of each hedged period: ·there is an economic relationship between the hedged item and the hedging instrument ●the effect of credit risk does not dominate the value changes that result from that economic relationship,and ●the hedge ratio of the hedging relationship is the same as that actually used in the economic hedge. 为了符合套期会计，套期关系必须在每个套期开始时满足以下要求： -套期项目和被套期工具之间存在经济关系 -信用风险并不是经济关系中的主要风险，并不主导该经济关系所导致的价值变化，并 且 -套期关系的套期比率与经济套期中实际使用的套期比率相同。 Rebalancing If a hedging relationship ceases to meet the hedge effectiveness requirement relating to the hedge ratio but the risk management objective remains the same,the company adjusts the hedge ratio of the hedging relationship so that it meets the qualifying criteria again.This is called rebalancing.An entity discontinues hedge accounting prospectively only when the hedging relationship ceases to meet the qualifying criteria after any rebalancing. 如果套期关系不再满足与套期比率相关的套期有效性要求，但风险管理目标维持不变， 公司将调整套期关系的套期比率，使其再次符合要求。这就是所谓的rebalancing 再 平 衡。只有当套期关系在任何再平衡后不再符合套期会计要求时，公司才会从当下开始往 后终止套期会计。 Fair value hedge accounting If the fair value hedge meets the qualifying criteria,the hedging instrument is re-measured at fair value.The gain or loss is recognised in profit or loss. The carrying amount of the hedged item is adjusted for the gain or loss attributable to the hedged risk only and is also recognised in profit or loss.Thus,all gains and losses attributed to the hedged risk are recognised in the statement of profit or loss,with the result that there is no net profit and loss effect,other than any hedge ineffectiveness. 如果公允价值套期符合条件要求，套期工具按公允价值重新计量。收益或亏损在损益中 确认。被套期项目的账面金额仅根据套期风险产生的损益进行调整，并在损益中确认。 因此，对冲风险导致的所有损益均在损益表中确认，除任何对冲无效外，不存在净损益 效 应 。 Note that if the hedged item is an equity instrument designated to be measured at FVOCl,then the gains and losses on the hedged item and hedging instrument are both reported in other comprehensive income. 如果被套期项目是指定以FVTOCI 计量的权益工具，那么被套期项目和套期工具的损益 都计入OCI 其他综合性收益。 Cash flow hedge accounting If the cash flow hedge meets the qualifying criteria,the hedging instrument is re-measured at fair value. Gains or losses on the effective portion of the hedging instrument are recognised in OCI. These amounts will be taken to a 'cashflow hedging reserve'as a separate component of equity.The ineffective portion of the gain or loss on the hedging instrument is recognised in the statement of profit or loss. 如果现金流套期符合要求，则套期工具按公允价值重新计量。套期工具有效部分的收益 或损失在OCI 确认。这些金额将作为权益的一个单独组成部分计入“现金流对冲准备金”。 套期工具无效部分则在损益表中确认。 When cash flows relating to the hedged item are reported in profit and loss,amounts in OCl are reclassified ('recycled')to the statement of profit or loss. 当与被套期项目相关的现金流在损益中报告时，记录在OCI 的金额被重分类(“循环”)到 损益表中。 Where a hedge of a forecast transaction results in the recognition of a financial asset or liability,the gains or losses on the hedging instrument that have previously been recognised in OCl are reclassified(‘recycled')in the same period as the asset or liability affects profit or loss(for example,in the periods when interest expense or income is recognised).If a hedged forecast transaction subsequently results in the recognition of a non-financial asset or non-financial liability,the company removes that amount from the cash flow hedge reserve and includes it directly in the initial cost or other carrying amount of the asset or the liability.This is not a reclassification adjustment and hence it does not affect other comprehensive income.However,if a company expects that any of a loss recognised in OCI will not be recovered in future periods,it should reclassify to profit or loss the amount that is not expected to be recovered. 如果预测交易的套期保值导致金融资产或负债的确认，以前在OCI 确认的套期工具的 收益或损失在资产或负债影响损益的同一期间重新分类(“循环”)(例如，在确认利息支出 或收入的期间)。如果套期预测交易随后导致非金融资产或非金融负债的确认，公司从 现金流套期准备金中移除该金额，并将其直接计入资产或负债的初始成本或其他账面金 额。这不是重新分类调整，因此不会影响其他综合收益。但是，如果一家公司预计在 OCI 确认的任何损失在未来期间不会被收回，它应该将预计不会被收回的金额重新分类 到损益中。

Question 2 As a result of economic turmoil,Mailings Co has stopped purchasing raw materials. The ability of customers to buy from Mailings Co has also been adversely affected by the economic difficulties.Highly probable future raw material purchases,and sales of finished goods,were designated as hedged items in cash flow hedging relationships. 由于经济混乱，Mailings 公司已经停止购买原材料。顾客从 Mailings 公司购买的能力也 受到经济困难的不利影响。极有可能的未来原材料购买和成品销售被指定为现金流套期 关系中的被套期项目。 What is the potential effect of the economic turmoil on Mailing Co's ability to apply hedge accounting and the effectiveness of hedging relationships? 经济动荡对 Mailing Co应用套期会计的能力和套期关系的有效性有什么潜在影响? Answer Because of the economic circumstances,Mailing Co no longer intends to purchase raw materials.Its customers are likely to be in a similar position.These transactions have been designated as hedged items in cash flow hedge relationships.However,if they are no longer highly probable than these are not eligible hedged items and so hedge accounting must cease. 由于经济形势，Mailing 公司不再打算购买原材料。它的客户很可能处于类似的境地。 这些交易被指定为现金流套期关系中的被套期项目。但是，如果它们不再是很有可能发 生的话，则不再符合套期会计要求，因此套期会计必须停止。 When the expected timing of a designated hedged transaction changes,an entity is required to reassess whether the hedged transaction identified in the entity's hedge documentation is still the same hedged transaction.In other words,it must assess whether the hedged transaction is still expected to occur. 当被指定的套期交易的预期时间发生变化时，要求企业重新评估套期文件中确定的套期 交易是否仍然是相同的套期交易。换句话说，它必须评估套期交易是否仍然预期发生。 If Mailing Co decides that a forecasted transaction is no longer highly probable,but is still expected to occur,the entity must discontinue hedge accounting prospectively and defer the gain or loss on the hedging instrument that has been recognised in other comprehensive income accumulated in equity until the forecasted transaction occurs. 如果Mailing Co 认为预测的交易不再很可能发生，但仍预计会发生，则企业必须从当下 终止套期会计，并将已在权益中累计的其他综合收益中确认的套期工具损益推迟至预测 交易发生。 If the forecasted transaction is no longer expected to occur the entity must immediately reclassify to profit or loss any accumulated gain or loss on the hedging instrument. 如果预测的交易预计不再发生，企业则必须立即将套期工具的任何累计损益重新分类为 损益。 Note that a change in the timing of a hedged forecast transaction,or increases in credit risk,may also cause a hedging contract to fail the hedge effectiveness assessment. 请注意，套期预测交易时间的变化或信用风险的增加。也可能导致套期合同无法通过套 期有效性评估。

Question 3 Review Co is preparing its financial statements for the year ended 31 December 20X7 .Review Co wishes to protect itself against the foreign exchange risk arising on 3 million dinars which it expects to receive on 31 January 20X8. Review Co正在编制截至20x7 年12月31日的年度财务报表。 Review Co希望希望保 护自己免受预计于20X8年1月31日收到的3 million dinars 的外汇风险。 Review Co enters into a forward currency contract on 1 November to sell 3 million dinars on 31 January 20X8,designating the contract as the hedging instrument in a cash flow hedging relationship. Management assesses the hedge to be effective. On 31 December 20X7,the currency contract has a positive fair value of $100,000 which is matched by a decrease in the fair value of the forecast cash flow of $98,000.The source of hedge ineffectiveness was analysed,and it was deemed that the hedge effectiveness criteria were still met and that no rebalancing was required. Review Co 于11月1日签订远期货币合同，计划于20x8 年1月31日出售3 million dinars, 同时将该合同指定为现金流套期关系中的套期工具。管理层评估套期是有效的。 在20X7 年12月31 日，货币合同的公允价值增加了$100,000,与此相匹配的是，预测 现金流的公允价值下降了$98,000。通过对套期无效性的来源分析，发现仍然符合套期 有效性要求，因此不需要再平衡。 On 31 January 20X8,Review Co receives the expected 3 million dinars and terminates the currency contract which has a fair value of $104,000.As such,there was no hedge ineffectiveness. (The above example also assumes that the sale is recognised at the same point in time that cash is received). 20X8 年1月31日，Review Co收到了预期的3 million dinars,并按照公允价值$104,000 终止了货币期货合同。因此，不存在套期无效性。 (上述示例还假设销售在收到现金的同一时间点得到确认)。 Discuss the accounting for the above cash flow hedge. 讨论现金流套期的会计处理。 Answer The effective portion of the hedge,that is the 'matched'$98,000 increase in fair value, is credited to OCl as the lesser of the cumulative gain on the hedging instrument and the cumulative change in the fair value of the expected future cash-flows.This willbe taken to a cash flow hedging reserve,which is an 'other component of equity'.The ineffective portion($2,000)is credited to profit or loss.The accounting entries willbe: 套期的有效部分，即公允价值增加与下降的$98,000 刚好“匹配”,作为套期工具的累 计收益和预期未来现金流公允价值的累计变化中的较小者记入OCI 贷方。这将计入现 金流套期保值准备金中，也就是“权益的其他组成部分”。而无效部分$2,000( 相当于 over-hedge 过度对冲)则计入损益。会计分录如下： Dr Currency contract-$100,000 Cr OCI-$98,000(有效对冲部分) Cr Profit or loss-$2,000(无效对冲部分) On 31 January 20X8, the fair value of the hedge has increased by $4,000.However, the hedge is now fully effective,so that the cumulative amount in OCl needs to reflect the entire $104,000 increase in fair value. 20X8 年1月31日，套期的公允价值增加了$4,000。但是此时套期已经完全有效，因 此OCI的累计金额需要反映公允价值增加的总和$104,000,将过去无效部分反向冲销，剩余计入OCl。Dr Currency contract-$4,000 Cr OCI-$6,000Dr Profit or loss-$2,000On 31 January 20X8,the cumulative fair value in equity must be‘reclassified to profitor loss'when the transaction is completed.The accounting entries are:在20X8年1月31日，当交易完成时，累积的OCI必须重新分类到损益中。会计分录 如下：Dr OCI-$104,000Cr Profit or loss-$104,000The currency contract is net settled and thus cash of $104,000 willbe received.货币合同因为是按照净额结算的，因此将收到现金$104,000。Dr Cash-$104,000Cr Currency contract-$104,000 此OCI的累计金额需要反映公允价值增加的总和$104,000,将过去无效部分反向冲销， 剩余计入OCl。 Dr Currency contract-$4,000 Cr OCI-$6,000 Dr Profit or loss-$2,000 On 31 January 20X8,the cumulative fair value in equity must be‘reclassified to profit or loss'when the transaction is completed.The accounting entries are: 在20X8年1月31日，当交易完成时，累积的OCI必须重新分类到损益中。会计分录 如下： Dr OCI-$104,000 Cr Profit or loss-$104,000 The currency contract is net settled and thus cash of $104,000 willbe received. 货币合同因为是按照净额结算的，因此将收到现金$104,000。 Dr Cash-$104,000 Cr Currency contract-$104,000 When does debt seem to be equity? IAS⑧32 Financial Instruments:Presentation outlines the definition of financial assets,financial liabilities and equity.In doing so,it establishes principles for presenting financial instruments as liabilities(debt)or equity,and for offsetting financial assets and liabilities. IAS 32 概述了金融资产、金融负债和权益的定义。在此过程中，它确立了将金融工具列示为负 债(债务)或权益以及金融资产和负债的抵消原则。 The classification of a financial instrument by the issuer as either debt or equity can have a significant impact on the entity's gearing ratio,reported earnings,and debt covenants.Equity classification can avoid such impact but may be perceived negatively if it is seen as diluting existing equity interests.The distinction between debt and equity is also relevant where an entity issues financial instruments to raise funds to settle a business combination using cash or as part consideration in a business combination. 发行人将金融工具分类为债务或权益，可能会对公司的杠杆比率、报告收益和债务契约产生重大 影响。股权分类可以避免这种影响，但如果被视为稀释现有股权，则可能会被视为负面影响。当 公司为了企业合并而筹集资金并将其作为现金对价或者对价的一部分时，债务和权益之间的区别 也很相关。 Understanding the nature of the classification rules and potential effects is critical for management and must be borne in mind_when evaluating alternative financing options. Liability classification normally results in any payments being treated as interest and charged to earnings,which may affect the entity's ability to pay dividends on its equity shares. 了解分类的规则和潜在影响对管理人员至关重要，特别是在评估备选融资方案时。负债分类通常 会导致任何支付都被视为利息并计入收益，这可能会影响公司支付其股息的能力(因为费用增加， 收入减少了)。 The key feature of debt is that the issuer is obliged to deliver either cash or another financial asset to the holder.The contractual obligation may arise from a requirement to repay principal or interest or dividends.Such a contractual obligation may be established explicitly or indirectly but through the terms of the agreement.For example,a bond that requires the issuer to make interest payments and redeem the bond for cash is classified as debt.In contrast, equity is any contract that evidences a residual interest in the entity's assets after deducting all of its liabilities.A financial instrument is an equity instrument only if the instrument includes no contractual obligation to deliver cash or another financial asset to another entity,and if the instrument will or may be settled in the issuer's own equity instruments. 债务主要的特征是，发行方有义务向持有方交付现金或其他金融资产。合同义务可能产生于对偿 还本金或是利息或是股息的要求。这种合同义务可以通过协议的条款明确或间接地确定。比如， 要求发行方支付利息并现金赎回的债券被归类为债务。相比之下，权益是指任何能够证明公司资 产在扣除所有负债后存在剩余权益的合同。只有当金融工具不包括向另一方交付现金或其他金融 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION 资产的合同义务，以及该工具将来可能以发行方自身的权益工具结算时，该金融工具才是权益工 具 。 For instance,ordinary shares,where all the payments are at the discretion of the issuer,are classified as equity of the issuer.The classification is not quite as simple as it seems.For example,preference shares required to be converted into a fixed number of ordinary shares on a fixed date,or on the occurrence of an event that is certain to occur,should be classified as equity. 比如，如果普通股的所有付款均由发行方自行决定，则该普通股被归类为发行方的权益。这个分 类并不像看起来那么简单。例如，需要在固定日期或在转换确定会发生的时候，将优先股转为固 定数量的普通股，应归类为权益。 A contract is not an equity instrument solely because it may result in the receipt or delivery of the entity's own equity instruments.The classification of this type of contract is dependent on whether there is variability in either the number of equity shares delivered or variability in the amount of cash or financial assets received.A contract that will be settled by the entity receiving or delivering a fixed number of its own equity instruments in exchange for a fixed amount of cash,or another financial asset,is an equity instrument.This has been called the 'fixed for fixed'requirement.However,if there is any variability in the amount of cash or own equity instruments that will be delivered or received,then such a contract is a financial asset or liability as applicable. 合同不是仅仅因为它可能导致收到或交付其自身权益工具而被划分为权益工具。这类合同的分类 取决于交付的股权数量是否有变化，或收到的现金或金融资产的数量是否有变化。由公司收到或 交付固定数量的自身权益工具以换取固定数量的现金或其他金融资产而结算的合同，是一种权益 工具。这被称为“以固定换固定”。但是，如果将交付或收到的现金或自有权益工具的数量有任何 变化，则该合同应视为金融资产或负债。 For example,where a contract requires the entity to deliver as many of the entity's own equity instruments as are equal in value to a certain amount,the holder of the contract would be indifferent whether it received cash or shares to the value of that amount.Thus,this contract would be treated as debt. 例如，如果合同要求公司交付与某一金额等值的自身权益工具，合同持有方并不在乎是否收到与 该金额等值的现金或股票，则该合同将被视为债务(因为付钱的义务就是固定好了的且根据合同 约定进行履约即可)。 Other factors that may result in an instrument being classified as debt are: ·is redemption at the option of the instrument holder? ·is there a limited life to the instrument? · is redemption triggered by a future uncertain event that is beyond the control of both the holder and issuer of the instrument? ·are dividends non-discretionary? 可能导致工具被分类为债务的其他因素有： -持有方可以选择赎回吗? -金融工具的寿命有限吗? -赎回是否由持有方和发行方都无法控制的未来不确定事件引起? -股息是不可自由支配的吗? Similarly,other factors that may result in the instrument being classified as equity are whether the shares are non-redeemable,whether there is no liquidation date or where the dividends are discretionary. 同样，可能导致该工具被分类为权益的其他因素包括股票是否不可赎回、是否没有清算日期或股 息是否可自由支配。 The classification of the financial instrument as either a liability or as equity is based on the principle of substance over form. Two exceptions from this principle are certain puttable instruments meeting specific criteria and certain obligations arising on liquidation.Some instruments have been structured with the intention of achieving particular tax,accounting or regulatory outcomes,with the effect that their substance can be difficult to evaluate. 将金融工具分类为负债或权益是基于实质重于形式的原则。这一原则的两个例外是符合特定标准 的某些可出售工具和清算时产生的某些义务。一些工具的结构旨在实现特定的税收、会计或监管 结果，其实质内容则可能难以评估。 The entity must make the decision as to the classification of the instrument at the time that the instrument is initially recognised.The classification is not subsequently changed based on changed circumstances.For example,this means that a redeemable preference share,where the holder can request redemption,is accounted for as debt even though legally it may be a share of the issuer. 公司必须在初始确认工具时决定工具的分类。该分类随后不会根据情况的变化而改变。例如，比 如持有方可以要求赎回的可赎回优先股需要被视为债务，即使在法律上它可能是发行方的股票。 In determining whether a mandatorily redeemable preference share is a financial liability or an equity instrument,it is necessary to examine the particular contractual rights attached to the instrument's principal and return elements.The critical feature that distinguishes a liability from an equity instrument is the fact that the issuer does not have an unconditional right to avoid delivering cash or another financial asset to settle a contractual obligation. Such a contractual obligation could be established explicitly or indirectly.However,the obligation must be established through the terms and conditions of the financial instrument.Economic necessity does not result in a financial liability being classified as a liability.Similarly,a restriction on the ability of an entity to satisfy a contractual obligation,such as the company not having sufficient distributable profits or reserves,does not negate the entity's contractual obligation. 在确定强制可赎回优先股是金融负债还是权益工具时，有必要检查该工具本金和回报要素所附带 的特定合同权利。区分负债和权益工具的关键点是，发行方没有无条件的权利可以避免交付现金 或其他金融资产来履行合同义务。这种合同义务可以明确或间接确立。然而，该义务必须通过金 融工具的条款和条件来确立。经济必要性不会导致金融负债被归类为负债。同样，对公司履行合 同义务的能力有所限制，如该公司没有足够的可分配利润或资本公积，则并不否定其合同义务。 Rights issues can still be classified as equity when the price is denominated in a currency other than the entity's functional currency.The price of the right is denominated in currencies other than the issuer's functional currency,when the entity is listed in more than one jurisdiction or is required to do so by law or regulation.A fixed price in a non-functional currency would normally fail the fixed number of shares for a fixed amount of cash requirement in IAS 32 to be treated as an equity instrument.As a result,it is treated as an exception in IAS 32 and therefore treated as equity. 当价格以记账本位币以外的货币记账时，配股发行仍可归类为权益。当实体在多个司法管辖区上 市或被法律法规要求上市时，则权利价格以发行方记账本位币以外的货币计价。非记账本位币的 固定价格通常不符合IAS 32中作为权益工具对待的固定数量现金换取固定数量的股票。但在IAS 32中则被视为例外，并因此被视为权益。 Some instruments are structured to contain elements of both a liability and equity in a single instrument.Such instruments -for example,bonds that are convertible into a fixed number of equity shares and carry interest -are accounted for as separate liability and equity components.'Split accounting'is used to measure the liability and the equity components upon initial recognition of the instrument.This method allocates the fair value of the consideration for the compound instrument into its liability and equity components.The fair value of the consideration in respect of the liability component is measured at the fair value of a similar liability that does not have any associated equity conversion option.The equity component is assigned the residual amount. 一些工具的结构包含单一工具中的负债和权益要素。此类工具(例如，可转换为固定数量的 股票并附带利息的债券即可转债)被视为单独的负债和权益部分。“拆分会计法”用于在初始 确认金融工具时计量负债和权益部分。此方法将复合型金融工具对价的公允价值分配至其负 债和权益部分。负债部分对价的公允价值以没有任何相关权益转换期权的类似负债的公允价 值计量(未来现金流的折现值)。权益组成部分则是发行筹集到的资金扣减债的部分候，剩余 的部分。 Additional performance measures 替代业绩指标 What is an APM? An APM is a measure of financial performance not specifically defined by the applicable financial reporting framework. APM 替代业绩指标是相关财务报告框架没有具体定义的一种财务绩效衡量指标。 IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements permits entities to disclose additional information that will help financial statement users to better understand a company's performance and position.Many companies therefore disclose APMs because they provide additional useful information for users who are trying to understand their financial performance and financial health. IAS 1允许公司披露额外的信息，以便帮助财报使用者更好地了解公司财务业绩。 因此很多公司对外披露APMs, 旨在为报表使用者提供额外的有用信息，以便更 好做出决策。 Different types of business generally disclose different APMs.Some APMs commonly disclosed by companies in the supermarket sector include: 不同类型的企业通常披露不同的APMs。 超市行业公司通常披露的一些APM 包 括： Like-for-like sales: often defined as the year-on-year growth in sales from stores open for more than one year.This enables comparisons of growth between retailers that have different strategies with reqards to store expansion and closure. 同类销售：开业一年以上的商店销售额的同比增长(YoY growth)。这使得在扩张 和关闭方面有不同战略的零售商之间的增长情，更具可比性。 Underlying profit before tax: often defined as profit before tax after excluding items that might distort comparability between periods.This enables additional insight into year-on-year performance and is in line with how performance is measured internally. 税前基本利润：扣除了可能扭曲不同时期可比性的项目后的税前利润。这有助于 进一步了解同比绩效，并且符合内部绩效衡量方式。 Retail free cash flow: often defined as net cash generated from retail operations , less interest and tax paid,retail capital expenditure,and lease payments.This measures the ability of the retail business to generate cash. 零售自由现金流：零售业务产生的净现金流，减去已支付的利息和税费、零售资 本支出以及租赁付款。该指标主衡量了零售业务产生现金的能力。 Net debt: often defined as cash equivalents less overdrafts,borrowings,lease liabilities and derivatives.This enables assessment of liquidity and whether the company can cover debt commitments. 净债务：现金等价物减去银行透支、借款、租赁负债以及衍生工具。该指标有助 于评估流动性以及公司是否能够偿还债务。 ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUMGOLD ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM Benefits and criticisms of APMs 优缺点 APMs can be important in assisting users in making investment decisions,as they allow them to gain a better understanding of a company's financial statements and to evaluate it through the eyes of the management. They can also be an important instrument for easier comparison of companies in the same sector,market or economic area. 优点：APM 在帮助报表使用者做出投资决策方面起着重要的作用，不光能够帮 助使用者更好地理解公司的财务报表，并通过管理层的视角对其进行评估。也可 以是一个重要的工具，便于同一行业、市场或经济领域的公司间进行比较。 However,APMs appear to be used by some companies to present a confusing or optimistic picture of their performance by removing negative aspects. Sometimes,there is bias in how they are calculated-for instance,companies tend to exclude a range of different expense items when calculating 'underlying profit'but remove far fewer items of income.Other issues include inconsistency in the basis of calculation from year to year,inaccurate classification of items and a general lack of transparency. Often there is little information provided on how alternative profit figures have been calculated or how they reconcile with the profit reported in the financial statements. 缺点：有优点便会有缺点。比如APMs 会被一些公司通过消除负面因素的方式， 呈现出令人困惑或是乐观的业绩表现。有时侯，计算方式也会存在偏差——比如， 公司在计算“基本利润”时往往会排除一系列不同的费用项目，但删除的收入项目 要少得多(使得利润也相应增加)。其他问题包括每年的计算基础不一致、项目分 类不准确以及普遍缺乏透明度。公司通常都很少提供关于替代利润数字的计算方 式，或是它们是如何根据财报中的信息进行相应调整的。 Best practice Best practice 具体应用 There is demand for more guidance in this area.However,a balance is needed to provide companies with enough flexibility to report relevant information,while also ensuring financial statement users are able to judge the usefulness of the APMs. APM 具体实操方面其实需要更多的指导。同时既需要平衡相关信息报告的灵活 性，又需要确保财报使用者能够评判APMs 的有用性。 To this end,the European Securities and Markets Authority(ESMA)has launched guidelines that address the concept,description and presentation of APMs.The main requirements are: 为此，欧洲证券和市场管理局(ESMA) 发布了处理APMs 的一系列概念、描述和 表示的指南。主要要求是： · Issuers should define the APM used,the basis of calculation and give it a meaningful label and context; · APMs should be reconciled to the financial statements; · APMs that are presented outside financial statements should be displayed with less prominence; · An issuer should provide comparatives for APMs and the definition and calculation of the APM should be consistent over time;and ·If an APM ceases to be used,the issuer should explain its removal and the reasons for the newly defined APM. · 发布人应该针对使用的APM 及计算基础给出详细的定义解析 ● APMs 应与财务报表一致。 · 不是基于财务报表得出的APM 无需突出显示； · 发布人应提供APM 的比较数据，同时 APM 的定义和计算方式应长期一致。 ● 如果不再使用APM, 发行人应解释其删除以及重新定义APM 的原因。 However,these guidelines may not be practicable when the cost of providing this information outweighs the benefits that users obtain from it.Companies presenting APMs will most likely incur both implementation costs and ongoing costs.Although most of the information required by the guidelines is already collected for internal management purposes,it may not be in the format needed to satisfy the disclosure principles. 然而，当提供这种信息的成本超过使用者从中获得的利益时，则会削弱这些指导 的实用性。提供APMs 的公司很可能会同时产生相应的实施成本以及持续成本。 尽管大部分信息是为企业内部管理目收集的，但可能并不满足披露要求。 GOLDEN EDUCATION ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM ESMA believes that the costs will not be significant because APMs should generally not change over periods.Therefore,ongoing costs will relate almost exclusively to updating information for every reporting period.ESMA believes that the application of these guidelines will improve the understandability, relevance and comparability of APMs. 但 ESMA 认为费用不会很大，因为APMs 一般不会随着时间的推移而改变。因 此，持续费用基本上是与每个报告期间的资料更新有关。 ESMA 还认为，有了 guidelines 的指引，APMs 相关的可理解性、相关性和可比性都将得以提高。 EXAMPLE Below is an example of an APM disclosure: 以下是某企业APM 披露的一个示例： To provide shareholders with additional insight into the performance of the business,an adjusted measure of profit(underlying profit before tax)is provided to supplement the numbers that have been presented in accordance with IFRS Accounting Standards.This adjusted measure reflects how the business measures performance internally. 为了向股东提供关于企业业绩的额外见解，提供了一种调整后的利润计量方法 (税前基础利润),用于补充按照IFRS 计算出的金额。这个调整后的计量方法 反映了企业是如何衡量内部绩效的。 Underlying profit before tax excludes items recognised in profit or loss before tax which,if included,could distort comparability between periods. Determining which items are to be adjusted requires judgement,in which the Group considers items which are significant either by virtue of their size and/or nature,or that are non-recurring. 基本税前利润不包括税前损益中确认的项目，如果包括的话，可能会扭曲各期间 的可比性。确定哪些项目需要进行调整需要一定的判断，集团应考虑期中由于其 规模及/或性质而具有重要性的项目或非经常性(偶发性)项目 Underlying profit is not an IFRS Accounting Standards measure and is not directly comparable to other companies.基础利润不是IFRS 会计准则的衡量标 准，也不能与其他公司直接比较。 20X1$m 20X1 $m Group profit before tax 38.6 24.3 Non-underlying items: 5.1 3.2Redundancy and closure provisions 5.1 3.2 (1.9)高顿教育Net expense from
