As noted in this article,some users are wary of the motivation behind APM disclosures.In the reconciliation,underlying profit before tax is substantially higher than group profit before tax.Is this APM providing useful information,or presenting an over-optimistic view of the group's performance? 正如本文所指出的， 一些用户对 APM 披露背后的动机持谨慎态度。在对账中， 基本税前利润大大高于集团税前利润。这种APM 是否提供了有用的信息，或者 对集团的表现提出了过于乐观的看法? Environmental,social and ethical factors 教育 环境、社会和道德因素 EDUCATION ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM Companies are expanding the range of APMs disclosed in their financial reports.Increasingly,these focus on the impact of their business model on the environment and on society.Performance can be measured in many ways, and not all of these are financial. 公司正在扩大其财务报告中披露的APM 的范围。这些公司越来越关注其商业模 式对环境和社会的影响。绩效可以通过多种方式来衡量，但并非所有方式都是财 务上的。 For example,Iceland,a frozen food supermarket in the UK,reports its percentage food waste’(food waste as a proportion of total sales).Iceland also discusses its progress towards the reduction of food waste year-on-year, and the initiatives used to distribute surplus food to charitable causes. 例如，英国的一家冷冻食品超市 Iceland 报告了其“食物浪费”的百分比(食物浪费 占总销售额的比例)。Iceland 还讨论了逐年减少食物浪费的进展，以及将剩余食 物分配给慈善事业的举措。 There are many reasons why APMs such as these are of interest to stakeholders: 利益相关者对APMs 感兴趣有许多原因： · · · ● ● · reducing food waste will reduce a company's carbon footprint,and a company's success here may attract green customers and investors reducing waste willhelp keep selling prices low,which is important in times of high inflation,or when there is a ‘cost of living’crisis lower costs will improve profits and cash flow. 减少食物浪费将减少一家公司的碳足迹，而一家公司在这里的成功可能会吸 引绿色客户和投资者的关注 减少浪费将有助于保持低售价，这在高通胀时期或出现“生活成本”危机时很 重要 降低成本将改善利润和现金流。 EDUCATI ON ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM Conclusion Financial statement users are demanding different types of information to assess the performance,position and prospects of companies.APMs can help fill this information deficit,but only if they are calculated and presented in ways that are useful. 财报使用者需要不同类型的信息来评估公司的业绩、财务信息和发展前景。 APMs 可以帮助填补这一信息缺口，但前提是它们必须以有用的方式计算和呈现。 Giving investors what they need This article looks at how reporting capital structure is challenging,but markets are keen for the information. 本文着眼于报告资本结构是如何具有挑战性的，但市场却热衷于获取这些信息。 Introduction Often the advice to investors is to focus upon cash and cash flow when analysing corporate reports.However insufficient financial capital can cause liquidity problems and sufficiency of financial capital is essential for growth. Discussion of the management of financial capital is normally linked with entities that are subject to external capital requirements,but it is equally important to those entities which do not have regulatory obligations. 当分析公司报告时，给投资者的建议通常是关注在现金及现金流。然而，金融资 本不足会导致流动性问题，而充足的金融资本对增长至关重要。对金融资本管理 的讨论通常与受外部资本要求约束的公司有关，但对于那些没有监管义务的公司 也同样重要。 What is it? Financial capital is defined in various ways.The term has no accepted definition having been interpreted as equity held by shareholders or equity plus debt capital including finance leases.This can obviously affect the way in which 'capital'is measured which has an impact on return on capital employed (ROCE). 金融资本有多种定义。该术语尚且还没有公认的定义，通常解释为股东持有的股 权或股权加债务资本，包括融资租赁。这显然会影响衡量“资本”的方式，从而影 响资本回报率(ROCE) 。 An understanding of what an entity views as capital and its strategy for capital management is important to all companies and not just banks and insurance companies.Users have diverse views of what is important in their analysis of 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION capital.Some focus on historical invested capital,others on accounting capital and others on market capitalisation. 了解一个公司对于资本及其资本管理(资本运作)战略的看法对所有公司都很重 要，并不仅仅针对银行和保险公司。用户对于资本分析中什么是重要的持有不同 的看法。有些侧重于历史投入资本，有些侧重于会计资本，还有一些侧重于市值。 Investor needs Investors have specific but different needs for information about capital depending upon their approach to the valuation of a business.If the valuation approach is based upon a dividend model,then shortage of capital may have an impact upon future dividends.If ROCE is used for comparing the performance of entities,then investors need to know the nature and quantity of the historical capital employed in the business.There is diversity in practice as to what different companies see as capital and how it is managed. 投资者对资本信息有具体但不同的需求，这取决于他们对企业估值的方法。如果 估值方法基于股息模型，那么资本短缺可能会对未来股息产生影响。如果ROCE 用于比较绩效，那么投资者需要了解在该业务中使用的历史资本的性质和数量。 实践中不同公司对待资本的态度以及管理方式是存在多样性的。 There are various requirements for entities to disclose information about 'capital.In drafting IFRS®7,Financial Instruments:Disclosures,the International Accounting Standards Board(the Board)considered whether it should require disclosures about capital.In assessing the risk profile of an entity,the management and level of an entity's capital is an important consideration. 关于“资本”的信息披露有着多种要求。在起草IFRS 7的时候，国际会计准则 委员会考虑过是否应要求披露资本。在评估公司的风险状况时，资本的管理和其 水平是重要的考虑因素。 The Board believes that disclosures about capital are useful for all entities,but they are not intended to replace disclosures required by regulators as their reasons for disclosure may differ from those of the Board.As an entity's capital does not relate solely to financial instruments,the Board has included these disclosures in IASe 1,Presentation of Financial Statements rather than IFRS 7. IFRS 7 requires some specific disclosures about financial liabilities,it does not have similar requirements for equity instruments. IASB 认为，资本披露对所有企业都是有用的，但它们并不打算取代监管机构要 求的披露，因为它们披露的原因可能与董事会的不同。由于一个公司的资本并不 仅仅与金融工具相关，因此 IASB将这些披露纳入了IFRS 1而不是IFRS 7 。IFRS 7要求对金融负债进行一些具体的披露，但对权益工具没有类似的要求。 The Board considered whether the definition of 'capital'is different from the definition of equity in IAS 32,Financial Instruments:Presentation.In most cases disclosure of capital would be the same as equity but it might also include some elements of debt (for example,some forms of sub-ordinated debt which are ranked lower than other debt on liquidation)or exclude some elements of equity(for example,components arising from cash flow hedges which will be reclassified to profit or loss in a future period).The disclosure of capital is intended to give entities the ability to describe their view of the elements of capital if this is different from equity. IASB审议了“资本”的定义是否不同于IAS 32中权益的定义。在大多数情况下， 资本的披露与权益相同，但也可能包含一些债务要素(比如，c某些形式的次级债 务在清算时排名低于其他债务)或排除一些权益要素(比如，现金流套期产生的将 在未来期间重新分类为损益的部分)。资本的披露旨在使企业能够描述他们对资 本要素的看法。 IAS 1 Disclosures As a result,IAS 1 requires an entity to disclose information that enables users to evaluate the entity's objectives,policies and processes for managing capital. This objective is obtained by disclosing qualitative and quantitative data.The former should include narrative information such as what the company manages as capital,whether there are any external capital requirements and how those requirements are incorporated into the management of capital. 因此，IFRS 1 要求公司去披露信息，以使得使用者能够评估公司的目标，政策 和资本管理过程。这一目标是通过披露定性和定量的数据来实现的。前者应包括 叙述性信息，比如公司资本管理的内容，是否有任何外部资本要求，以及这些要 求该如何纳入资本管理。 Some entities regard some financial liabilities as part of capital whilst other entities regard capital as excluding some components of equity for example those arising from cash flow hedges.The Board decided not to require quantitative disclosure of externally imposed capital requirements but rather decided that there should be disclosure of whether the entity has complied with any external capital requirements and,if not,the consequences of non-compliance.Further there is no requirement to disclose the capital targets set by management and whether the entity has complied with those targets,or the consequences of any non-compliance. 一些公司将部分金融负债视为资本的一部分，而其他公司则将一些权益的组成部 分排除在外，比如现金流对冲产生的部分。IASB 对于披露信息的数量没有要求， 但是要求公司披露是否遵循了外部资本的要求，如果没有，则需要披露不遵守规 定的后果。此外，对于管理层设定的资本目标没有披露要求，包括公司是否达成 了这些目标，或未达成所需承担的后果，都没有披露要求。 Examples Examples of some of the disclosures made by entities include information as to how gearing is managed,bow capital is managed to sustain future product development and how ratios are used to evaluate the appropriateness of its capital structure.An entity bases these disclosures on the information provided internally to key management personnel. 公司披露的信息包括如何管理杠杆、如何管理资本以维持未来的产品开发，以及 如何使用比率来评估其资本结构的适当性。公司根据内部提供给关键管理人员的 信息来进行披露。 If the entity operates in several jurisdictions with different external capital requirements such that an aggregate disclosure of capital would not provide useful information,the entity may disclose separate information for each separate capital requirement. 如果企业在多个具有不同外部资本监管要求的区域运营，以至于资本的综合披露 无法提供有用的信息，那么企业则可以针对不同的资本需求进行单独的披露。 Besides the requirements of IAS 1,the IFRS Practice Statement 1: Management Commentary suggests that management should include forward-looking information in the commentary when it is aware of trends, uncertainties or other factors that could affect the entity's capital resources. 除了IAS 1的要求之外，管理层评价书建议当管理层在意识到趋势、不确定性或 其他可能影响公司资本资源的时侯，需要在建议书中包含这些前瞻性的信息。 Companies Act Additionally,some jurisdictions refer to capital disclosures as part of their legal requirements.In the UK,Section 414 of the Companies Act 2006 deals with the contents of the Strategic Report and requires a 'balanced and comprehensive analysis'of the development and performance of the business during the period and the position of the company at the end of the period. 此外，一些司法管辖区将资本披露作为其法律要求的一部分。在英国，《公司法 2006》第414 节涉及战略报告的内容，并要求对于该期间的业务发展和绩效以 及该年底公司的整体状况进行“平衡且全面的分析”。 The section further requires that to the extent necessary for an understanding of the development,performance or position of the business,the strategic report should include an analysis using key performance indicators.It makes sense that any analysis of a company's financial position should include consideration of how much capital it has and its sufficiency for the company's needs. Section 414还进一步要求，为了理解业务的发展、绩效或地位，战略报告应包 括使用关键绩效指标的分析。任何对公司财务状况的分析都应该考虑它有多少资 本以及它是否足以满足公司的需求，这样才合理。 The Financial Reporting Council Guidance on the Strategic Report suggests that comments should appear in the report on the entity's financing arrangements such as changes in net debt or the financing of long-term liabilities. 财务报告理事会关于战略报告的指导意见建议，报告中应出现关于公司对于融资 安排的意见，比如净债务的变化或长期负债的融资。 Capitalisation table In addition to the annual report,an investor may find details of the entity's capital structure where the entity is involved in a transaction,such as a sale of bonds or equities. 除了年报之外，投资者还可以在公司参与的交易(如出售债券或股票)中找到其资 本结构的详细信息。 It is normal for an entity to produce a capitalisation table in a prospectus showing the effects of the transactions on the capital structure.The table shows the ownership and debt interests in the entity but may show potential funding sources,and the effect of any public offerings. 对于企业来说，在招股说明书中编制资本化表，来显示交易对资本结构的影响是 很普遍的。该表显示了企业中的所有权和债务权益，但也可能显示潜在的资金来 源以及任何公开发行的影响。 The capitalisation table may present the pro forma impact of events that will occur as a result of an offering suchas the automatic conversion of preferred stock,the issuance of common stock,or the use of the offering proceeds for the repayment of debt or other purposes.The Board does not require such a table to be disclosed but it is often required by securities regulators. 资本化表可能会显示由于发行而发生的事件的预计影响，如优先股的自动转换、 普通股的发行或使用发行所得偿还债务或其他目的。国际会计准则委员会并不要 求披露此类表格，但证券监管机构经常要求披露。 It can be seen that information regarding an entity's capital structure is spread across several documents including the management commentary,the notes to financial statements,interim accounts and any document required by securities regulators. 可以看出，有关企业资本结构的信息分散在几个文件中，包括管理层意见书、财 务报表附注、中期账务和证券监管机构要求的任何文件。 Debt and equity Essentially there are two classes of capital reported in financial statements, namely debt and equity.However,debt and equity instruments can have different levels of benefits and risks. 从本质上讲，财务报表中报告的资本有两类，即债务和权益。然而，债务和权益 工具可以有不同程度的收益和风险。 When an entity issues a financial instrument,it has to determine its classification either as debt or as equity.The result of the classification can have a significant effect on the entity's reported results and financial position. Liability classification impacts upon an entity's gearing ratios and results in any payments being treated as interest and charged to earnings.Equity classification may be seen as diluting existing equity interests. 当一个公司发行金融工具时，它必须确定其分类为债务还是权益。分类结果会对 公司的报告结果以及财务状况产生重大影响。负债分类会影响杠杆比率，并会导 致利息费用增加进而影响 earnings。权益分类则可能视同会稀释现有股权。 IAS 32 sets out the nature_of the classification process but the standard is principle based and sometimes the outcomes are surprising to users.IAS 32 does not look to the legal form of an instrument but focuses on the contractual obligations of the instrument . IAS 32规定了分类过程的性质，但该标准是基于准则来的的，有时结果会令人 感到惊讶。IAS 32并不关注所发行金融工具的法律形式，它更强调的是所发行 的金融工具是否具有无法避免的合同义务。 IAS 32 considers the substance of the financial instrument,applying the definitions to the instrument's contractual rights and obligations.The variety of instruments issued by entities makes this classification difficult with the application of the principles occasionally resulting in instruments that seem like equity being accounted for as liabilities.Recent developments in the types of financial instruments issued have added more complexity to capital structures with the resultant difficulties in interpretation and understanding. IAS 32考虑的是金融工具的实质，将定义应用于工具的合同权利和义务。公司 发行的各种工具会使这种分类变得困难，应用这些准则有时会导致看似权益的工 具被记为负债。近期对于发行的金融工具类型的发展，增加了资本结构的复杂性， 造成了解释和理解上的困难。 Diversity and difficulty There is a diversity of thinking about capital,which is not surprising given the issues with defining equity,the difficulty in locating sources of information about capital and the diversity of business models in an economy. 人们对资本的看法各不相同，这并不奇怪，因为权益的定义、寻找资本信息来源 的难度以及一个经济体中商业模式的多样性都存在问题。 Capital needs are very specific to the business and are influenced by many factors such as debt covenants,and preservation of debt ratings.The variety and inconsistency of capital disclosures does not help the decision-making process of investors.Therefore the details underlying a company's capital structure are essential to the assessment of any potential change in an entity's financial flexibility and value. 资本需求对企业来说是非常具体的，并受到多方因素的影响，如债务契约和债务 评级的保持。资本披露的多样性和不一致性对于投资者的决策过程没有帮助。因 此，公司资本结构的详细信息对于评估其财务灵活性和价值的任何潜在变化至关 重要 。 The integrated report framework In 2013,the International Integrated Reporting Council(IIRC)released a framework for integrated reporting.This followed a three-month global consultation and trials in 25 countries. 2013年，在为期三个月的全球咨询和25个国家的试验之后，国际综合报告理事 会(IIRC)发布了综合报告框架。 The framework establishes principles and concepts that govern the overall content of an integrated report.An integrated report sets out how the organisation's strategy,governance,performance and prospects,which lead to the creation of value.There is no benchmarking for the above matters and the report is aimed primarily at the private sector but it could be adapted for public sector and not-for-profit organisations. 该框架确立了综合报告整体的原则和概念。一份综合报告阐述了组织的战略、治 理、绩效和前景以及如何通过这些来创造价值。上述事项并没有基准，报告主要 针对私营企业，但也适用于公共部门以及非营利组织。 GOLDEN EDUCATION ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM The primary purpose of an integrated report is to explain to providers of financial capital how an organisation creates value over time.An integrated report benefits all stakeholders interested in a company's ability to create value, including employees,customers,suppliers, business partners,local communities,legislators,regulators and policymakers,although it is not directly aimed at all stakeholders.Providers of financial capital can have a significant effect on the capital allocation and attempting to aim the report at all stakeholders would be an impossible task and would reduce the focus and increase the length of the report.This would be contrary to the objectives of the report,which is value creation. 综合报告的主要目的是向金融资本的提供者解释一个组织是如何随着时间的推 移创造价值的。综合报告有益于所有对公司创造价值能力感兴趣的利益相关方， 包括员工、客户、供应商、商业伙伴、当地社团、立法者、监管者和决策者，尽 管它并不直接针对所有利益相关方。金融资本的提供者可以对资本配置产生重大 影响，同时试图将报告涉及所有利益相关方是一项不可能完成的任务，将会减少 重点，还会增加报告的篇幅。这将违背报告的目标，也就是创造价值。 Historical financial statements are essential in corporate reporting,particularly for compliance purposes,but do not provide meaningful information regarding business value.Users need a more forward-looking focus without the necessity of companies providing their own forecasts and projections. Companies have recognised the benefits of showing a fuller picture of company value and a more holistic view of the organisation. 历史财务报表在公司报告中至关重要，特别是出于合规目的，但无法提供有关业 务价值的有意义的信息。用户需要更具前瞻性的关注点，而无需公司提供自己的 预测和计划。公司已经认识到展示更全面的公司价值和组织观所带来的好处。 The International Integrated Reporting Framework will encourage the preparation of a report that shows their performance against strategy,explains the various capitals used and affected,and gives a longer-term view of the organisation.The integrated report is creating the next generation of the annual report as it enables stakeholders to make a more informed assessment of the organisation and its prospects. 国际综合报告框架将鼓励编制一份报告，显示其在战略方面的表现，解释所使用 和受影响的各类资本，并提供对组织的长期看法。综合报告正在创造下一代的年 度报告，因为它使利益相关方能够对组织及其前景在获得更多信息的情况下，做 DEN EDUCATION 出评估。 Principle-based framework The IIRC has set out a principle-based framework rather than specifying a detailed disclosure and measurement standard.This enables each company to set out its own report rather than adopting a checklist approach.The culture change should enable companies to communicate their value creation better than the often boilerplate disclosures under International Financial Reporting Standards(IFRS®).The report acts as a platform to explain what creates the underlying value in the business and how management protects this value. This gives the report more business relevance rather than the compliance led approach currently used. IIRC 规定了一个基于原则的框架，而不是详细的披露和衡量标准。这使得每家 公司能够提出自己的报告，而不是采用清单的方法。文化的改变应该使公司能够 更好地交流他们的价值创造，而不是按照IFRS 的标准要求披露.该报告作为一个 平台，解释是什么创造了企业的潜在价值，以及管理层该如何保护这一价值。这 使报告更具业务相关性，而不是目前使用的以合规为导向的方法。 Integrated reporting will not replace other forms of reporting but the vision is that preparers will pull together relevant information already produced to explain the key drivers of their business's value. Information will only be included in the report where it is material to the stakeholder's assessment of the business.There were concerns that the term 'materiality'had a certain legal connotation,with the result that some entities may feel that they should include regulatory information in the integrated report.However,the IIRC concluded that the term should continue to be used in this context as it is well understood. 综合报告不会取代其他类型的报告，但其愿景是，编制者将汇集已经产生的相关 信息，以解释其业务价值的关键驱动因素。只有当信息对利益相关方的业务评估 具有重要意义时，才会将其纳入报告中。有人担心“重要性”一词有一定的法律含 义，因此一些企业可能认为他们应该在综合报告中包括监管信息。然而，IIRC 的结论是，在这种情况下应该继续使用这一术语，因为它是众所周知的。 The integrated report aims to provide an insight into the company's resources and relationships that are known as the capitals and how the company interacts with the external environment and the capitals to create value.These capitals can be financial,manufactured,intellectual,human,social and relationship,and natural capital,but companies need not adopt these classifications.The purpose of this framework is to establish principles and content that governs the report,and to explain the fundamental concepts that underpin them.The report should be concise,reliable and complete,including all material matters,both positive and negative in a balanced way and without material error. 综合报告旨在提供对公司资源和关系(也就是所谓的资本)的深入了解，以及公司 如何与外部环境和资本互动以便创造价值。这些资本可以是金融资本、制造资本、 智力资本、人力资本、社会和关系资本以及自然资本，但是公司不需要采用这些 分类。这一框架的目的是确立指导报告的原则和内容，并解释背后的基本概念。 报告应该简明、可靠和完整，包括所有重要事项，正面的和负面的内容，都应以 相对平衡的方式展现，并且没有重大错误。 Integrated reporting is built around the following key components: 综合报告围绕以下关键内容： 1.Organisational overview and the external environment under which it operates 2.Governance structure and how this supports its ability to create value 3.Business model 4.Risks and opportunities and how they are dealing with them and how they affect the company's ability to create value 5.Strategy and resource allocation 6.Performance and achievement of strategic objectives for the period and outcomes 7.Outlook and challenges facing the company and their implications 8.The basis of presentation needs to be determined,including what matters are to be included in the integrated report and how the elements are quantified or evaluated.

8. 需要确定列报的基础，包括综合报告应包括哪些事项，以及如何量化或评估 这些要素。 The framework does not require discrete sections to be compiled in the report but there should be a high level review to ensure that all relevant aspects are included.The linkage across the above content can create a key storyline and can determine the major elements of the report such that the information relevant to each company would be different. 该框架不要求在报告中编制独立章节，但需要高层审查，以确保所有相关方面都 被包含了。上述内容之间的联系可以创建起一个关键的故事情节，并确定报告的 主要元素，以便与每个公司的相关信息都将有所不同。 Relationship with stakeholders An integrated report should provide insight into the nature and quality of the organisation's relationships with its key stakeholders, including how and to what extent the organisation understands,takes into account and responds to their needs and interests.Further,the report should be consistent over time to enable comparison with other entities. 综合报告应深入了解组织与其主要利益相关方关系的性质和质量，包括组织如何 以及在多大程度上理解、考虑和回应他们的需求和兴趣。此外，该报告应长期保 持一致，以便能够与其他企业进行比较。 South African organisations have been acknowledged as among the leaders in this area of corporate reporting with many listed companies and large state-owned companies having issued integrated reports.An integrated report may be prepared in response to existing compliance requirements -for example,a management commentary.Where that report is also prepared according to the framework,or even beyond the framework,it can be considered an integrated report. An integrated reportcmay be either a standalone report or be included as a distinguishable part of another report or communication.For example,it can be included in the company's financial statements. 南非组织被认为是公司报告领域的领导者之一，许多上市公司和大型国有公司都 发布了综合报告。可以根据现有的合规要求准备一份综合报告，例如，管理层评 价书。如果报告也是根据框架编写的，甚至超出了框架，则可视为一份综合报告。 综合报告可以是独立的报告，也可以作为另一份报告或通信的可明确区分的部分。 比如可以包含在公司财务报表中。 The IIRC considered the nature of value and value creation.These terms can include the total of all the capitals,the benefit captured by the company,the market value or cash flows of the organisation and the successful achievement of the company's objectives.However,the conclusion reached was that the framework should not define value from any one particular perspective because value depends upon the individual company's own perspective. It can be shown through movement of capital and can be defined as value created for the company or for others.An integrated report should not attempt to quantify value as assessments of value are left to those using the report. IIRC 考虑了价值和价值创造的本质。这些术语可以包括所有资本的总和、公司 获得的利益、组织的市场价值或现金流以及公司目标的成功实现。然而，得出的 结论是，该框架不应该从任何一个特定的角度来定义价值，因为价值取决于个别 公司自己的角度。它可以通过资本的流动来呈现，可以被定义为给公司或他人创 造的价值。综合报告不应试图量化价值，因为价值的评估最终要留给报告的使用 者 。 Many respondents felt that there should be a requirement for a statement from those 'charged with governance'acknowledging their responsibility for the integrated report in order to ensure the reliability and credibility of the integrated report.Additionally,it would increase the accountability for the content of the report. 许多受访者认为，应该要求哪些“负责治理”的人发表声明，承认他们对综合报告 的责任，以确保综合报告的可靠性和可信度。此外，还将加强对报告内容的问责。 The IIRC feels the inclusion of such a statement may result in additional liability concerns,such as inconsistency with regulatory requirements in certain jurisdictions,and could lead to a higher level of legal liability.The IIRC also felt that the above issues might result in a slower take-up of the report and decided that those 'charged with governance’should,in time,be required to acknowledge their responsibility for the integrated report while,at the same time,recognising that reports in which they were not involved would lack credibility. IIRC 认为，包含这样的声明可能会导致额外的责任问题，比如与某些司法管辖 区的监管要求不一致，并可能导致更高的法律责任。IIRC 还认为，上述问题可 能会导致对报告的接受速度较慢，并决定应及时要求“负责治理”的人员承认他们 对综合报告的责任，同时认识到没有他们参与的报告将缺乏可信度。 There has been discussion about whether the framework constitutes suitable criteria for report preparation and for assurance.The questions asked concerned measurement standards to be used for the information reported and how a preparer can ascertain the completeness of the report. 对于该框架是否构成报告编写和保证的适当标准进行了讨论。所提问题涉及所报 告信息使用的衡量标准，以及编制人员如何确定报告的完整性。 There were concerns over the ability to assess future disclosures,and recommendations were made that specific criteria should be used for measurement,the range of outcomes and the need for any confidence intervals be disclosed.The preparation of an integrated report requires judgment but there is a requirement for the report to describe its basis of preparation and presentation,including the significant frameworks and methods used to quantify or evaluate material matters.Also included is the disclosure of a summary of how the company determined the materiality limits and a description of the reporting boundaries. 一些人对评估未来披露的能力表示担忧，并建议应使用具体标准进行衡量，结果 范围以及任何置信区间的需求需要被披露。编制综合报告需要主管判断，但报告 需要描述其编制和列报的基础，包括用于量化或评估重大事项的重要框架和方法。 还包括披露公司如何确定重要性限制的摘要以及报告边界的描述。 The IIRC has stated that the prescription of specific KPls and measurement methods is beyond the scope of a principles-based framework. The framework contains information on the principle-based approach and indicates that there is a need to include quantitative indicators whenever practicable and possible. Additionally, consistency of measurement methods across different reports is of paramount importance.There is outline guidance on the selection of suitable quantitative indicators. IIRC 指出，具体的 KPI 和衡量方法的规定已经超出了基于原则的框架范围。该 框包含了关于以原则为基础的方法的信息，并表明有必要在可行和可能的情况下 将量化指标包含进去。此外，不同报告中测量方法的一致性至关重要。针对选择 合适的量化指标的指标，有相关的纲要指导。 A company should consider how to describe the disclosures without causing a significant loss of competitive advantage.The entity will consider what advantage a competitor could actually gain from information in the integrated report,and will balance this against the need for disclosure. 公司应该考虑如何在不造成重大竞争优势损失的情况下描述披露的信息。公司将 考虑竞争对手实际上可以从综合报告中的信息中获得什么好处，并需要与披露需 求之间做出平衡。 Companies struggle to communicate value through traditional reporting.The framework can prove an effective tool for businesses looking to shift their reporting focus from annual financial performance to long-term shareholder value creation.The framework will be attractive to companies who wish to develop their narrative reporting around the business model to explain how the business has been developed. 公司很难通过传统的报告来传达价值。对于希望将报告重点从年度财务业绩转移 到长期股东价值创造的企业来说，该框架被是一个有效的工具。该框架将对那些 希望围绕商业模式制定叙述性报告以解释业务是如何发展的公司具有吸引力。 Using the business model of a company to help analyse its performance Understanding the business model of an entity is helpful in analysing and communicating the essence of a business and for predicting the implications of a change in circumstance on a business.Strategic Business Reporting (SBR) candidates should use this technique to improve their answers to SBR questions and this article should help them to do this. 了解一家企业的商业模式对于分析以及沟通企业本质是有帮助的，同时还有助于 预测环境变化对于企业的影响。 The business model in the context of Integrated Reporting is a company's system of 'transforming inputs, through its business activities, into outputs and outcomes that aim to fulfil the organization's strategic purposes and create value over the short,medium and long term’.The definition may seem quite theoretical but there are a number of entities who use this definition to disclose the resources (also known as capital)that the organisation draws on as inputs to its business activities,and how these are converted to outputs(products, services,by-products,and waste),which then have a further impact on the various capitals and stakeholders. The business model describes the entity's activities,asset configuration,and the way in which the business adds value including the generation of its cash flows and its customers,products and services.The aim is to provide users with insight on the ability of the business to adapt to changes,for example,in the availability,quality,and affordability of inputs,and how these changes can affect the organisation's longer-term viability. 综合报告背景下的商业模式算得上是一个公司的系统，“通过公司的业务活动， 将投入转化为旨在实现组织战略目标，并能在中长短期内创造价值的产出及成 果”。定义可能看起来很理论化，但其实有许多企业使用此定义来披露为商业活 动所投入的资源(也称为资本),以及这些资源如何转化为产出(产品、服务、 副产品和废物),从而对各种资本和利益相关者产生进一步的影响。商业模式描 述了企业的活动、资产配置以及业务增值的方式，包括其现金流及其客户、产品 和服务的产生。其目的是为了让用户了解企业适应变化的能力，例如投入的可用 性、质量和可负担性，以及这些变化如何影响组织的长期生存能力。 Business model information can be fundamental to investor analysis.It provides context and understanding to the other information in the annual report.The provision of business model information can demonstrate an entity's understanding of its business and key drivers which can create investor trust and reduce risk.Additionally,investors need to understand the business,how the business has performed,and how this performance has been affected by various factors.There is an argument that the information regarding the business model should be presented outside the financial statements,such as in the management commentary. 商业模式信息可能是投资者分析的基础。它为公司年报中的其他信息提供了相关 的背景和理解。商业模式信息的提供可以证明企业对其业务和关键驱动因素的理 解，这其实可以建立投资者的信任并降低风险。此外，投资者需要了解公司业务， 业务表现得怎么样，以及相关业绩是如何受到各种因素影响的。有一种观点认为， 有关商业模式的信息应该在财务报表之外呈现，比如在管理层评价书中。 Investors willneed to know how the business model is responding to market trends and how the strategy supports the key components of the business model.They will wish to know how management considers the risks and opportunities across the business model and how money is made and value generated and re-distributed.Investors will look at the Key Performance Indicators(KPls)and how they reflect the key components of the business model.Having the business model play a role in financial reporting presumes that investors have a good understanding of it prior to assessing the entity's financial position and performance. 投资者需要了解商业模式是如何应对市场趋势的，以及公司战略是如何支持商业 模式中的关键组成部分。他们希望了解管理层如何考虑整个商业模式中的风险和 机遇，以及如何赚钱、产生价值并重新分配价值。投资者将关注关键绩效指标 (KPI), 以及它们是如何反映商业模式中的关键组成部分。假设投资者在评估 企业财务状况和业绩之前对商业模式有很好的了解，是可以让商业模式在财务报 告中发挥作用的。 There has been discussion as to whether the business model should be considered in standard setting and in particular whether the term should be defined in the Conceptual Framework for Financial Reporting (the Conceptual Framework).The International Accounting Standards Board (the Board)has decided not to define the business model in the Conceptual Framework even though there was reference to the business model in an early Exposure Draft. 人们一直在讨论是否应该在制定准则时考虑商业模式，特别是是否应该在财务报 告的概念框架性框架中界定这一术语。IASB 决定不这么做，尽管在 DE 早期征 求意见稿中有提到商业模式。 However, the term has been implicit in International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRSR)for a while. For example, International Accounting Standard(IASR)2 Inventories generally requires inventories to be measured at the lower of cost and net realisable value.However,IAS 2 includes an exception that allows commodity traders to measure their inventories at fair value less cost of sale with changes in fair value less cost to sell recognised in profit or loss.The justification for this different treatment is that the inventory of commodity traders is principally acquired with the purpose of selling in the near future and generating a profit from the fluctuation in prices. 但是，其实该术语在国际财务报告准则(IFRS)中已经隐含了一段时间了。比如， IAS 2存货一般要求存货是以成本和可变现净值中的较低者计量。然而，IAS 2 包含一个例外，允许商品交易商以公允价值减去销售成本来衡量其库存，并且增 减变化确认进损益表中。这么处理的理由是，商品贸易商的库存主要是为了在不 久的将来出售并从价格波动中获利。 IFRS 9 requires classification and measurement of financial assets based on an entity's business model.Although IFRS 9 does not contain a definition of the term ‘business model’,it does include some implicit assumptions about its meaning.IFRS 9 views the business as based upon how the entity's assets and liabilities are managed. IFRS9 要求根据企业的商业模式对金融资产进行分类和计量。虽然IFRS 9并 没 有包含“商业模式”的定义，但它确实包含了一些关于其含义的隐含假设。IFRS 9 中商业模式主要看的就是企业是如何管理其持有的金融资产和负债的，也就是看 持有目的。 It states that an entity should classify financial assets as subsequently measured at either amortised cost or fair value on the basis of both: (a)The entity's business model for managing the financial assets and (b)The contractual cash flow characteristics of the financial asset. 准则规定，企业应根据以下两个条件将金融资产分类为以摊余成本法或公允价值 进行后续计量： (a)管理金融资产的商业模式(持有目的) (b)金融资产的契约现金流特征。 To qualify for an amortised cost classification,the financial asset must be held to collect contractual cash flows rather than be held with a view to selling the asset to realise a profit or loss.For example,trade receivables held by a manufacturing entity are likely to fall within the 'hold to collect'business model if the trade receivables do not contain a significant financing component in accordance with IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers. 要符合摊余成本法分类，持有金融资产必须是以收集契约现金流为目的，而不是 为了出售以实现利润或损失。例如，根据IFRS 15,如果应收账款不包含重大融 资成分，则生产制造型企业持有的应收账款可能属于“持有以收取”(持有到期) 业务模式。 A debt instrument is classified as subsequently measured at fair value through other comprehensive income(FVOCl)under IFRS 9 if it meets the 'hold to collect'and the 'sell'business model test.The asset is held within a business model whose objective is achieved by both holding the financial asset in order to collect contractual cash flows and selling the financial asset.This business model typically involves greater frequency and volume of sales than the hold to collect business model.Integral to this business model is an intention to sell the instrument before the investment matures. 根据IFRS9, 如果债务工具符合“持有以收取”(持有到期)和“出售”业务模式测 试的话，则该债务工具应被分类为按照FVTOC I(公允价值增加变动计入OCI) 进行后续计量。该资产的商业模式目的是通过持有金融资产以收集契约现金流以 及出售金融资产来实现的。这种商业模式通常比持有到期等待收款的商业模式拥 有更高的销售频率和销售量。这种商业模式的一部分是在资产到期之前将其出售 掉。 Fair value through profit or loss(FVTPL)is the residual category in IFRS 9.If the business model is to hold the financial asset for trading,then it is classified and measured at FVTPL.Some stakeholders have suggested that the requirements for equity investments in IFRS 9 could discourage long-term investment.Their view is that the default requirement to measure those investments at fair value with value changes recognised in profit or loss may not reflect the business model of long-term investors. 损益公允价值(FVTPL )是IFR S9 中的剩余类别。如果业务模式是持有金融资产 进行交易，则按FVTPL 进行分类和计量。一些利益相关者认为，IFRS 9对股权 投资的要求可能会阻碍长期投资。他们认为，以公允价值衡量这些投资并在损益 中确认价值变化的默认要求可能无法反映长期投资者的业务模式。 The term 'business model'has also been used in other standards. IFRS 8 Operating Segments defines an operating segment as a 'component of an entity that engages in business activities from which it can earn revenue and incur expenses'.An entity with more than one business model is likely to also have different segments.If an entity has a business model,it would have internal reporting information which measure the performance of the business model which may in fact be the business segments. 商业模式术语在其他准则中也被使用过。IFRS 8 将经营分部定义为“从事经营 活动并从中赚取收入和产生费用的企业的组成部分”。拥有一个以上商业模式的 企业也可能拥有不同的细分市场。如果一个企业有一个商业模式，它应该是要有 内部报告信息来衡量其绩效，且该商业模式事实上可能就是个业务部门。 IAS 40 Investment property distinguishes a property that is held by entities for investment purposes from the one that is intended to be occupied by the owner. An investment property differs from an owner-occupied property because the investment property generates cash flows largely independently of the other assets.IAS 40 sets out the two main uses of property(owner occupied and investment)which implicitly correspond to different business models.An owner-occupied property should be measured at depreciated cost less any impairment loss,which is an appropriate way of reflecting the use of the property.Whereas,investment property is measured at either fair value with fair value changes recognised in the statement of profit or loss,or on the same cost basis as for an owner-occupied property.These different accounting treatments reflect the different uses of these assets. IAS 40投资性房地产，区分了企业是出于投资目的持有房地产还是打算自用。 投资性房地产不同于企业自用的房地产，因为投资性房地产产生的现金流很大程 度上是独立于其他资产的。IAS 40规定了资产的两种主要用途(企业自用和投资), 这两种用途隐含地对应着不同的商业模式。企业自用的财产应以折旧成本减去任 何减值损失来计量，此方法能够恰当地反映出资产的使用方式。投资性房地产既 可以以公允价值计量，同时公允价值变动在损益表中确认 (FV model),也可 以以与企业自用房地产相同的成本(Cost model)为基础。这些不同的会计处理 反映了这些资产的不同用途。 If the business model continues to play a significant role in standard-setting,it could give insight into how the entity's business activities are managed and help users assess the resources and liabilities of the entity.Alternatively,it can be argued that this approach may reduce comparability as it could result in different classification,measurement or disclosure of the same transaction. This may introduce bias in the way the financial statements of an entity are reported,and therefore make comparisons between entities difficult.It could encourage less neutral reporting as preparers may present the most favourable outcome which creates a conflict with faithful representation.This approach can make financial statements of entities with similar business models and in the same industry,more comparable.Thus,the difficulties with the definition of the business model and its consistent application should not constitute a reason for excluding it because it has relevance in terms of the financial decision-making needs of the users of the accounting information. 如果商业模式继续在标准设置中发挥重要作用的话，那么它可以深入了解企业的 业务活动是如何被管理的，并帮助用户评估企业的资源和负债。同样地，也有人 认为，这种方法可能会降低可比性，因为它可能导致对同一交易产生不同的分类、 计量或披露。这可能会导致企业的财务报表报告方式产生偏差/偏见，从而使企 业之间的比较变得困难。它可以鼓励并不中立的报告，因为编制者可能按照不同 的计量方式来呈现最有利的结果，从而造成与公允陈述相冲突。这种方法可以使 具有相似商业模式和在同一行业中的实企业的财务报表更具可比性。因此，商业 模式的定义及其应用一致方面的困难不应构成排除它的理由，因为它与会计信息 使用者的财务决策需要有关。 It has always been the case that different businesses will account for the same asset in different ways depending on what its role is within the firm's business model.A change in the entity's business model is a significant event,because it implies a change in how assets and liabilities are used in the cash flow generation process,that is when and how gains and losses are recognised. 一直以来，不同的企业都会以不同的方式来计量相同的资产，这取决于它在公司 的商业模式中所扮演的角色。企业商业模式的变化是一个重大事件，因为这意味 着资产和负债在现金流生成过程中的使用方式发生了变化，即损益的确认时间和 方式也会随之受影响。 So,although the 'business model'concept first appeared in IFRS 9,there had been an implicit use of the concept in IFRS for a long time.The business model is not discussed in the latest Conceptual Framework and,as a result,it may be argued that there is no consistency of its use in IFRS.The United Kingdom's decision to leave the European Union highlights the unpredictability and disruptive nature of the business environment.However,it also illustrates the need for business models to be resilient and flexible to what is happening inside and outside an organisation and to help stakeholders better understand how a company will adapt to significant change.SBR candidates should be aware of these issues and be able to provide examples of these inconsistencies in an exam context. 因此，尽管“商业模式”概念首次出现在IFRS 9中，但长期以来，国际会计准则 一直在含蓄地使用这一概念。最新的概念性框架中没有讨论该商业模式，因此， 可以认为其在 IFRS 中的使用并不一致。英国退出欧盟的决定凸显了商业环境的 不可预测性和破坏性。但同时，它也说明了商业模式需要对组织内外发生的事情 具有弹性和灵活性，并帮助利益相关者更好地了解公司将如何适应重大变化。 SBR 考生应该意识到这些问题，并能够在考试中提供这些不一致的例子。(主 要结合准则及概念性框架进行分析) The definition and disclosure of capital 资本的定义和披露 This article is useful to those candidates studying for Strategic Business Reporting.It is structured in two parts:first,it considers what might be included as the capital of a company and,second,why this distinction is important for the analysis of financial information. 文章由两部分组成：首先，它考虑了什么可以作为公司的资本，其次，为什么这种区分对于财务 信息的分析很重要。 Essentially,there are two classes of capital reported in financial statements: debt and equity. However,debt and equity instruments can have different levels of right,benefit and risks. When an entity issues a financial instrument,it has to determine its classification either as debt or as equity.The result of the classification can have a significant effect on the entity's reported results and financial position.Liability classification impacts upon an entity's gearing ratios and results in any payments being treated as interest and charged to earnings.Equity classification may be seen as diluting existing equity interests. 从本质上讲，财务报表中报告了两类资本：债务和权益。但是债务和权益类工具有着不同程度的 权利、收益和风险。当一个公司发行金融工具时，它必须确定其分类是债务还是权益。分类结果 会对公司的财报产生重大影响。负债分类会影响其负债比率，并会相应产生利息费用影响收益。 权益分类则可能会稀释现有股权。 IASe 32,Financial Instruments:Presentation sets out the nature of the classification process but the standard is principle-based and sometimes the outcomes that result from its application are surprising to users.IAS 32 does not look to the legal form of an instrument but focuses on the contractual obligations of the instrument.IAS 32 considers the substance of the financial instrument,applying the definitions to the instrument's contractual rights and obligations. IAS 32准则列出了分类的性质，但准则毕竟是principle-based,也就是基于原则的，所以某些 时候得出的结果会令报表使用者感到惊讶。IAS 32并不关注金融工具的法律形式，而是更注重 契约的实质以及约定的义务。IAS 32考虑金融工具的实质，将定义应用于工具的契约权利和义 务 。 More complexity The variety of instruments issued by entities makes this classification difficult with the application of the principles occasionally resulting in instruments that seem like equity being accounted for as liabilities.Recent developments in the types of financial instruments issued have added more complexity to capital structures with the resultant difficulties in interpretation and understanding.Consequently,the classification of capital is subjective which has implications for the analysis of financial statements. 公司发行的金融工具多种多样，这使得分类很困难，应用这些准则有时会导致看似权益的工具被 记为负债。近期发行的金融工具增加了资本结构的复杂性，导致解释和理解上的困难。因此，资 本的分类其实是主观的，对财务报表的分析也是有影响的。 To avoid this subjectivity,investors are often advised to focus upon cash and cash flow when analysing corporate reports.However,insufficient financial capital can cause liquidity problems and sufficiency of financial capital is essential for growth.Discussion of the management of financial capital is normally linked with entities that are subject to external capital requirements,but it is equally important to those entities that do not have regulatory obligations. 为了避免这种主观性，通常建议投资者在分析公司报告时可以关注现金和现金流。然而，金融资 本不足会导致流动性问题，而充足的金融资本对于公司增长至关重要。对金融资本管理的讨论通 常与受外部资本要求约束的公司有关，但对那些没有监管义务的公司同样重要。 Financial capital is defined in various ways but has no widely accepted definition having been interpreted as equity held by shareholders or equity plus debt capital including finance leases. This can obviously affect the way in which capital is measured,which has an impact on return on capital employed(ROCE).An understanding of what an entity views as capital and its strategy for capital management is important to all companies and not just banks and insurance companies.Users have diverse views of what is important in their analysis of capital. Some focus on historical invested capital,others on accounting capital and others on market capitalisation. 金融资本有多种定义，但但并没有被广泛接受的定义，通常被解释为股东持有的股权或股权加债 务资本，包括融资租赁。这显然会影响资本的计量方式，进而影响资本回报率(ROCE) 。 理解 一个公司对资本的看法及其资本管理策略对所有公司都很重要，并不仅仅是银行和保险公司。用 户对资本分析中的重要内容各自有不同的看法。一些比较关注历史投资资本，另一些比较关注会 计资本，还有一些会关注市值。 Investors have specific but different needs for information about capital depending upon their approach to the valuation of a business. If the valuation approach is based upon a dividend model,then shortage of capital may have an impact upon future dividends.If ROCE is used for comparing the performance of entities,then investors need to know the nature and quantity of the historical capital employed in the business.There is diversity in practice as to what different companies see as capital and how it is managed. 投资者对资本信息有特定但不同的需求，这取决于他们对企业估值所使用的方法。如果估值方法 是基于股息模型，那么资本短缺可能会对未来股息产生影响。如果ROCE 用于比较公司业绩， 那么投资者需要了解企业中使用的历史资本的性质和数量。在实践中，不同公司对资本的定义和 管理方式存在差异。 There are various requirements for entities to disclose information about 'capital'.In drafting IFRSe 7,Financial Instruments:Disclosures,the International Accounting Standards Board (the Board)considered whether it should require disclosures about capital.In assessing the risk profile of an entity,the management and level of an entity's capital is an important consideration.The Board believes that disclosures about capital are useful for all entities,but they are not intended to replace disclosures required by regulators as their reasons for disclosure may differ from those of the Board.As an entity's capital does not relate solely to financial instruments,the Board has included these disclosures in IAS 1,Presentation of Financial Statements rather than IFRS 7.IFRS 7 requires some specific disclosures about financial liabilities;it does not have similar requirements for equity instruments. 对有关“资本”的信息披露有着各种要求。在起草 IFRS 7的时候，国际会计准则委员会考虑了是 否应该要求公司披露资本。在评估公司的风险状况时，其资本的管理及水平是一个重要的考虑因 素 。IASB 认为，资本披露对所有公司都是有用的，但它们并不打算取代监管机构要求的披露， 因为它们披露的原因可能与董事会不同。由于一个公司的资本并不仅仅与金融工具有关，IASB 已将这些披露纳入 IAS 1 而不是 IFRS7 。IF RS 7要求对金融负债进行一定的披露；不过对于权 益工具害没有类似的要求。 The Board considered whether the definition of capital is different from the definition of equity in IAS 32.In most cases,capital would be the same as equity but it might also include or exclude some other elements.The disclosure of capital is intended to give entities the ability to describe their view of the elements of capital if this is different from equity. IASB 审议了资本的定义是否与IAS 32权益的定义有所不同。在大多数情况下，资本与权益相同， 但也可能包括或不包括其他一些要素。资本披露旨在使公司能够描述其对资本要素的看法(如果 与权益不同的话)。 As a result,IAS 1 requires an entity to disclose information that enables users to evaluate the entity's objectives,policies and processes for managing capital. This objective is obtained by disclosing qualitative and quantitative data.The former should include narrative information such as what the company manages as capital,whether there are any external capital requirements and how those requirements are incorporated into the management of capital. Some entities regard some financial liabilities as part of capital,while other entities regard capital as excluding some components of equity -for example,those arising from cash flow hedges. 因此，IAS 1要求公司披露信息，使报表使用者能够评估公司管理资本的目标、政策和流程。这 一目标是通过披露定性和定量数据实现的。前者应包括叙述性的信息，比如公司资本管理的内容、 是否有任何外部资本要求以及这些要求如何纳入资本管理。一些公司将一些金融负债视为资本的 一部分，而其他公司则将资本视为不包括权益的组成部分-比如，现金流套期产生的部分。 The Board decided not to require quantitative disclosure of externally imposed capital requirements but rather decided that there should be disclosure of whether the entity has complied with any external capital requirements and,if not,the consequences of non-compliance.Further,there is no requirement to disclose the capital targets set by management and whether the entity has complied with those targets,or the consequences of any non-compliance. IASB 决定不要求对外部强加进来的资本要求进行量化披露，而是决定去披露公司是否遵守了任 何外部资本的要求，如果没有遵守，则需要披露不遵守的后果。此外，也没有要求公司要披露管 理层设定的资本目标以及是否符合这些目标，或任何不遵守规定的后果。 Examples of some of the disclosures made by entities include information as to how gearing is managed,how capital is managed to sustain future product development and how ratios are used to evaluate the appropriateness of its capital structure.An entity bases these disclosures on the information provided internally to key management personnel.If the entity operates in several jurisdictions with different external capital requirements,such that an aggregate disclosure of capital would not provide useful information,the entity may disclose separate information for each separate capital requirement. 公司披露的一些信息需要包括：如何管理杠杆率、如何管理资本以维持未来产品开发以及如何使 用比率来评估其资本结构的适当性等信息。一个公司要根据内部提供给关键管理层的信息，进行 披露。如果公司在具有不同外部资本监管的几个司法管辖区运营，此时资本的综合披露则不会提 供有用的信息，而应该针对每个单独的资本要求披露相关的信息。 Trends Besides the requirements of IAS 1,the IFRS Practice Statement Management Commentary suggests that management should include forward-looking information in the commentary when it is aware of trends,uncertainties or other factors that could affect the entity's capital resources.Additionally,some jurisdictions refer to capital disclosures as part of their legal requirements. 除了IAS 1要求之外，IFRS 实践声明管理评论，当管理层意识到未来可能的趋势、不确定性或 其他可能会影响企业资本资源的因素时，应在管理层意见书中包括前瞻性信息。此外，一些司法 管辖区也将资本披露作为其法律要求的一部分。 In the UK,Section 414 of the Companies Act 2006 deals with the contents of the Strategic Report and requires a 'balanced and comprehensive analysis’of the development and performance of the business during the period and the position of the company at the end of the period.The section further requires that to the extent necessary for an understanding of the development,performance or position of the business,the strategic report should include an analysis using key performance indicators. It makes sense that any analysis of a company's financial position should include consideration of how much capital it has and its sufficiency for the company's needs.The Financial Reporting Council Guidance on the Strategic Report suggests that comments should appear in the report on the entity's financing arrangements such as changes in net debt or the financing of long-term liabilities. 在英国，《2006年公司法》第414 条涉及战略报告的内容，并要求对期间内的业务发展和业绩 以及期间末时的公司状况进行“平衡且全面的分析”。条例还要求，在理解业务的发展、业绩或地 位所必需的范围内，战略报告应包括使用关键绩效指标的分析。任何对公司财务状况的分析都应 该考虑它有多少资本以及它是否足以满足公司的需求。财务报告理事会在关于战略报告的指导意 见中建议，报告中应出现关于该公司的融资安排的意见，如净债务的变化或长期负债的融资。 In addition to the annual report,an investor may find details of the entity's capital structure where the entity is involved in a transaction,such as a sale of bonds or equities.It is normal for an entity to produce a capitalisation table in a prospectus showing the effects of the transactions on the capital structure.The table shows the ownership and debt interests in the entity but may show potential funding sources and the effect of any public offerings.The capitalisation table may present the pro forma impact of events that willoccur as a result of an offering such as the automatic conversion of preferred stock,the issuance of common stock, or the use of the offering proceeds for the repayment of debt or other purposes. 除了年报之外，投资者还可以找到公司参与交易的资本结构的细节，比如出售债券或是股票。公 司在招股说明书中编制资本化表，显示相关交易对于资本结构的影响是正常的。该表显示了公司 的所有权和债务权益，但可能也显示了潜在的资金来源和任何公开募股的影响。资本化表可能显 示因发行而产生的影响，比如优先股的自动转换、普通股的发行，或将发行收益用于偿还债务或 其他目的。 The Board does not require such a table to be disclosed but it is often required by securities regulators.For example,in the USA,the table is used to calculate key operational metrics. America Corporation announced in February 2016 that it had 'made significant advancements in its ongoing initiative toward improving its capitalization table,capitalization,and operational structure'. IASB 不要求披露此类表格，但证券监管机构则经常会要求披露此类表格。例如，在美国，该表 就被用于计算关键运营指标上。美国公司于2016年2月宣布，它“在改善其资本化表、资本化 和运营结构的持续举措中取得了重大进展”。 It can be seen that information regarding an entity's capital structure is spread across several documents including the management commentary,the notes to financial statements,interim accounts and any document required by securities regulators. 可以看出，有关一个公司资本结构的信息分布在几多份文件中，包括管理层意见书、财务报表附 注、中期账目以及证券监管机构要求的任何文件。 The Board has undertaken a research project with the aim of improving the accounting for financial instruments that have characteristics of both liabilities and equity.This is likely to be a major challenge in determining the best way to report the effects of recent innovations in capital structure. IASB 进行了一个研究项目，目的是改进对同时具有负债和权益特点的金融工具的会计核算。在 确定资本结构创新影响的最佳报告方式方面，这可能是一个重大挑战。 There is a diversity of thinking about capital that is not surprising given the issues with defining equity,the difficulty in locating sources of information about capital and the diversity of business models in an economy.Capital needs are very specific to the business and are influenced by many factors,such as debt covenants and preservation of debt ratings.The variety and inconsistency of capital disclosures does not help the decision making process of investors. 考虑到股权的定义问题、资本信息来源的定位困难以及经济中商业模式的多样性，人们对资本的 思考多样性就并不奇怪了。资本需求是企业特有的，受多方因素影响的，如债务契约和债务评级 的保护。资本披露的多样性和不一致性对于投资者的决策过程并没有很大帮助。 Therefore,the details underlying a company's capital structure are essential to the assessment of any potential change in an entity's financial flexibility and value.An appreciation of these issues and their significance is important to candidates studying for Strategic Business Reporting. 因此，公司资本结构的细节，对于评估其财务灵活性和公司价值的潜在变化都至关重要。
